user_comment: I am so impressed!! I bought 6 pairs in various colors to liven up my workout wardrobe for spring. These pants are amazing. I spent a lot of time reading reviews before I bought, and I can’t believe others had issues. They are amazing. The sit below my belly button, which I like. They hit just below my ankle. The material is thin and stretchy... perfect for warmer weather. I’m 5’4, 125 pounds. I have wider hips and a flat stomach, so I went with the Medium. Fits exactly as I wanted them to. I will absolutely be purchasing a few more pairs!

SAVED THE DAY I volunteered to work 4 days at a PGA event at an LA course and although I wear a lot of black pants, the long ones were all starting to get worn. I went into a last minute panic and searched online for stock at local stores, which proved fruitless. Amazon to the rescue. These arrived in 2 days with my Prime and fit perfectly. I wore for 2 days, washed and wore for 2 days again, and now they are my daily go-to casual pants. (Signaling the direction of a tee shot in the photo. You never see the hundreds of volunteers behind the scenes on TV.). PS: I am 5’7” and the length of the black was perfect with trainers.

My New Favorite Pants to Never Leave the House Perfect fit! These pants are great. They fit like high quality leggings. I wanted something that wasn't just 100% skinny fit so that's why I went with these cause the have the flared leg. I'm like 5'3(ish lol) about 150 so I got the medium. They're a little long on me but I recognize I'm vertically challenged when it comes to pants haha. But they fit well, snug on the body, but not in a constricting way, like a good t shirt (but thicker fabric, so no weird underwear lines). I'm a fan

It’s comfortable, looks and feels good even though it’s 100% polyester, and it falls right below my ankles so I didn’t have to get them hemmed. Cute and lightweight Cute pants able to be dressed down or up. I used for some desert weather and it didn’t feel heavy or suffocating. Even with the activity and sand it only snagged a bit but no tears which is amazing considering it’s not activewear. Being short 5’2 the length didn’t need adjusting at all. It was form fitting near my hips (I am wider there) but everywhere else was loose fitting. I rinsed it and air dried since traveling and it held up ok.it wasn’t sheer at all

RUNS BIG! I’m 5’4 & weigh 125 lbs. These pants are super cute & comfortable. They run big & have tons of stretch & they also have a drawstring & 2 nice sized deep front pockets. They look like Cordaroy if look up close but they don’t feel like it., they are really soft & on the thinner side but they do provide some warmth & they also have breathability. The quality is excellent! I tried to shrink them & washed them in hot water & then dried them on high heat but they did not shrink at all. The legs are really long so I compensated some of the length by folding the waistband over & it helps them fit better. I do recommend

My absolute fav pants!! Plz read if you get the shrink issue, I found a solution!! The shrinkage is real, BUT THEIRS A SOLUTION. love these pants. I own 4, and live in them!! When I first got them I fell in love, their super flowy, comfy and soft. When I washed them they shrunk tremendously and the material was super rough. I was very disappointed but luckily I don’t like wrinkles and I use a steamer instead of an iron and THAT DID IT. Ladies you need a steamer to bring these pants back to life, to their normal self. I don’t dry them, I wash, air dry them, and then steam them. I get compliments every time I am wearing them. They are a little long on me but I wear platform shoes most of the time so that helps me not drag them all over the floor. So buy them you won’t be disappointed. I am 5ft, 27in waist, 39 in hips, and wear a size M.

I’m 5’0 129lbs and purchased the small. I love them. Perfect lightweight material. Lots of stretch in the waist, not so much in the thighs. The length is fine for me … I will probably wear platform sandals with them. I saw reviews knocking these because people shrank them…. But just read the fabric care instructions. They are linen/cotton OF COURSE they shrink if you put them in the dryer! Wash cold, hang dry and you’ll be fine.

Very light and comfortable on a warm day, and with pockets These linen pants are light and comfortable on a warm day, and I receive compliments when I wear them. I'm 5'6", about 160 pounds, right between L and XL according to the size chart. I went with XL and they fit great after laundering--just the right length for me, but my legs are short for my height. I machine washed cold gentle cycle and hung them to dry, no problems. I love the pockets, although the pants can sag a bit if I have something heavy in a pocket and haven't tied the drawstring tight enough. The grey color works with many different tops and is not see-through.

Will be getting these in more colors!!! I am short and thick so I kind of expected these to be tight on me. I was a little disappointed at first because I wanted them to fit me like the model but let’s be real these look soooo good!! They are a little on the shear side but they’re white so that is expected. Wear nude or no undies and you’ll be good!! I am 5’1 and with a little heel these were just the right length for me. The material is very soft and comfy. They have a little stretch to them which is a plus for us fluffier girls. The back of the waste band is elastic which also helps them fit better in my opinion. They did come with a ribbon like belt but I took it off because it was too short for my waste so it looked funny tied. I love these so much!

Too short and loose I am 5’7 130lbs and I ordered a small. They are too short and a little loose in waist (I think xs would fit better). I like the shape of the pants, but they just don’t work for me. The white material is very see through but I was expecting that.

Love them! Wore these wine tasting in Italy from very early in the morning to very late and night and they were so comfortable all day. I am 5’7 so as you can see they sit more at my ankle. I am also roughly 135lbs and wore the XS. The small fit as well but I preferred the fit a little more snug with the XS. They also washed up beautifully and I hung them to dry and they look great. I gave 4 stars instead of 5 bc there were loose strings on the pants when I received them that needed to be trimmed off in quite a few places. Otherwise great pair of pants!

Could not love them more I’m 5’6”; 126lbs (flattish stomach, smallish booty but meaty thighs). Bought medium and love the fit. Could have assuredly fit in a small but wanted a more relaxed look and the medium is perfect without looking enormous. Length is also great with flats and hits right at the top of my foot without dragging the floor. With the partial elastic waist it’s easy to pull up or down depending your height. Liked them so much I bought in white, apricot and navy. The white is not a super bright white. When I tried on with a crisp white top you can tell. That wasn’t a big deal to me but fyi if you’re looking for a bright white. Also the white is a bit see through but I’ll just wear nude underwear. Love these pants for summer - Kinda beachy.
xl white pants on 5'11"

The fabric is also polyester and didn’t seem breathable to me. I will be sending these back!

No Back Pockets As Advertised, Buy a Size Up Very nice pants, quality, and cut but there’s no back pockets as shown on the advertised photos and buyer’s review photos. Bought a medium (my typical size) and also a large as suggested by Amazon. I’m 5’9”, 135lb. and the large fits perfect, it’s even long enough for my height which is not usual for an inexpensive priced pants. The medium is too short and tight at the waist. Buy a size up and don’t expect back pockets.

Not great for curvy women and came with stains I’m 5’8”, 200 pounds, and bought an XL. XL usually fits me (I fit into size 12 to 14 typically), especially when the pant cut is supposed to be loose, but these bunch up weirdly at the waist, aren’t particularly flattering around the hip area, and are see through enough to see my underwear. I do like how soft and breathable the material is, but the pair I got has red bleeding through it on the bottom. They seem to look really nice on other girls, but if you have a similar shape to mine or want pants that don’t come with stains on it, I don’t recommend these pants.

Perfect Summer Pants! Buying 3 Pairs Love these pants! Perfect fit. I usually wear a size 26 in pants and the XS in white fits perfect. I also got the S in apricot for a baggier fit to wear around the house/long walks. I'm getting the grey too! They are just the perfect fit, lightweight summer fabric and so comfy. I'm 5'7" and don't have to get them hemmed. Knocking down a star because after you wash them, a bunch of tiny threads unravel and you can tell that the seams on the belt loops aren't finished. It's a great design, but a cheap execution. I trimmed the extra threads, but it's still annoying and for this reason I won't wear them to work or more formal settings.

SHEIN linen pants Material is thin, quality of the stitch is poor quality thread is coming out of every seam, the zipper is very cheap and the button is also very cheap. The length is very long for me (5ft4in) the cut is actually nice. I will be returning these. I bought a similar pair for cheaper off SHEIN and they ripped after 2 washes on delicate and laying flat to dry, which I can foresee these doing the same thing unfortunately. The SHEIN ones were as see through as these either. Also in the picture you can see a mark. It’s not a mark that’s just a different color of thread embedded in the fabric which is had a couple of those throughout.

Lightweight Summer Pants These pull on, elastic waist pants are cute and made from a breathable 95% Rayon, 5% Spandex blend. This gives them a touch of stretch for added comfort. The waistband is a total of 2 3/4”, with a little frill at the top. So the elastic is about 2”, with 3/4” of bunched frill at the top for a cute, feminine look. There is a sewn in tie at the waist as well, and you can pull it and tie in the front or tie in the back for a different look. These pants have front slit pockets and fall at about the top of my foot. I am 5’ 2” and 127 lbs for reference, so they would be more of a capri style on some wearers. Cute Summer pants!

Look and feel great These pants are a great-quality stretchy material that feels good. As you can see in the close-up picture, it’s got a diagonal pattern more similar to jeans rather than the completely smooth knit I’ve seen in other professional yoga pants I own. But it’s still fine enough that the texture isn’t all that noticeable. I like the shape of these pants overall, BUT they tend to sag after just the first few minutes of wearing them, so I have to hike them up a little every so often. (Maybe if I had gotten a smaller size that would be less of an issue, but they aren't loose anywhere, so I was hesitant to size down.) -I’m 5’5”, and the 31” inseam falls below my ankle but doesn’t drag the floor. -It’s fairly high-waisted (comes to half an inch under my belly button). -I’m 142lbs, and the Large is a pretty good fit for me. Definitely would stretch for a few more pounds, and a medium might have actually worked better, but I’m fairly happy with the fit. Overall, a nice pair of work pants!

Not ‘short’ length, Not ‘skinny’ cut on slimmer legs I bought a size Small, Short in Black. 𝗙𝗮𝗯𝗿𝗶𝗰: I didn’t particularly like the feel of the fabric, but the fabric is quite thick and suuper stretchy. Owing to their thickness, they might not be the best choice for warm work environments; but, probably offer some decent coverage for your everyday Normal Human lines, lumps, & bumps, as well as not being see-through. I say “probably” because I only tried them on briefly and (unfortunately) knew right away I’d be returning them. 𝗪𝗮𝗶𝘀𝘁: These size small pants have a waistline that rests without stretching on an ~25” waist. I’m sure they could comfortably accommodate waistlines up to at least 27”. side note: the area below the waistband is cut wider, so they fit just a bit awkwardly in that area. 𝗟𝗲𝗻𝗴𝘁𝗵: I ordered a “short” size, as petite size inseams are usually for heights down to ~5’4”, and short inseams usually accommodate ~5’3” and shorter (I’m about 5’1”). The size charts say the inseam should be 27”. These pants are labeled ‘29” inseam’ (printed into the inner waistband). Even 27” wouldn’t give me the cropped look in the product photos, but 29” is unwearable without alterations. 𝗖𝘂𝘁: I’m pretty lean with plenty of muscle. My legs measure roughly 13 1/4” around the calf and 18” around the upper leg, at about halfway between groin and knee. Unlike the model in the product photos, my legs were swimming in these pants. I measured the width of this product’s legs. The 1/2 width is about 7 5/16” across. For comparison, I also measured the leg width of a pair of my skinny jeans at right about the same spot. The 1/2 width of the skinny jeans is approx. 5 7/8” across. So, if you’re rocking some gorgeously thick legs, these might be your perfect skinny pants. If you’re sporting slimmer stems, definitely look elsewhere for that ‘skinny’ fit. 𝗜𝗺𝗽𝗼𝗿𝘁𝗮𝗻𝘁 𝗻𝗼𝘁𝗲: The back pockets are fake; the front pockets are real and quite generous compared to many pockets marketed to women. 𝘁𝗹;𝗱𝗿— I’m giving the pants ⭐️⭐️⭐️✰✰ + super fast shipping + waistline measurement accuracy + fabric thickness + front pockets I can put my whole hand into - lower waist cut/tailored a bit awkwardly - length inaccuracy compared to size charts - leg width/cut description & images vs reality - fabric texture - fake back pockets

Fit could be a tad better. These are a great soft and elastic material that are form fitting without being overly tight. That's a plus. However at the same time, the cut seems just a tad off, giving me some tightness in the seams of the interior inseams, giving a look that needs to be covered up with something longer. At the waist, the material hugs just the bit of flub I have at my sides and kinda bulges out. So... needless to say, I'll be wearing cardigans with these pants and not tucking in any cute tank tops. The material is soft and they do look flattering at the legs. I am 5'7", and I do wish they were a bit longer, but I knew the inseams wouldn't be the length I need to reach my ankles. All in all, decent dress pants that are pretty comfortable.

Nice for work These are a decent pair of black dress stretch pants. I feel that these will go well with some blouses and shirts that I have for work. I will need a belt with these as there isn't any definition in the waistline of these and I feel that they may tend to slide down during the day without a belt. These fit as expected. They formed well to my body without making me feel self-conscious. I am 5'2" and these did touch the floor in the back when I was standing barefoot. If I wear these with a platform shoe or heel they will do nicely and not touch the ground. I feel that these would look nice on a variety of body types. I am rather curvy, but I think this would look nice on larger women and women who are on the thinner or less curvy side.

Beautiful quality pants. They fit exactly as described :-) I'm always a little wary of ordering pants online because the sizing can be way off. However, these fit exactly as I expected based on the product description and sizing chart. I'm 5'4" and weigh 125lb so I typically wear an XS or size 2 in pants. However, the sizing chart on Amazon indicated a waist and hip measurements in an XS that made me think they would be too small. So, I ordered the small and they fit me perfectly. I have short legs so I knew that a 29" inseam would be too long and that I would need to hem them. However, because of the excellent product description, I was not disappointed when the product arrived and it was exactly how I expected it to be. The actual pants themselves are beautifully soft and comfortable. The material is great quality and they are extremely well made. They have quite a bit of stretch to them and so are not uncomfortable when you sit down. They have faux pockets in the back which are cute and there are real pockets in the front. I don't actually like that you can see the outline of the pocket at the front so I'm going to cut the pockets off and stitch up the pocket opening to turn them into faux pockets like at the back. These are really lovely pants and I highly recommend them!

Flowy and comfortable pants First, and most importantly, they fit me really well around the waist. These are the first pants I've owned since having a baby that I can say that about, but these are able to somehow hold onto my belly and not fall down or dig in. I think maybe it's the stretchy material and wide the ruched waistband. I'm 5'10" so finding pants long enough for me is a challenge. These are good because they are cropped and I don't think there's a particular length they have to reach like regular pants. That said, these are longer than I expected. They have not made me feel like I'm too tall to wear them. Second, and almost as important as fitting well, they are comfortable. Like mega comfortable, because they are super soft and lightweight and stretchy. If you are looking for structured pants, look elsewhere. If you want pants that you can get down in the floor to play with your kids, wear all day painlessly, and not bother to change into pajamas at bedtime because they are just that comfortable, these are for you. Third, how they look. I personally don't know what to do with these style wise. I don't own any short or cropped shirts (I'm tall haha). I think they would look cute if the waistband was more visible, but I can't style it that way without updating my wardrobe. So for the looks of the pants, I've knocked off a star simply because I don't know what to wear with them that would be a cute outfit. Fourth, the functionality of these pants surprised me. They went through the wash well with the settings I use for all my clothes (cold/cold wash, extra low dry for 1 hour, spin without heat for a while to cool). The fit didn't seem to change after washing (I have only washed once so far). The pockets are generous in size, my (fairly large) phone fits all the way inside with plenty of extra room at the top to keep it from falling out when I sit. I can even do things like sit on the floor and not only does the phone stay securely in my pocket the whole time, even when getting up, it is comfortable and I don't feel the phone jabbing my hip or anything when I move or sit. Also, the pants stay out on my waist even when adding the weight of the phone to the pocket. Over time, the waistband may get slightly crooked from the uneven load, but it doesn't make the pants fall down or hang cattywompus. I haven't found any lounge pants or even jeans that perform so well at comfortably and securely holding my phone in a pocket. Usually they do well at one (if that!) but not both. I am a SAHM and these pants work really well for my lifestyle. They are very casual, so I don't know if I feel comfortable styling them for wearing on outings. I've primarily used them at home. If you like this wide leg flowy style, for sure give them a try.

I was SUPER skeptical of these jeans but they are really great. I like them A LOT. I gave them 4 stars only because A) the material is such that if you have cellulite or rolls of any kind, it will probably show (the material is a little thinner) and B) because if they offered an extra small option I would probably need it but it’s not available. For reference I’m 5’5” weigh 108 lbs and my dimensions are 32-24.5-35.5. I wear a small or extra small in most clothing. I often have a hard time with pants because despite not being super tall all of my height is in my legs. So the typical 28-29 inch inseam provided by most woman’s pants is just too short. (I look for 32 inseams whenever I can find them!) But these fit great! I’d prefer they be a little bit snugger around my butt and upper thighs but all things considered these pants are a win for sure. I would say pay close attention to the photo provided by the manufacturer of the pants listing the dimensions. They seem to be pretty accurate. Seriously, give these pants a shot. Especially if you’re someone who has a hard time finding them long enough. They aren’t the longest pants I own BUT they are longer than average for sure. And they are very stretchy. I can see now why people reverence being able to fit into smaller sizes quite comfortably. I’m going to buy a few more pair in different colors :) UPDATE: Love LOVE these pants!! I got them in more colors and they all fit great. They seem to wash well too. So far the colors haven’t bled at all (though I have washed them with like colors) and they have not shrunk. I have not but them in the dryer though. I did see that some of the sizing is inconsistent but nothing significant. Also, the white pants are a bit see through so be prepared to purchase nude undies for when you wear them. Otherwise I am so super happy with this pants and I can’t stress enough how hard it is to find them that fit right (especially online). TRY THESE!!! You won’t regret it.

I can see now why people reverence being able to fit into smaller sizes quite comfortably. I’m going to buy a few more pair in different colors :) UPDATE: Love LOVE these pants!! I got them in more colors and they all fit great. They seem to wash well too. So far the colors haven’t bled at all (though I have washed them with like colors) and they have not shrunk. I have not but them in the dryer though. I did see that some of the sizing is inconsistent but nothing significant. Also, the white pants are a bit see through so be prepared to purchase nude undies for when you wear them. Otherwise I am so super happy with this pants and I can’t stress enough how hard it is to find them that fit right (especially online). TRY THESE!!! You won’t regret it.

I was SUPER skeptical of these jeans but they are really great. I like them A LOT. I gave them 4 stars only because A) the material is such that if you have cellulite or rolls of any kind, it will probably show (the material is a little thinner) and B) because if they offered an extra small option I would probably need it but it’s not available. For reference I’m 5’5” weigh 108 lbs and my dimensions are 32-24.5-35.5. I wear a small or extra small in most clothing. I often have a hard time with pants because despite not being super tall all of my height is in my legs. So the typical 28-29 inch inseam provided by most woman’s pants is just too short. (I look for 32 inseams whenever I can find them!) But these fit great! I’d prefer they be a little bit snugger around my butt and upper thighs but all things considered these pants are a win for sure. Love this! I got this in a S, M, and L to see which one fit the best! So I got this in a small, medium and large to see which size would fit me best. I am 5'5 for height and 115-120lbs(I haven't weighed myself recently to know my weight exactly). Waist is 29inches, hips is 34 inches. The size small was soo small! Size medium seemed too small because the zipper seemed too far out but because these pants are soo stretchy, it does zip up without feeling like the button is going to rip off. The size large for me was too loose feeling for my legs. The butt, it just didn't hug my body right. The size medium was the best choice for me. And let me tell you, it is soooo comfortable! My current new favorite pants for work especially! But... these are really nice pants for wearing while I am off of work too!I love that it is stretchy. I love that I can choose the length of these pants. I ordered the medium long. I love that there is a zipper too! Zipper plus stretchy and not thin quality is just super excellent!! Get this and find the right size for you!Edit: I accidentally put this in the dryer once! Oh no!!!! But actually, I still wear this for work. I am soo surprised that I can still wear it. The zipper part on the top portion of the pants remain still stretchy. My legs fit fine as well. The only difference that I could tell is the length of the pants. It did get a lil short after being in the dryer. Not high waters short, just a lil short. Still wearable for work for me. Since this product is stretchy, it does stretch back out some. I do not recommend putting these pants in the dryer though- it smelled a lil burnt.. hang dry is best.

Great Quality I needed these last minute for a themed dance and my daughter is between sizes as she is too tall for girls sizes without them being too large in other areas and too short for a lot of women’s and junior sizes. Her normal size is 00 or 0, she is 5’1” and 80 pounds. These are like really soft stretchy jeans, she loves the way they feel and although they are a little loose in some areas, they fit perfectly in the waist with room to grow and the length is perfect. I did wash and dry them before wearing and they did not shrink at all, nor did the color fade. Very nice pants, I would recommend!

Sizing is way off, Quality and color is great I was so bummed, I was hoping to find my new favorite jeggings in multiple colors :-( Material, quality of workmanship and color is really good. I always wear leggings and jeggings in a U.S. large (That has been my go to size for years). I'm 5'3.5 in tall and 157lbs. I have been wearing time and Tru jeggings forever. When I took these out of the bag I was shocked at how small the waist was. I haven't worn anything that looked anywhere near that size for over 25 years! I tried them anyway since I heard they were super stretchy and couldn't do them up, massive muffin top! Unlike my Time and Tru's which are very comfy. They also are not high waisted at all. I've attached pics of my standard Time and Tru jeggings and these. There is a 5 inch difference! (There was no way I was going to post a pic with my belly hanging out). I might re-order in a couple of sizes up, because everything apart from the sizing was great. If you have a very small waist for a large (vs. a belly like me), a large 'might' work for you, but I'd still consider sizing up by at least 1 size.

Seriously, my New FAVORITE pair of jeans I am 5'3 and about 110 lbs, I purchased a size Medium: my legs are long and I guess for a short person my thighs and calves are bigger than the normal 5'3 person.... I'm just un proportionate... But these jeans are long enough to not look sloppy, look super cute with booties and not short enough to look like flood water pants (like the rest of my jeans). They don't ( or at lest not yet) get loose around the knees, and they have pockets! FRONT AND BACK POCKETS! which is nice b/c then my bootie doesn't look as flat as it is, and a front pocket is nice to have. I love these jeans in the fact that they are causal enough to wear to work/church and casual enough to wear to my daughters soccer practice without looking like I'm wearing leggings. I live in these jeans! Usually when i find a pair of pants, they will fit my waist and be too short or too long, or they fit my legs, but not my waist, or they are long enough and fit my waist but are too tight on my thighs... (un proportionate) These jeans are super stretchy and so comfortable that when I get home I am not dying to get out of them. They hug and fit in all the right places. I only gave 4 stars b/c the zipper doesn't like to stay up. Other than that, I want every color, I purchased black, wine and charcoal and cant decided which color is my favorite.

Amazing jeans but a little short I have been looking for colored jeans for work that are comfortable and stretchy. I'm currently in ED recovery so I'm slowly gaining weight and my sizes are all over. I LOVE that these are similar to real jeans instead of legging material but have a lot of stretch to them like leggings do. The color is great as pictured! The only downside is that they are short in length. I am 5'3, 240ish pounds and ordered a 1x. They stop right above my ankle which is a lot shorter than I normally pick but I rolled them into capris and I'm sure they will look great with fall boots! Another downside is that they are thin. I have a tattoo on my butt that if I am not wearing covering underwear can be seen a little through the material. But that's not enough to keep me from loving them! Definitely buying more colors!

Pretty great, maybe size up I really like these pants and man are they stretchy. I’m 5’9’ and about 150 lbs. (I’m usually an 8/10 in pants) I originally bought a large, but since I’m pear-shaped (W: 27.5”, H: 42.5”) they were tighter in the legs than I was wanting, although the waist fit perfectly. So I ordered an x-large and that was overall a better fit. I have to wear a belt to avoid slippage in the waist (story of my life) but the legs are not quite as a snug. (Except the calves…they are still really tight. Maybe I have big calves?) So I guess my advise would be to order a size or two up if you don’t want your pants to feel/look painted on. They seem pretty good quality. The material is a bit strange, but the color is a beautiful maroon. I’m pretty sure I’ll order another color sometime soon.

Finally found some pants for ME! Ever since I had my second baby, none of my pants fit. NONE. So I have been living in leggings and yoga pants. I was 145lbs (size 8) before and now I am 170 (size 10), so my hips got wider. I really needed Long pants, because I'm tall. These are comfy, stretchy but don't look like leggings, and the material is string enough for support and not to show cellulite (you know...) Only down side is they are TOO HIGH in the front going past my belly button which I haven't worn for decades. I'll probably order more of these pants in a size Medium when I lose the baby weight.

I had to order these 3 times to get my correct size. First I bought large since another review said they were very stretchy I decided to look at them as leggings and L is my size. These were extremely small. So going by that size I sized up to 3XL short. These where extremely big. Tried one last time and did a 1XL short. These fit. But I could probably do an XL short. I did not like that they are a bit wider at the ankle than I would like. I’m hoping they fit a bit better after I wash them and some shrinkage. I’m also hoping they don’t fade in the wash. I will update after I have worn them. But they are very comfortable and I’m very happy with the color and stretchy material. Just what I was searching for. Price is awesome too. For reference I’m 5ft 3inch 199lb

I want a pair in every color. Let me start off by saying I hate buying pants. Doesn't matter if it is online or in the store. I can try on 20 pairs of pants in a store and maybe, MAYBE walk out of the store with 1 pair of pants. I have a high waist, little to no eh-hem rear, and thicker thighs. This can make it very difficult to find pants that I love and that fit well. To make matters worse I am a size 7 or 27 which I have found is an increasingly hard pants size to find when you are a frugal shopper. For reference I am 5' 4" and 133 lbs. I came across these and decided to give the size Medium a shot. These pants are all I've ever wanted in pants. So far I have bought two colors: Dusty Rose and Burgundy. The colors are gorgeous. The material feels similar to jeans, but it is much softer. They are super stretchy and they fit like a glove. I really feel beautiful in them. They can be dressed up with a nice work blouse for the office or dressed down with a band t-shirt for a concert. I want to own a pair in every color and toss all my poor fitting pants. I just hope these stay available for long enough for me to buy them when I have funds. Edit: By the way... They have pockets. Real pockets. Deep pockets! Women's pants never have pockets. These are the perfect pants.

i used to love them... updated: 4 months in and you can now see the fibers that some reviewers had mentioned. The stretchy fibers are fraying and mine developed in my butt area. I work a desk job so not sure if its because I sit too much or it just happens from stretching over time. Again, only had them 4 months and they're getting to the point that I can't wear them because I'm afraid they'll be more noticeable as time goes on. I only wash them occasionally so its not from over washing. They were the perfect go to work pants up until now :( the pic shows what I'm talking about.... it may not show up on other colors but certainly does on black pants :( yes thats a close up lol but eventually you wont need to be that close to see the light colored fibers against a black background original review: so far so good! they are a little higher waisted than I'd like but thats just a weird pref that I have. They go all the way to my ankles, aren't too tight anywhere, and so far day 3 have held their figure and stretchiness. Haven't washed them yet so we'll see how they hold up. I'm 5'5 and 126 lbs, athletic build and these do the trick! smaller waist but enough stretch for my butt and calves so I'll be ordering more if they hold up!

The sizes have changed- they’re now smaller :( But these are still acceptable I have two pairs of these pants in black and they’re amazing. They’re a cross between jeans and jeggings. The problem is that they have a lot of elastic fibers woven in which makes for a really comfortable wear but over time and washing the elastic fibers break through the fabric and you’ll see little white strings all over your pant legs. BUT they’re so comfortable that I’m willing to overlook it and just buy more when that happens (hence the four stars). My main problem is that after purchasing two of these in black, I purchased the gray plaid pair and they fit completely different than the black pair! I’m 5’7’’ and 203lbs (16) and the XL was more than roomy for me (maybe even too roomy after a full day of wear). So when I ordered the gray plaid XL you can imagine I was more than surprised when I couldn’t even get them over my butt! (even lost a couple lbs recently, so that’s not the culprit). So beware that not all colors fit the same (for some reason?) Update: I took another star off, because I bought another black XL pair this week and it fits the same as the gray plaid pair. BE ADVISED: I’m assuming this company has changed the sizing for their pants (without indicating so)- size up!

Great pants, size is forgiving, order your true size. I was looking for some comfy pants to wear during spring and summer. I’m in love with these pants, like the rest of the reviewers. I’m two months post partum, 5’9 and 220lbs. While the reviews said there was lots of stretch, I was hesitant to order my true size (XL). I ordered a 2x and it was too big. I can take them off without unbuttoning the pants! I ordered a white in 1x and fushia in xl. The xl definitely fits and is super comfortable, but as the pictures show, there is more bunching in the legs so I think the 1xl fits a little better out of the dried but the xl is better at the end of the day because it doesn’t stretch out as much. I washed the pants according to the directions and I did not notice shrinking. My pink pants did not bleed either. The white pants are see through so wear a long shirt and light underwear. Can’t go wrong with this price or these colors!

I literally love everything about these pant. They are pretty stretchy they actually stay at my ankle even after washing and drying multiple times now and still haven't shrunk up. They are actually still black and don't fade color after one wash, even now still look like when I got them and i have washed them a handful of times. There are front and back pockets!! (Which is really rare in women's pants like these) and for the price they are just AMAZING!!! I have been looking like something like these for soooo long for work because I have to wear black pants but they always tend fade out really quick or shrink up. I will definitely be getting some more! I'm 5'5 and between 103lb-107lb and wear a size 2 in all pants I own

Great for the price I am 5’5” 135lbs. I bought a small as I usually wear small or size 4 pants. I bought these for the office because I hate spending money on office clothes (rather save and spend my money on more expensive outdoors gear). They appeared super tiny when took out of the package, but they are super stretchy (fit more like a jegging) so they actually fit good if you like skinny pants. They cover my ankles so the length was perfect. The stitching isn’t amazing as you can see in the photo but not surprised because of the cost. I actually really like them though. We will see how they hold up being washed, if they don’t fade too much or shrink too much I will buy more colors.

I gave my info and posted pix because that's what I look for in reviews to help me choose! Hope this helps others!

I have long legs and an inseam of 30 inches would fit me perfectly.

Real usable pockets! (: But wrinkle prone ): I was really happy to find out that there were real actual usable pockets in the front instead of the fake pockets that they always put on women’s pants. However, the pockets in the back are fake. Cant win ‘um all, I guess!The pants are very stretchy, so they’ll give a very slimming fit with a tighter appearance. However, you can expect to still be very comfortable in these pants due to them being so stretchy. I’m about 5’7” or 5’8” and about 180 pounds and purchased a size XL, and I’d say they were a good fit and perfect length. Also, I carry most of my weight in my hips, butt, and thighs, and usually I feel like my hips look just too big, but these pants really gave a nice and natural slimming affect (or effect? Ahhhh I’m always forgetting the difference!) for me and my body type.Also I love that these pants have belt loops! these pants look amazing with a nice tucked in blouse or sweater with a cute belt. Just keep in mind that the belt can’t be too wildly thick because the belt loops are still just a tad bit smaller than average.There’s one con with these pants though, and it’s that they are very susceptible to wrinkles. If they come out wrinkly from the dryer, that’s really not a big issue because once you put them on, they’ll stretch around you so nicely that the pants won’t have wrinkles anymore. The issue is that once you’ve been wearing them all day or for a couple of hours, they’ll start to wrinkle and crease around the knees and crotch area. The pictures I’m sharing is after I’ve worn the pants all day at work, so you can see what I mean about the wrinkles. I find this to be a common problem with a lot of pants and honestly these pants are not as bad about it as some others because some other pants can start to wrinkle up so bad around the crotch area that they start to give the appearance of a camel toe, but these don’t ever get that bad. There’s an easy solution for you though if you’re worried about this happening, just buy them in black. The black won’t show the wrinkles like the colored pants. I’m probably going to buy a pair in black too because these really would be the perfect pair of just plain professional black pants to have around. However, I still really love the green pair that I purchased even despite the wrinkles, and I will continue to wear them! These pants are still so super flattering, and that’s why I just couldn’t take a star off for the wrinkles.**UPDATE**So I did end up buying the black pair of these really soon after I had purchased the green pair. Both of these pants have become my absolute favorite professional pants to wear. I literally wear the black pair almost once a week because they’re just such an easy professional go-to and go with almost everything. The vibrant coloring for both pants has never faded. However, I’m coming back to deduct a star because I have had to restitch all the belt loops on both pants. The stitching quality on these pants is downright terrible. For that, these pants should at least be cheaper because if we are all going to have to spend an hour restitching these pants then we all deserve a serious discount for them. Now, maybe it’s just because I have a bigger butt and hips, and I’m always having to pull up my pants by the belt loops, but still, I don’t have to restitch the belt loops on my other pants. Also, I’ve noticed that the stitched seam that goes down the butt looks like it’s starting to loosen and might eventually break or rip soon. I really hope that doesn’t happen because I love these pants so much. They’re so comfortable and such a great fit. I’ve honestly been debating buying more pants in different colors, but I haven’t yet because I don’t know if it’s worth it paying like $26 + having to restitch the belt loops again & the pants may not even last a whole year as I’m worried about with my black pair. Ugh, again, I can’t express enough how much I love these pants and how they look, but I hate that the quality is greatly lacking with the stitching so purchase at your own risk knowing you’ll have to re-sew them ):

I love these! The fit on these pants is much better than I expected... they don't cling right to my hips and butt like a lot of pants like this do; they're just a tiny, tiny bit looser. That being said, you still need to order the same size you normally would. I'm 5'8" and 155, and wear a size 12. Pardon the belly in the pic...🤷‍♀️ The fabric of these pants is very nice... not too thick or thin so they can be worn year-round. Excellent quality. They came out of the dryer beautifully. The only tiny little complaint I have for these is that while the waistband appears to be about 3 inches wide, the elastic band inside is only about 1/2 inch wide, and I can already tell I'll have an issue with it rolling and twisting as time goes on. It would be nice if it was about 2 inches wide. I think it would feel more secure around the waist also, although it's fine now.

Pardon the belly in the pic...🤷‍♀️ The fabric of these pants is very nice... not too thick or thin so they can be worn year-round. Excellent quality. They came out of the dryer beautifully. The only tiny little complaint I have for these is that while the waistband appears to be about 3 inches wide, the elastic band inside is only about 1/2 inch wide, and I can already tell I'll have an issue with it rolling and twisting as time goes on. It would be nice if it was about 2 inches wide. I think it would feel more secure around the waist also, although it's fine now.

5' 8" and 150 lbs, got the medium. I have been looking for nice work pants that didnt break the banks. I found these and they had good reviews and weren't expensive so I decided to give them a try. I ordered the tan and white (with pockets). The white pants were cut more like straight leg pants than skinny (which isn't a huge deal) and were bit smaller in the waist. I actually like the fit of the white pants better BUT they are see through. Definitely need to wear a long tunic or sweater with the white pants. The tan weren't see through at all but they were a bit shorter than the white pants. I couldnt get a good picture with the tan pants on but I attached a picture of them side by side and you can see the difference. They fit more like ankle pants. Both fit well around the waist but were a little hard to get over my thighs and butt. Overall I just didnt love them, but they didnt fit bad enough for me to return them. I'm 5' 8" and 150 lbs and I got the medium in both pairs.

Great pants I originally bought the size 16 regular and they were so long. I’m 5’3 so I returned for the 16 short and they are a perfect fit. Super comfortable and perfect for the office. Has pockets and very flattering on my plus size body. Great value for the money

Expensive looking material!! I usually don’t write reviews, but these pants are incredible! I’m 5’9, 145 lbs, 26.5 in waist measurement and I got a size 2 with 32” inseam. Size 0 inseam not as long as advertised It’s hard to find size 0/XS work slacks with a 32”+ inseam. These were advertised as having a 32” inseam even at that size. I measured them after I got them after thinking they seemed short and they are in fact 31”. I love everything else about these pants, but I wish they were as long as they claimed to be. I wanted to buy them in other colors, but I’m scared they are all going to be shorter than 32” too. :( Update: after washing them in cold water and drying them on the lowest heat setting, they still shrunk an inch and a half and are now like 29.5” and too short to wear at all. :-/ super disappointing

Good Fit in Waist, Made for a Tall Person The gray color of the pants is very nice. The weight is surprising - the material is heavier than I expected. The waist fit is accurate to my size but the length is made for a tall woman (5'8" and above). At 5'7", I can only wear these slacks with very high heels.These pants are not designed to flatter one's figure. They lay straight down without any contour or styling. The sewing craftmanship is good. Overall I would give the quality an 8 out of 10 and the fashion design a 6 out of 10.

High quaity material and craftsmanship but VERY long! These were just a bit wrinkled when they came from the packaging, but right away, I felt they were really high-quality fabric. They are thicker pants with sturdy stitching, and they ironed out very easily with a quick steam/poly setting on my iron. After that, they did not wrinkle even when I grabbed them and balled them up, so that's great news. They will travel well, and when I arrive at my meetings, I won't be a wrinkly mess. The main issue is they are VERY long. I am 5'7", and these have at least two extra inches that will need to be hemmed up. I would say though, the quality of these pants is so good that I am willing to hem them rather than return them. As far as the other sizing - I wear a 16 and they were perfect. They don't ride up in the back and they are nice and flat in the front. Once hemmed, these will be my go-to pants for meetings and travel.

Finally, cotton blend work pants! Materials/Design/Construction - I don't like wearing work/office pants in general, just constricting and uncomfortable. I also hate polyester which most work pants seem to be. Saw these in gray, noticed the cotton blend, and the flat waistband, no crinkled bunchy elastic. AND, there is stretch in the waist even though it is flat, proving it is possible to create a flexible waistband without looking frumpy. Straight leg, sleek, not too wide. Material feels so much nicer than stuffy polyester. No complaints with construction. Fit/Sizing - I am 5'8" tall, U.S. women's size 6, long torso. Amazon sells globally so I never just order my typical U.S. size, but I couldn't find a size chart so used my general Amazon store rule which is, go 1 size up from U.S size. Ordered size 8 (regular, not short) and like the fit; a little roomy in the crotch and thighs but I like how relaxed the waist feels so wouldn't go down. Some reviewers said pants were too long but I'd actually appreciate another inch or two, so consider your own height, shoes and preferences. Photos taken right out of package, the little wrinkles on the fly could easily be steamed or ironed out, otherwise rather wrinkle-free which is impressive.

Extremely Comfortable & Pretty Pants I was really pleasantly surprised when I received these pants and put them on. They are very flattering and they are extremely well made. The sewing is excellent and the way they’ve put the pants together is quality. I am 5’2” and I ordered size 10 short and they are the perfect length for me. I usually wear a size 8 in stretchy pants, but I wanted to be sure that I’m going to be comfortable and that was a good decision. They fit perfectly and the cut is very good. The black color is very nice and attractive. I’m planning to purchase additional colors of these same pants. Zipper quality is smooth and well sewn too. I even received extra buttons with the pants in case one is lost. They are machine washable and they recommend hang dry which I usually do with my pants. It’s also excellent that the manufacturer offers 20 different options of sizes and length of these pants; that is impressive. I also love that these pants have pockets. A great purchase and definitely a recommend from me for anyone looking for quality good looking pants at a reasonable price.

I already ordered more. Feels like sweats, looks like work! Size reference: I am 5’6” and 124-128 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 in jeans and dress pants. Comfortable and great for taller ladies! These are surprisingly awesome pants for the price. The material is thick and has a lovely drape. They are soft and stretchy, and they don't feel at all like you're wearing slacks. I'm 5'10" and I rarely order pants online because everything is too short, but I took the risk because I saw reviewers saying they were long, and I'm happy to that they fit my 34" inseam perfectly (they just brush the tops of my feet)! The pants are very flattering and I feel good in them. I was nervous about size so I ordered up to a 16 because there wasn't a size chart when I ordered, and they're definitely a size too large for me in the waist , so I'd say they fit true to US sizes. I will have the waist taken in, which is easy to do, but I like the fit on my hips and thighs--they feel more modern than the skinny fit of all the pants from 10 years ago. I would definitely order these again. They wash well--I use cold, delicate--and I just hang them to dry. They don't even require ironing. They feel and look a lot like slacks I have from Banana Republic that I paid $98 for, so they are an absolute steal IMO. I will likely order a couple of pairs in other colors (and I'll order the size 14). For reference, I'm 5'10", 190 lbs, 38dd, 32 jeans, inverted triangle/apple shape.

So nice! These pants are awesome. Great quality and the fabric doesnt wrinkle. The waist is very stretchy however the waist band is smooth and not scrunchy in the back like a lot of these pants are these days. The back has no pockets amd these are perfect for work or dressing up. For reference I am 5'5 116lbs and ordered a small. They fit long on me so I have to wear with at least 2 inch heels which is fine. Very comfortable and flattering fit.

High waisted perfection These black slacks exceeded every expectation I had. I never have any luck with these types of pants on here because of the material always being too tight. These pants are absolute perfection. I am wearing a size 4 and I am 5’3, 135 lbs and my lower measurements are 27-36. The material has a nice elasticity to it so it stretched nicely around my waist/hips. I love that it is high waisted and that they don’t hug my butt and thighs. I love the loose hang of them. They are very flattering to wear and look great with a blazer. I would recommend these pants.

Great work pant I’m really happy with these pants. A lot of the work pants out there these days seem to be wide-leg and I’m glad to find nice ones that are straight. I’m 5’9” and around 150 lbs and usually wear a size 6, but I will go up to an 8 in some pants and that’s what I ordered in these. The fabric feels nice, maybe a little stiff but nothing that bothers me at all. Overall these are really great pants and I recommend.

Nice quality pants, but inseam shorter than described I really like these pants and wanted to keep them, but the inseam is only 30.25 inches when the description and the tag say they're supposed to be 32 inches long. I purchased the size 6 in black. They fit true to size, and are a nice mid weight- substantial enough to be smoothing and not look cheap and flimsy. I'm tall with long legs, and really need the 32 inch inseam length. I thought maybe I just got a bad batch, so returned them and reordered. Unfortunately, they were the same 30.25 inch length. I contacted the seller and they said to return them and try again in a month or so to reorder. I will do that, and certainly hope that they corrected their quality control issues, and produce the pants in the stated length. It would end my endless search for a comfortable pair of nice looking black slacks.

In love! Perfect perfect perfect Every gal should have a pair of these, they’re equivalent to the little black dress. I’m 5’2, 100lb and I ordered a size 2. These are snug in the thigh/hip with a little left over in the waist band. The 29” length is perfect for me and I’m wearing an 1 1/2” boot heel in the pic. These have faux back pockets, front pockets large enough for a phone, and belt loops. Material is 53% cotton, 39 nylon, 8 spandex. These are beautiful pants that can be dressed up or down! Professional women look no further bc these are well made, nice weight, classy and elegant with the straight leg opening that accommodated boots perfectly. Finding dress pants that fit me is a nightmare and now I no longer have to fret when an occasion pops up; funeral, interview, nice restaurant, etc. Washing instructions: at or below 60C, steam iron, drip dry

Great material but waist band runs small and doesn’t have much stretch I bought these in a size 6. Size 6 women’s at stores like JCPenny normally are my ideal size. The waist on these is absolutely Too Snug and tight , it is very uncomfortable. Normally I’m either a size S or M depending on the brand. These pants are 14 inches across the band when I measure them with tape (per see the picture attached), which seems like it could fit especially with these being advertised as stretch, I don’t think the waist is stretchy at all for this type of pants, I would probably have to size up to an 8 but I’m not sure I will be as these I am going to return due to the band being non stretchy imo. For reference right now I’m weighing around 130 lbs , my height is 5’1.5 inches my waist measures approximately 28-29 inches and my hips are 40.5 inches, athletic build.The material is smooth and pretty thick and feel comfortable on the skin as the fabric is 53% cotton 39% nylon 8% spandex , so material feels professional and soft. Front pockets are real and back pockets are fake which I think looks good because it doesn’t look bulky on the backside but smooth and sleek on the bum. Overall these pants could be great , and the inseam options are very good for different heights, but with 8% spandex I would expect way more stretch and these don’t have that. I hope this helps someone as I have a hard time buying clothing. On my experience stretch pants usually can accommodate 8-10 lbs loss or gain weight and these just aren’t forgiving and won’t stretch to your size with weight fluctuations.

Seriously good pants! I am ALWAYS afraid to order pants that I can't try on. I went by the size chart and ordered a 12 (For reference I'm 5'8 and my measurements are 41-31-42). Typically I wear an 8 or 10, but I wanted to be safe. I definitely could have ordered a size down. BUT I still seriously LOVE these pants. The material is thick, but sooooo stretchy! And they are truly high waisted, which I love. (No having to pull them up when I sit down so my kangaroo pouch isn't squeezed in half) There is a little bit of "puddling" at the ankle, but I'll usually be wearing boots with these so I don't care. And there's pockets! Good ones that I can actually put my hands all the way in! (The back pockets are not real pockets, but who cares when you have real front ones!) And although I haven't tested them to see if they are 100000% opaque, I'm pretty sure they're going to pass a lot better than some others I've tried. 1000/10 recommend.

Material that moves with you I ordered these black stretchy work pants in 14short since I'm 5'4" so I tend to need pants on the shorter side. Even though I ordered the short version, I still find I have to roll the cuff of the pants under once to give me a clean look, otherwise the pants look baggy on me- I included a photo with one pant leg cuffed and one pant leg as is. My hips measure 45" with a 34" waist, so according to the size chart I was taking a chance on my normal size 14, but aside from being a bit too long, these pants fit me like a glove. Love that the pants have an actual zipper and strong hold button closure, plus real belt loops. The butt has two fake pockets and the front has two real pockets but they're small. I appreciate how these pants are actually high waisted. The material is stretchy, comfortable, and moves with my body.

Thick and stretchy. Size reference: I’m 5’6” and 130 lbs. I normally wear a size 4 but ordered the size 6 from iChosy *based on past purchases from the brand. I’m pleased with the fit. The waist has some stretch but doesn’t did into me while seated at my desk. The length is pretty much full on my shorter legs. After wearing for 8 hours, the fabric did loosen so I think my normal size would've held my shape better by the end of the day. *Past experience with iChosy: I ordered the high waist wide straight legs in size 4 short and loved them. The length was just right for me to be able to wear with flats. The waist was a little loose, which I prefer to skintight. I promptly ordered 2 more styles-Bootcut and Barely bootcut-in the same size 4 short. Both of them were much tighter, uncomfortably so, and I'm not sure if it just the brand inconsistency or the different styles of pant. I will end up donating them due to the sizing issue though, both of those fit more like a 2. So I decided to order a size up this time…. Description: I keep ordering iChosy brand work pants because the fabric is thicker and has a lot of stretch. Anyone who works in a business dress setting can appreciate having a work pant that looks professional but feels ready to lounge. My other pairs have held up well in the wash and delicate dry cycles. Another great feature is the functional and deep hip pockets that will actually hold a phone. The waist is higher than my navel. The only negative is the sizing issue based on my 4 pairs. Definitely recommend trying but maybe order your usual size and 1 up to compare.

I think these deserve more than five stars. Slam dunk. These are some seriously GREAT pants. They are made of cotton, spandex, and nylon. This gives them a great weight and a fabric that doesn't wrinkle easily. They are stretchy including the waistband so they are comfortable. I got 8 which is my normal size. If you are between two sizes, get the bigger one. You have the option of choosing short length or regular length. I chose regular. I am 5'4" and this is just right. It would even be floor-length for someone a few inches taller. The pants are somewhat formal so I am curious to know how the shorter version would look, it is a personal preference. These pants will be your go-to due to how easy they are to wear. AND they are washable!!

Overall great, just a few minor complaints These came out great, they feel heavy duty and the definitely do the job, they are pretty hot to be in during hot weather but that's to be expected from pants like these, I took these out for a 2 day hike and they held up great, I felt like I could abuse these as much as I liked and they would still hold up. There are a few specific aspects of the pants that I will go over: 1. The knee pads: Trying to insert the knee pads will cause you to re-evaluate your entire life but once you understand the technique it's not a big deal, other than that they function great and really protect your knee when kneeling, only complaint is that they are very hard and inflexible so they can get uncomfortable when sitting. 2. The size: I measured my waist before ordering and ordered a 34" waist size, but when I tried the pants on, they were too loose on my waist even after tightening the waist adjustment mechanism, so I was forced to use a belt (which I was planning on using anyway so it wasn't that bad). 3. Overall comfort when walking: While walking in these pants after a while I noticed that the extra reinforcement material on the crotch area in the back of the pants would fold into itself and would bother me so I had to readjust it every once in a while, also the material of the pants is not very stretchy but I didn't find it bothering.

Great work pants I work 12 hour shifts, a few days a week at a restaurant as a bartender/server. I needed new work pants that were durable, stain resistant, and comfortable. These were all of that and more. The restaurant I work at has a semi-strict dress code and these met their standards. I am 5’5” and 160 pounds, a medium fits perfect, just a little on the long side. I also want to add that I ordered a large first and it was very simple to return and order a new size.

Comfortable, flattering, and appropriate for business causal These pants are very comfortable and extremely flattering. I am 5’ 6” and weigh 195. I ordered the 14 and I love them. I could probably have ordered the 12 but I like the 14s for non spanx days!

Amazing fit and nice quality I was skeptical buying pants for work online without trying them on and read tons of reviews first before buying. While I was looking for a lower rise pant and not one that goes to my belly button, these are amazing in every other way. They fit so nicely, keep the crease well in the leg, and look professional. They are great to wear more casually with a blouse tucked in but also would pair nicely with a suit jacket for more formal days when I don’t want to wear a skirt. I am definitely going to check out the other colors. I love the chunky waistband, it gives some personality without being over the top as I don’t ever wear belts. For reference I am 5’ 3” and they do drag the ground if I’m not in heels so I imagine with wearing flats most days the bottoms will get tattered if I don’t hem them up a bit. I am not sure how they will hold up after multiple washings but I am hopeful! They did not stretch throughout the day and start falling off like most other pants seem to do. Overall very happy and impressed for the price.

I am 5’8” around 130 lbs and I purchased a size SMALL and they are too big for me. The stretch is nice and the rise is perfect, but they are a bit baggy and make me feel matronly. The Amazon ad and bag which the pants came in states they are a size S, but the tag on the pant says size 4 but I feel like they are closer to a size 6. I will be trying the XS & i hope they will be closer to what I’m looking for.

I usually wear a size XL or XXL and pants. I ordered these pants in XL and they fit me just fine. According to the tag, the pants are 93% polyester and 7% spandex. They feel very comfortable and they’re pretty stretchy. The pants are a bit see-through, so you may want to wear seamless nude underwear or an oversized top to cover your caboose.

I ordered my regular size at first, but they were a bit tight, so I returned them and went a size up, which fit perfectly. I’m 5’5", and the length was just right. I also ordered another variant (black and white), but they seemed much thinner, so I returned those. (took away 1 star only because of the black pair). I definitely like the yellow and white pair much better. Highly recommend sizing up for the best fit if you’re hippy.

Great pants/ Not for Petites For reference I am 5 ft tall and approximately 119 lb, curvy petite build. The fabric felt really nice and was decent quality. The design was also very cute and fashionable. The only reason I will not be keeping the item and deducted one star is that they are not petite friendly. I ordered a small and the elastic waistband was on the border of being a little too snug but fitting well enough if I wore a top that was not fitted. The design of the pant made it versatile to be casual and flexible enough to dress it up. There was an excess of 5 to 6 in at the bottom for someone of my height. I chose the lighter color option and it is see-through but okay for what I needed it. If this garment was cheaper I would keep it and invest the money to have it hemmed but the price tag was already on the steeper side and and was not worth investing additional dollars to get it hemmed.

Love the Pockets and Fit. These are very lightweight flow pants. It has an elastic waist with a little bow to tie. The elastic is triple sewn and stays up, has a smocking sewn look. The length is very long. I am 5’7” and this is the length they are. I have worn a wedge shoe with this to bring up the length. In the picture I’m wearing flip-flops. I am going to try to shorten these so I could wear them with a flat sandal during the summer. Other than the length, these pants are perfect. Even though I’m 5’7” I don’t have long legs.I like the black-and-white color. I change this with an all white shirt, all black shirt or a combo.As far as washing, I wash them in the washing machine, but I do hang them to dry. They dry pretty quickly and they need a little steaming sometimes after to make the flow, right. There are no directions as far as drying these pants, but they are polyester so they should do fine in the dryer.

Comfortable but not flattering What my wife thinks of her new pants... Pros- - These are comfortable, free flowing pants. Perfect for summer, spring or fall days. - Love that they have pockets. - I like the deep stone grey color. They will go with many different tops. - The ruffled top and attached belt make the pants cuter and more interesting to look at. Cons- - Not flattering! Unless you are a super model, these will not look the same on you as they do the girl in the ad photo. Plus I think they edit those phots to make her look better. I am 5'2" 115 lbs and these make my bottom half look SO much bigger. Even my husband said so. - These are short, long pants. I am short and they fit me just a tad too short. - They are a little snug in the waist. I am usually between a size S and M. I got the M and they are a bit tight, not too bad, just realize that if you are between sizes, I would suggest going up a size just for the waist comfort. Overall I will keep these to wear around the house but I don't think I will wear them outside the confines of my own home.

Great pants! Details to share I'm sharing because I find detailed reviews extremely if they contain details beyond "I love this." I hope this is to someone else shopping. _ I'm 5'7", weigh 150 lbs (sometimes 158 lbs.). My shape is hourglass, meaning I have hips (40") and a smaller waist (33") I am over 50, so I have done some shopping in my life. My US size is 10-12 at Macy's and 12 at Target. _I like clothes that do not bind, loose fit, but do not look like I am wearing an old canvas bag. _I like good fabric, and I am a longtime sewer, so craftsmanship of clothes is important to me. I look at the construction of a garment. So, these pants: _Linen fabric is quality and a mid-weight, no shine and has a great but subtle linen texture - the fabric is soft to the skin and does not scratch or itch. Good for three seasons in the north of the USA. The drape is excellent right out of the bag without ironing or putting them in the dryer for a touch-up. The elastic at the waist is about 1-1/4 inch wide and not stiff - the elastic waist could easily accommodate a waist 8 inches bigger than mine or more - it depends on the fit you like - I actually might make the elastic a little tighter, an easy 10 minute fix if you sew or know someone who does sew. The pants have two good sized pockets. The construction is excellent. The fabric is cut correctly so it drapes correctly. _Depending on your lifestyle and personal style these pants can be worn almost anywhere besides a formal event. How you style with a top, shoes and accessories is the key. Definitely outside pants, not sleeping pants. _When shopping online make sure you get a tape measure and know your measurements. I ordered US L and the fit was as expected for me - I could have sized down one size to a US Medium, but I like a loose fit. Also, make sure you look at the manufacturer's size chart, not the generic Amazon size chart to make your size selection. _My photos are of the pants right out of the bag, and in front of a dusty (sorry) mirror, but hopefully they are to you. _I have been wearing them for a few hours now and these pants are so comfortable, so cute. If this is your style, your silhouette, I can completely recommend these pants. IXIMO Women's Casual Cotton Linen Baggy Pants with Elastic Waist

These pants are AMAZING... most of the time. I LOVE these pants. That may be an understatement. I am 5'5, 127lbs, and the mediums fit me nearly perfectly. I need to take in the waist line by just a tiiiiny bit for them to be perfect, but I also have a pretty defined hourglass figure (tiny waist, larger hips/butt), which is why I love wearing pants like these. I've spent years shopping worldwide for pants JUST like these, and these ones are among the most comfortable, durable, thick, and flattering that I've purchased. I have two pairs of the khaki and two of the black, and I'd like to buy more since I wear them daily, but... That's where my 4 stars instead of 5 come in. I've ordered from this company a few times, and the quality and color of the material is hit or miss and tends to change. My first pairs of khaki and black were thick, good-quality material, good and consistent color, but when I reordered another pair of khaki - this time in both medium and small to see if the small fit any differently - the fabric of the small was noticeably different. It was thinner and more yellow-toned than the medium pair I loved so much. I returned them. The mediums were fine; they were made of the same soft, thick linen my original pair had been made of, same soft khaki color, etc. A month later, I tried ordering another two. a medium pair of black and a medium pair of khaki, assuming the quality problem was perhaps linked to the small size only, but the mediums that just arrived are the color and quality I had returned before! In my photo, the pair on the left are the new pair that I'm returning. It's difficult to tell in a photo since you have to physically touch them to truly feel the difference, but it's absolutely noticeable in-person, so much so that I can't stand the feeling of them against my legs. The black was also the poorer-quality fabric. I decided to eat the cost and try washing them immediately to see if that helped, but even washed and air-dried, they're noticeably different quality from my first black pair I ordered two months ago. I'm disheartened - I want to buy so many of these so I never have to worry about ruining a pair, but I don't want to keep ordering and returning in hopes that the pair that arrives will be the quality and color I love so much. There's definitely a consistency problem, so be warned of that. If you end up receiving the better quality pants, you'll love them. I know I do.

Great Pants love the thicker linen fabric, i’m 5’11” and and they go all the way to my ankle even if i wear them at my waist. great comfy pants, will be a new fall favorite. the tapered cut adds cool movement when wearing.

Khaki color runs smaller than Gray or Blue I really like these pants other than the fact that sizing isn't consistent across the colors. I ordered the gray, the blue and the khaki. The Gray and Blue fit me, but the Khaki felt like it was about a size smaller than the other two colors around the hips. I measured them against each other and found about an inch difference. These pants shouldn't be worn tight because the seams don't look very strong. They are otherwise very breathable and comfy. Some folks might find them to be a little short. I'm 5'4 and the length is just about right for me with a little bit of a long fit.

my favorite pants absolutely adore these pants. 5’6 200lbs pear shaped wearing the XL. i have them in grey, green, and black. be mindful of what style you are getting to ensure it is the fit you want, all of mine are the original style. the three i have have are pretty damn consistent in fit and length but the waistband is just slightly looser on the grey than it is on the other two, after a few wears they all begin to feel the same. the material is not see through, it is a very sturdy and borderline heavy fabric but still light enough for me to feel comfortable wearing them throughout the summer in 100+ degree weather. they are so comfortable and flattering, but i think your height does matter for them to fit you correctly. they ride right on my belly button for my ideal length, i think people who are taller or have longer legs than me would need to wear them in a low rise fashion to prevent a high waters look. shorter people can definitely just opt for a higher waist or hem them to make it work. i wash them on delicate and hang them up to dry, i do not want to risk destroying them in the drier. i’m super happy with these pants and wear them most days. i’ve only had them during summer months but you could definitely wear them with leggings underneath for added warmth in the winter.

Pay attention to the style when you pick the color These pants are terrific. The color is not bright white (which is what I was hoping for) but the white is "whiter" than cream. The waistband is wide and comfortable. I am 5'6" with a short torso and I ordered a medium. I'm usually a 4/6. The length is perfect for me and I really like the elegant balloony bottoms. The fabric is medium-weight and totally fine modesty-wise. There is no fabric content tag, but I suspect the pants are more cotton than linen. There is a linen-like texture to the fabric, but the feel of the fabric leans towards cotton. As other reviewers have noted, the pockets are low, but I like their low placement. When you order, pay attention to the style names - some pants are available in different styles so be sure to look at the larger photos to make sure you're ordering the correct silhouette. I see these pants getting a lot of summertime wear in my future - I wish they came in olive green!

The MOST Comfy Pants! I live in these pants. The only time I'm not wearing them is when they are being washed, summertime, and when loungewear is inappropriate. They are so fun too. My BF calls them my "balloon pants." I've worn them around the house and when traveling. And I get SO many compliments on them when I wear them out. They can actually be dressed up a bit if you can believe it. AND POCKETS!!! Get yourself a pair of birks and tie a v-neck t-shirt in a knot + messy bun = DUNZO. The pants are thick and seem like great quality for the price. The biggest downside is waiting for them to hang dry since it's not recommended you put them in the dryer. Though, I imagine if you did put them in the dryer they would shrink up a ton (cotton/linen does that), so don't do that. I got the gray color. It's has a tinge of blue to it, overall I'd call it a dusty blue color. I'm 5'2" 140 lbs and they drag a bit on me but not much. I could see these being pretty short on someone that is taller (over 5'5'). Get them, you'll love em!

Comfy and versatile, perfect for apple body type, great alternative to jeans for travel These pants have become an unexpected staple in my wardrobe. They are incredibly comfortable, have very deep pockets, are surprisingly wrinkle resistant, and can be reworn multiple times without laundering (much like jeans). I’m 5’7” with a 31-32” waist (apple shape) and wear and 8-10 in pants. I have several pair of these pants in both small and medium. I’d recommend going with the smaller if you’re in between sizes. The attached pics are my size M; the S are less baggy on my bum. I travel a lot and these are my default pants for long flights. They are a bit bulky to pack when not being worn, but are so versatile that I find they are worth the space. They are a med-thick cotton (not linen) and are comfortable in a wide range of temperatures. They are easy care. I wash them in the machine with cool water on the regular cycle and dry them on low heat. Packing wrinkles fall out easily using the “spritz and smooth” method (spritz with plain water and smooth with hands while laid flat on the bed).

Great quality I ordered Style4 in Blue, size Large I am 5’5” and approximately 170#. My inseam is approximately 32”. I have a rectangular figure with a thicker waist and not a lot of hips. I usually wear a US women’s size 10 or medium to large. I ordered these in size large after reading reviews and deciding not to size up. They fit comfortably. The waistband is a little snug for my liking, but not problematically tight. They fit right above the navel and just below the ankle on me. The crotch gusset hangs down just about 2.5” when pulled up to my waist. The quality seems very nice: good pockets, sturdy, and well made. I live in a hot climate and wear a lot of light linen clothing in the summer. The fabric is a bit heavier than I was expecting. That’s not a complaint; I still like the pants, but it may be something to take into consideration if you order them for summer wear. I have not laundered them yet, so I can’t say whether they’ll bleed or shrink. I have uploaded a close-up photo of the fabric for your reference.

Not sure I went with the size chart & reviews of people with my height & weight....they just seem so long on me. They looked great on the reviews. I'm 5'3, 140 & I ordered a large. I had to flip my waist band down and they're still really long. I suppose oversized is what I wanted🤷

Not For Curves (reviewing from my bro’s account) — i’m so bummed!! i wanted to love these pants. ordered a L after reading reviews; i love baggy clothes but didn’t want to swim in them, so i went with my true size. i got the ones with the pleats, maybe that’s where i went wrong — they’re roomy in the leg like i wanted but the crotch is cut really weird. they’re also pleated in the back. doesn’t matter how/where i placed them, it did that thing where it looks like your thighs are eating the pants. if i dropped them to sit lower on my hips, they rise on my bum. good length (5’2”, they’re at my ankle). the material is soft, didn’t get “scratchy” from them. definitely a blended fabric; not heavy or super light, but you can get away with them in like, 70 degree weather. if you have thighs, hips, and a bum, i do not recommend; at least the pair with pleats. i may cross my fingers and try the regular, loose pair!

Very Nice, Indeed Great pants, and an interesting bottom hem that reminds me of my favorite Eileen Fisher pants (but at a fraction of the price)! I've been on the fence about these pants for several weeks, but decided to trust the reviewers and take a chance. I'm 5'6.5" and wear a size 12 (normally ranging from size 10-14 when my weight fluctuates). I opted to get the larger of sizes to fit me, a large rather than a medium; as I can have them tailored if too big. A good decision. The linen fabric is tightly woven and a good weight, this is a quality fabric. Pictured are the pants as they currently fit, I will actually get the legs taken in about an inch, and try out the size medium when I order another color. Yes, I will be buying at least one other pair of these pants! UPDATE: I've bought several pairs of these gorgeous trousers. I have 2 pair each of grey, the turquoise blue, and the off white. and single pairs of the deeper blue, black and mauve.
