Absolutely love the cut and weight of these pants! I absolutely love these. Look, I was one of those big baggy Hot Topic pants kids back in the 90s and early 2000s. I've tried for a long time to ensure the skinny jeans, slim fit jeans look. I'm done with it. I can't stand peeling off my pants or the discomfort of jeans. I bought these and I don't ever want to go back. I love these so much I bought a second pair immediately after the first arrived. I washed these and put them in the dryer on normal heat and they shrank a little, but enough that they still fit without hugging too close or cutting into me. I can still pull them away and have a good several inches. The weight on these is great. Airy for summer, but heavy enough for winter, and I've got enough room to throw on thermals underneath if I want to. They're perfect. So far the stitching has held up. For reference, I'm short and squat. I'm 5'4", 135 lbs. These don't drag when I walk which is a miracle. (I wear my pants at my belly button.) They're tapered at the very bottom so they're not too wide legged at the bottom. I was worried because wide leg pants on me looks terrible but these are cut in a way that's super flattering. I would say the only con? Is that the gray is a bit lighter than I expected based off photos but I love them anyway. I'll update if anything terrible happens but I intend to live in these. Absolutely wonderful pants lol.

I'm 5'5 150lbs 44 butt and 33waist. Muffin top approved. I chose size large. Reviews all said "true to size." Fits surprisingly well. I wanted baggy and long. They are baggy and long. 80% linin, 20% cotton VERY nice. Sturdy but breathable fabric, strong seams. Wide elastic waist that would be perfect fit if I lost 2lbs. Lots of elastic waist pleats. Nice deep side pockets. Strong seams down front and back, and sides of each leg. Lantern cut is cool. My 1st experience with this style. I love the length without having to walk on my cuffs. Beautiful x hatch weave. I'm super impressed. It's over 100° right now and I'm bummed I cant wear them. Maybe wet and then wrung out... They may be very cooling like that. My only negative comment is the color. The grey in the pictures is dark slate/blue steel grey. Mine are normal med grey. I'll keep them but they don't " pop " like I expected. Even in my own pictures they Iook darker than they are. I chose these finally because of a review from an older women who said she will wear these for the rest of her life. I agree. They are pro. My images are from right out of the bag so will smooth out nicely soon.

These pants looked great in the pictures, egged on by the positive reviews I decided to get them. I ordered according to the size chart provided, I measured myself 3 times to be sure. However when the pants arrived they did not fit at all like the advertised picture. The pants are way smaller than advertised! I requested a refund from the seller. They only offered $10 and told me the sizing is correct. They will NOT give you 100% refund. For context, I’m 5’5” and weigh 130 pds. I should’ve listened to the other reviews that also mentioned they were too short. The material is okay and waistband not too tight, so very comfy but definitely made for a shorter person. Pictures: I the first 2 pictures I posted, the pants are sitting at my waist and as you can see the length of the pant is above by ankle. In the third picture I tried bringing the pant length to sit at my ankles however that meant bringing the waist of the pants to sit below the line of my bellybutton, I’m pointing to show where that line is in the picture. Wearing pants that low isn’t very comfortable for me or very flattering looking.

Extremely Comfortable, But Fabric Pilled and Split I bought these pants to take with me on a long vacation. They were so amazingly comfortable that I was about to order a second pair. However, when I took a closer look after wearing the pants for about the 10th time, I had noticed pilling in the area near the crotch on the pant legs, and upon closer examination the fabric was thinning. By the end of the day a hole had appeared in the area. Due to this I will obviously not be getting a second pair, which a shame because I really did love them. Maybe if you don't have thigh that rub together when you walk they will be fine, but if you have thick thighs like I do, be warned.5'6", 165 lbs, Size Large - Fit was great (loose and comfortable)

Great summer pant I love these pants. I own around 4 pairs of cotton linen pants and the combo is great for summer and humid weather. Linen wicks off moisture better than any other fabric. I should know since I’m a fashion product developer. I’ve studied all textiles and I’m in love with natural fabrics such as linen, cotton, and rayon. I also can’t deal with synthetics like polyester and most clothing on Amazon is polyester crap mass produced and private labeled. The length is perfect for me and most pants are short for me. I’m 137 lbs and 5’ 7”. I have an issue with almost all pants being too short if they are made by cheap Chinese brands and also many cheaper department store brands. I will probably buy more of these in other colors.

Update, Love pants are the business!!! Update: I probably could have kept the medium size as they stretch after wearing. Extremely comfortable, do wrinkle but not as much as 100% linen. I wore mine literally all day, by end of day they look very big on my body.I'm 5'1 145 lbs. Initially ordered a medium they fit just a tad tight, returned them and ordered a Large, perfecto! These are mostly linen, with some other material. It says 100% linen, but when ironing they smooth out very nicely, and when wearing they don't wrinkle as much as 100% linen. I love them. Ordered black, khaki and tan colors. I followed the instructions, wash cool, barely rinse then laid flat. Ironing was effortless. I would not fold over a hanger, instead, will hang by the waist area. Very well made, love the ankle gathering, and decent size front pockets, faux back pockets. I'm very pleased with this purchase.

Wore out in less than 7 months. So disappointed. 😞 I really liked these pants. They fit well and were very comfortable. It is difficult to find clothes that are made of 100% natural fibers, so I was willing to pay to get what I wanted. These pants arrived at my house on October 18th 2020 and on May 4th 2021 the khaki pair ripped on the inner thigh. As you can see from the photographs above, the fabric is threadbare! For $30 a pair, I expected a little more longevity. Extremely disappointed and feeling cheated. Update: Unfortunately I tried one pair and liked them so I ordered a number of others in different colors. On June 13, 2021 the Navy pair ripped.

Cute, like the elastic at waist, wish they were longer I love the green color of these linen pants. They are a cute style and a little bit dressier than my other linen pants since they have the button/zip closure. I like that they have elastic at the back of the waist, though, so the sizing is a bit adjustable. I generally wear a size 8/10 in bottoms (135 lbs/5'6") and the medium fits just fine. If I wear them where they should fall - high waisted - they are shorter than my preference. If I pull them down, I can get them just about long enough. When I washed them I did not dry them, to avoid shrinkage in the length. The pockets are great! Totally big enough to hold a phone.

These pants have a button fly, which can sometimes be tricky to find the proper waist fit, but the back of these pants have an elastic waist that is perfect for a 'custom' and comfortable fit! The length of the pants is about 28" inseam, which hits me right at pant length but will be more of a culotte fit for someone taller. The waist has belt loops, as well as a removable 2-strand tie belt accessory. The fabric itself is a little rough, which caused me to remove one star; it's light and wrinkles just like linen, but is not quite as smooth in texture as I'd have hoped. Overall, these are decent quality for the price point, and very wearable summery/warm weather pants. I'm pleased with them and would recommend these pants, especially to petites looking for an inseam that won't drag on the ground.

Love love love, soft and flattering, very good quality These pants are exactly what I was hoping they would be, but better. The photo you see is after a wash and tumble in the warm dryer (minimal shrinkage). And no wrinkles. I ordered these size S pants - a linen and viscose (rayon) mix, for a 5'3", 110 lb petite young woman with a 24" waist and curvy hips. Although the actual waist, even washed and dried, is a bit larger at appx 26", the pants can be tightened for a smaller waist with the attached belt or another belt. Also, I noticed that there is room to move the button over about 3/4" to make the waistline a bit smaller. For those who have larger waistlines, there is stretch of about 3", maybe a tad more. These are a higher waist pant, meaning, belly button area, and there is adequate fabric in the behind so that it isn't tight or prone to give a wedgie. In fact, the way the pants are cut in the back, and the fabric, makes for a nice drape that resembles the model in the photo. The pants are well-cut and have good stitching, with no loose threads or shoddy workmanship. They are similar to what you might find at one of the large anchor department stores in the mall. The fabric is neither too thin (not see-through) nor too thick (denim). It's perfect for spring and fall, and summer days that aren't over the mid-80s. I would say they are the thickness of a soft chambray shirt, thicker than a man's dress shirt, but not stiff at all. I am so impressed with this fabric composition that I am going to continue to look for garments with this mix: 55% Linen / 45% Viscose. The price point is an excellent value and I would have no hesitation whatsoever about buying again.

Full Length Pants Unless You're Really Tall I'm 5'2", 140 pounds. I ordered my usual size, Medium. They fit fine around the waist because of the stretchy elastic, but they are not capris on me. The legs come all the way down to the top of my foot. The color is definitely not maroon. It's terra cotta. They come out of the package with a faded, pre-washed look. Some people will like that, but it's not my cup of tea. To me they just look old and over-washed. They're also way too wide in the hips and legs. I understand that they're meant to be "wide-legged," and the width might be ok if you're over 5'8" and they're really capris on you. But on me there's way too much fabric. They end up looking baggy and ill-fitting, make me look dumpy. I'm going to have to take about 2" off the outside of the legs and maybe 6-8" off the hem. I washed and line-dried them before trying on, and they came out very wrinkled, which is a bummer because I'm not about to haul out the ironing board. Next time I'll try the dryer on low and see if that works better. On the plus side, they do appear to be well made and are a nice, light weight for summer wear. Final note: I am extremely sensitive to tags in clothes. I always have to remove them. This pair of pants, like so many others, has the tags sewn directly into the seam, so when I removed them it left a hole behind and a sewing chore for me to do. Annoying to have to mend a brand new garment!

Front band strange These pants are pretty cute for summer and fit fairly well except the front band area is a bit strange. The back and sides of the waistband and regular and straight, but in the front the band kind of curves down more and thickens. I’m not really sure what the point of that is but I feel it’s unflattering and almost just looks like a sewing mistake. I’ll still probably wear them a fair amount this spring/summer. I’m 5’5 and they do hit above the ankle as advertised but that is that awkward length where these pants would probably only look good with flip flops and look strange/cut off with tennis shoes etc.

Quality pants These women’s wide leg capri pants were delivered swiftly in simple packaging. They have been made of a good quality cotton blend material with no loose threads, neat stitching and hemmed beautifully. The material feels heavier than expected and is not at all see through in the denim blue color I ordered. Using the size chart provided, I ordered a size medium and they fit true to size. I’m usually a small-med, 5’6 130lbs for reference. The elastic waist is very stretchy and forgiving, thus allowing you to size up or down depending how you prefer your pants to fit. They sit right above my feet, hitting my ankles so too short for winter and boots but will be nice in warmer weather. If you’re petite they will probably fit like regular length pants. They have pockets! Not big ones but pockets nonetheless! The color is more true in the first and last photo. I’m happy with these pants and look forward to wearing them when it gets warmer. I highly recommend this seller and these pants. I will probably order more pairs in the other colors available.

TO ALL MY TALLER GIRLS Me: 5’10ish 140 lb woman. I’m typically a small-4/6 34 inseam 27 pre pregnancy 33 postpartum I bought a small- before babies Bought medium- postpartum To all my tall girls that feel out of place… MY TALL GIRLS, THESE PANTS COULD BE FOR YOU! These pants can definitely be dressed up or dressed down for a more chic look. They are thin and perfect for spring/summer weather. The material isn’t high end material but it isn’t trash either. The stitching is nice through out and clean. Normal trousers sit lower low but these are true high waist pants. The bottom is cuffed up and its sewed to keep in place(see picture) If you carry your weight in your midsection I can see these highlighting that area for you. Consider wearing shape wear to smooth everything out. I noticed it was less forgiving on my postpartum lower belly but pre-pregnancy I did not have that problem. The inseam seems to be approx 32 but because it’s cuffed and only sewed on 2 opposing sides- it slightly drapes adding a some additional length. If you’re short, these will be way too long for you. Pros: THEY FIT TALLER WOMEN!!!!!! I put these on with a low block heel and also platform sneakers. Classy/chic look can be achieve getting the most usage out of styling these for different occasions. Iron/steam easily Cons: They aren’t wrinkle free and hold a bit of wrinkles if you stay in a position for way too long. It doesn’t wrinkle nearly as bad at linen. 👍

Hardly any stretch, best for standing Photo size reference: I am 5’6” and 130 lbs. I normally wear a size 4/27 but decided to order the 28 in the trouser jeans. For my desk job, I was glad for the extra inch as there is very little stretch in the fabric. However, the denim did lose some shape after sitting a few hours and I did not love the “fupa” effect of the loosened lower abdomen area. Super high-waisted, with hip and back pockets. My silhouette was fly when I first put the jeans on but by the end of the day I had some lumps and bumps from all angles. I absolutely counted down the minutes until I could change though because the jeans are not comfortable for cross-leg sitters. The stiff fabric dug into all my crevices and made a few more. My opinion is that Joes Jeans is a sought after brand of high-quality denim…but this particular pair is best worn standing for this wide hipped female.

Higher high rise, run small I like the look of the Joe's Jeans The Trixie Trousers, but they have are a higher high rise than I was expecting and aren't the most comfortable for me. They totally cover my belly button. This important information isn't in the listing, but I found it on the brand website: Rise: 11.5", Back Rise: 15.125", Leg Opening: 22.5", and Inseam: 28" *cuffed*. I also found that some other sites say these run small, and I agree. I have a 29-inch waist and 36 hips, and I got a 28. They fit me snugly, but I prefer some extra breathing room, so I probably should have sized up to the 29 in these. I wear a 28 or 6 in other jean brands and have worn a 28 in Joe's in the past. I was thinking these might be a bit bigger, because they have some stretch. They do stretch out a little and feel more comfortable after I've been wearing them a while, but it's not a big difference. I'm 5'5", and the height is perfect for me when I'm standing -- they are just above the top of my feet. But, when I sit, they ride up a bit, I guess because of the cuffs and high rise. These won't be favorite jeans, but they are fun and trendy. I recommend them. Just make note of the 11.5" inseam and size up if you're between sizes.

Love Joes Jeans! I have to say I have always loved this brand. Their jeans are so comfortable and only get better the more you wear them. These have such a flattering fit! I am also LOVING a cuffed jean lately. It really adds a little something to a look and makes a pair of classic jeans a bit more interesting. Wearing these with a classic pump, flat, or sling back would look really chic! I ordered my normal size and they fit great. I am only 5’2” so they aren’t as cropped on me as on the model, but I don’t mind one bit. Love them!

Truly high waisted pants! If you want true "high waisted" pants, these are the ones! I have been searching for pants that actually came up to my waist for a few years. The total length of the medium size pants from waist to bottom of hem is 42 inches. And the waist width with pants folded is 14 inches. The waist band is snug. So, if you want a little breathing room, get the next size up. I am 5 ft 5 in and weigh 129 pounds and the length is perfect for me; however, the waist is a little more snug than I prefer after washing them. They are 85% cotton, so I think they shrunk slightly in the width, but surprisingly not in the length. I ordered the royal blue color. However, these pants are more of a navy blue color. I highly recommend them!

Comfortable, soft, and cool, quality is alright This is my favorite style of rayon pants: elasticated one-size-fits-most waist, and wide in the legs. When I first opened the package my initial thought was: the threads are too coarse and the weave is too wide. However, after washing them and running them through the dryer on a low heat they became the more familiar soft, flowing rayon that I'm more familiar with. The fit works well for me. I have a 35" waist and 46" hips. They are wide in the legs, which I love. These are a great length for me; I like pants grazing above the ankle. They have pockets - handy. About the quality: I would say it's just okay. As you can see in the photos, the ends of the drawstring are unfinished and ragged. Also, there is an obvious gap where the white elastic shows through the opening in the waistband. I've already removed the drawstring and whip stitched the opening closed so no white is seen. [I don't see the reason to have a drawstring unless you have an 18" waist and you have to tighten the pants up to stop them from falling off your body]. There are loose threads here and there, and the workmanship is just average. Bottom line: would I buy them again? Possibly. I'm not sure these pants are a good value, however, based on the quality. I don't imagine they are going to last for more than a season. Knowing that, and not having high expectations, but considering they are a style and fabric that works for me during hot weather, and they are conveniently available on Amazon, I'm giving them 4 *.

The pockets are deep and hold my Pixel 8 Pro without any issues, and the drawstring keeps the pats at my waist. The pattern is bright and beautiful, and other than some wrinkles that will come out after washing (which I don't actually care about) these are ready to go. Soft and Stretchy Jeans; CONSULT THE SIZE CHART! These jeans are super soft and have tons of stretch! However, when ordering I didn't read the size chart, which was an error on my part. I am 5'5" and 180 pounds. I wear a size 12-14 in Maurices Jeans, and so I ordered a 14 in these. I am large in my stomach, thighs, hips and badonkadonk. LARGE. Ordering too small lets these jeans hug all my curves, and it's not flattering in the slightest. Again, MY MISTAKE because I did not consult the size chart. I should have ordered an 18, and I think these would have been a lot more flattering as they would skim over those curvy areas, rather than highlighting all that's wrong with them. I have goals to lose some weight, so I'll be wearing these at home for now, and saving them for public when I reach my goals. If I haven't said it enough, consult the size chart! Take your measurements, match them up with the chart, and then order!

Glad I followed their sizing chart I normally wear a size 4 or 6 in jeans but ordered these in size 10 due to their chart and I'm glad I did because these fit. These jeans are very soft and extremely stretchy--not a lot of structure if that makes sense. The tag says 1% spandex but I believe there's a lot more spandex than what their tag says. In addition to a regular set of belt loops these jeans have a narrower 2nd set of loops right beneath for the included decorative denim strap/belt. The denim strap/belt is easily removable (just looped through), which is good because you'll have to undo it each time you need to use the restroom. I don't have a very full behind and I find these jeans more flattering on me than most. At 5'4" these are a great length on me. I hope this information helps with your purchasing decision.

Light Blue- Size 16 I am 5' 3' and I ordered these light blue straight leg jeans in my usual size of 16. I really like how the high waist fits, not tight and the top of the pant waist doesn't roll down like my other pants do. I don't have to hide the top part of them like I do my other pants. The spandex in the material gives the jeans enough stretch so they move with my body, plus the material is not as heavy so the pants are more enjoyable and comfortable to wear. They call theses straight leg, I guess by the way the pants hang, I would rather have this type of straight leg instead of the ones that are tight down by your ankles. The only thing I would change about these jeans would be the length, I would like them a little bit longer. I am not sure how the frayed ends are going to hold up and if I wanted to hem the jeans, I won't be able to since they are already shorter than I like.

Jackson approves! I’m really impressed - and did not expect to be. Jeans are jeans. But these are maybe one of three pair of jeans in my lifetime that are not too long! I am 5’1” - so this is a rarity. I bought a size 14. Also, I had not noticed in the description that spandex is one of the materials - this doesn’t mean at all that they are tight, but rather they have very nice give in the tighter areas (i.e. waist). They are indeed frayed - so this is a worry as they might continue to fray. I have some Stop Fray that might protect the hem. The description also states they are straight-legged. True, but very wide, as wide as some bell bottoms. One more thing - unfortunately the jeans have a strong odor, almost like gasoline. Most of the smell came out in the first wash; I’m certainly hoping it will dissipate altogether in the next wash. See picture. Nice jeans. I really like them.

Flattering and stretchy material. I am typically a size 8/10 and I ordered the L. 160lb, 5’7”. These pants fall about ankle length and I really like the fringe on the bottom as well as the length. The mirror doesn’t do them complete justice. They also fit me very well in my hips and bottom being tighter there and loose down my thighs and legs. I really like the way they fit and they are extremely stretchy and comfortable to wear. I am able to actually pull out some of the material around my waist as you can see. They do stretch out a little as you wear them but nothing abnormal, and they don’t fall down as you wear them. I put these in my washer and dryer and they didn’t shrink or have any type of seams that fell apart after washing. They are a very good quality jean and I would 100% recommend to any middle size woman who has a little bit of a belly and loves leggings and doesn’t want to wear jeans. These jeans are still very comfortable.

Great fitting camo wide leg jeans I'm 5'2" and 124 lbs, and got these in a small since a medium in these types of 'barrel leg' jeans in the past has been too big. The waist is a bit snug, but overall a good fit everywhere else. Hits right at the ankle so no hemming required. The fabric is high quality medium weight denim in a camo design. Two side deep pockets and 2 standard back pockets. The waist has a cotton drawstring waist band that you can choose to use or not. Fun and trendy for fall.

Cute Style But…. The style of the - Kedera Women Striped Pants Wide Leg Trousers Casual High Waisted Pants Summer Flare Jeans with Patched Pockets – is super cute but I there are a couple issues with these jeans that leave me feeling slightly disappointed. They fit my fuller figure however, at the side of the legs near the hips, they weirdly bulge out a little. This is not evident on the photos of the models in the listing. Perhaps, they are slightly too big? A size chart at the time of my purchase would have been . Also, the stripes at the back center seam do not line up correctly and this too looks weird in my opinion. The fabric is stiff as well. I’m hoping that with multiple washings, it will soften up and look better on. For sizing reference, my waist measures 38”, and my hips measure 48”. I am 5'4" tall. I purchased a size XX-Large.

Absolutely obsessed I LOVE these pants. I am 5’3 ish and about 145lbs and they fit perfectly. They have nice stretch but not too much, it holds its shape well throughout the day. I washed them inside out on a delicate cycle. All the pearls have stayed in tact through a few wears and washes!

SO BRIDAL I wore these pearl jeans for brunch on my bachelorette and they are so perfect! Definitely going to style different and wear again! LOVE These pants are an absolute FAVE! They were super comfortable. Every person I came in contact with complimented them. Size Medium 5’3 145lb.

These pants could have a great potential (for me) if the size was x2 smaller. 5 stars for the fabric - love it! 3 stars for the style - too wide, too long. Love the fabric - it feels silky, lightweight, fantastic for steamy S Fl! It is somewhat perforated and that’s why I like it. I have similar fabric shorts from Lululemon and those shorts are my FAVORITE item from that brand because of that airy/breezy perforated fabric. Full Five stars for that. Hate how wide and long these pants are on my 5’3, 26 waist, 106lbs, 33 hips frame. The waist is very spot on, but the rest of the pants is just too overwhelming. If the seller makes the same style pants (pleated, dress up or down type) but half of the fabric in the width, shorter length (25-26 for petites) and more of the mid waist than high waist, I would most certainly and happily buy that. But for now, I don’t see myself wearing it, just too enormous for me. If you are taller with similar measurements and enjoy the wide leg style - go for it, the fabric is great.

Great casual summer pants These pants will be great for summer. I ordered the Army Green color in Large. I find them true to size, very roomy with plenty of stretch, and nice deep pockets. I'm 5'6" and I found them to be just a tad long when I wear them at my natural waist. The first photo is right out of the bag; the second is after hemming them 2 inches and steaming out most of the wrinkles. I think they'll be versatile. While thin, they're not see-through at all, so they can be worn shopping or out to lunch as well as at the beach. While I think I could dress them up for work, I feel like they'd be a wrinkled mess by the end of the day. Overall, I'm very happy with these pants.

Accurate to product photos. More for looks than comfort on me. I am 5’6” inches tall, 123 pounds on an empty stomach, and a consistent US size 4 in pants and jeans. I ordered size 4 in olive from Khaki and Blue and they do fit true to size. The waist is low-rise. The material is stiff and even makes some noise when I walk. No stretch whatsoever so size up if you are between sizes or don’t like things digging into you while seated. After sitting for long stretches, the lack of stretch really starts to dig in around the seated section of my lower torso and upper thighs. There were some steam marks upon arrival on the back of the pants but they came out in the wash. All 4 pockets are deep but the pants are a little too tight for me to even be able to use them for much. I’m afraid the size 6 would be too loose in the waist but probably would’ve been less intrusive for my desk job. However, I do like the style and look of these pants with the flaring leg and hip hugging. In conclusion, the stiff/thick material with no stretch will keep this olive pair out my regular line-up but the design and style will draw me, when I’m feeling peppy and can stand up a good portion of the day. Definitely a high quality pair of pants!

No stretch, size up?… These are definitely work pants, not fashion pants. They are a thicker than average material so they should last a long time. Straight leg. They are fairly soft. They don’t have any stretch to them, so beware of sizing. I would recommend sizing up a full size. Mine didn’t fit and that’s before I even washed them. I am 5’4”. They are a little long on me, but not too bad - with shoes on, they won’t drag on the floor. They are well-made.

Drape Beautifully I liked these trousers even more than I thought I would. With white/cream, it is always a concern that they’ll be thin or see-through, which is not the case at all here. The fabric is silky and light, but not chintzy or cheap feeling. The draping is perfect for a lightly pleated front, Creating a clean line that doesn’t hug or accentuate my belly, which is also a concern for me, as I am not as flat in the stomach area. I ordered an XL, with the intention of having a looser fit, I normally wear a 12/14, for reference and am 5’3”. They were comfortable and loose, but not clown baggy. My shoes have a little lift, which is perfect for giving enough extra height so they didn’t drag on the floor, for my smaller height. They have a tailored look, without the tailored price. Love the pockets and extra elastic on back, which allows for more comfortable fit at the waist. I wore them to work and got a ton of compliments. I may need to buy in all colors now.

Office Pants I’m 5/6”, around 165 lb, and have short legs, so I got an XL. Those pants are pretty baggy. They fit okay, but as you can see in my photos, they are a bit long for me. They would be fine if I wore heels, but I no longer have any heels, so I need to send the pants for an alteration. Although the fabric is supposed to be breathable, it doesn’t feel like it’s breathable. Does it feel durable? Yes, but not so breathable. That said, because the pants are pretty baggy, they won’t stick to my legs, so it’s okay. The fabric is thinner, but I got them in black, so it doesn't see-through. The side pockets are good. I can put my big iPhone Pro Max in them, and I like that. I also like the elastic back. It definitely helps keep my pants in place. Those pants are not my favorite, especially since they don’t look very flattering on me. However, those are good as office clothes, so I wear them when I have to go into the office.

Flowy, comfortable, and trendy Size reference: I am 5'6" and 130 lbs. I ordered the medium and am very pleased with the fit. The size chart recommended a small but my measurements are 27/37 with wide hips and I did not want to risk the waist being too tight so I went up to the next size. Description: The wide waistband is elastic with a functional cream drawstring. The material looks like linen with little cream threads mixed in and is not see through. The fabric is better than linen, in my opinion, with a slight silky feel. I washed on regular and dried on delicate and there was not a wrinkle to be seen! Recommendation: For!!!! I love these pants. They are comfortable for travel, nice enough for work, and can be either dressed up with a tucked in shirt or dressed down with a crop top, tank, or tee. They do wrinkle a bit during wear, especially when seated, but still not as bad as true linen. I have no critiques and would absolutely order again.

Interesting Fabric These pants looked super cute on the model. I like the fabric, it is very interesting. Don’t know how to describe it, but it looks nice and feels nice on the skin. The fabric tag says it is Viscose-46%, Polyamide-51%, and Elastane- 3% . The elastic waist makes them easy to slip on and off. They have roomy side pockets. They are well sewn, with straight and true seams. I bought size large, because large seems to fit me best most of the time. But these pants were 3-4 inches too long. I’m 5’2”. If you have a matronly figure these pants are not the best choice. Doesn’t matter if you tuck your shirt/blouse in or wear it out over the waist, all the gathering and bow around the waist makes you look thicker than you already are. For someone young, slim and tall these pants would look very stylish. They may require a little ironing.

Comfortable Paperbag Pants -Great Length I love the color of these pants. It is a neutral army green color with a little amount of texture that matches with so many different tops. The pants are very comfortable and lightweight. I am 5'5"(5'4.5" really) and these are the perfect length for me when wearing flat sandals and worn on my waist. These will be my go-to pants when it gets a little warmer. The elastic band had a plenty of stretch, but the material of the pants did not have any stretch.

Comfortable, but Short [Height: 5'5| 155lbs | Bust 37 | Waist 27 | Hips 37] [Ordered: Medium] I can never tell how long a pair of pants will be on me based on the stock pictures, and often some reviews of those in a similar height [weight can be carried differently]. In this case, these pants are short. The inseam is 27" on these, and I prefer an inseam of 32" or longer (depending on the shoes). However, there is a hem that can be removed to increase the length of the pants. Overall, they are cute and comfortable to wear!

Great Fit and an Interesting, but Classic Color This tie waist, paper bag style design is a flattering high waist design. The brown color is quite an interesting version of the conservative classic. The tie at the waist is attached, so you won't need to worry about losing it. The length hits perfectly (I'm 5'2"), and the high waist hits at the natural waist. the fit is just loose enough to give the desired style, but not so loose that they look sloppy. The pleats give just the right amount of tailoring to the finished style, and the pockets are deep and usable. These are a great choice for a versatile work to running around town look.

Very dressy and classy, while still wide and comfortable. Very nice looking pants, very dressy and professional. I ordered the beige. The word says beige, but the picture looks more like a very light sand, almost white. They are as pictured. I was actually hoping for something a little more beige because I’ve never had much luck with virtually white pants. They all seem to stain so easy. The first time I wear them they come home with an ink spot, coffee stain, or something. These did not! I was very impressed. I wore them to the office on a regular workday. I took the train into the city. Wore the pants all day. When I came home, they looked great. A few wrinkles around the hip flexors, which is normal for an office job. I was eating a breakfast bar at my desk and dropped a few chocolate chips on the pants so I went into the bathroom and just wiped them off with a wet paper towel and the chocolate came right off. It’s great to be able to have such a light colored pair of pants that don’t stain easy. And they’re so easy to care for. They were a little creased out of the package, as you can see by some of the photos. I tried them on right away. I was wearing a black and purple knee brace and I just happened to have on black underwear. Could not see through them at all, which can be a problem with such light colored pants quite often, and they fit perfect. I touched them up with a steam iron and it came out fine. Care instructions. The tag inside the pants list just the care instructions symbols, and I didn’t know what they all meant, but there is a card attached to the pants that translates them. You might want to save the little card, that’s what I did. Care instructions for these pants translate to: Do not bleach, dry clean, Machine wash up to 86°F general cycle. iron up to 230°F tumble dry low. So you can dry clean them and they’re very nice pants. They’re worth it if you want to. But it also says machine wash up to 68°F gentle cycle, tumble dry low, iron up to 230°F. So I threw them in the washer by themselves on delicate, and then threw them in the dryer on low. And I am very impressed. They’re clean and they came out of the dryer virtually wrinkle free. So I’m not gonna bother to test iron them, they don’t need it. Very easy to care for. Sized as stated. I measured myself and bought the one according to the measurement chart, and it fit perfectly. May be too short for average to tall people. The one thing the measurement chart does not say is the inseam. This concerned me because I’m 5’4”” with a 28 inch inseam, and very long waisted. These pants fit me perfectly, I can wear them with flats. They come up to my waist. I measured the extra large inseam is 29 inches so it’s perfect for me. However, as my sister says, for anyone who actually has legs, lol, they may end up, looking like high water pants. But from my short legs, they’re perfect. I’m thrilled with the inseam.

Short and curvy approved! Here are the details: The questions we all have were fully checked off. I’m 5’2, 194 pounds, and built like a box. Seriously, you could pack me in the same rectangle box these pants arrived in. I ordered the lighter beige (think French vanilla) and the darker khaki (not a light tan but a true khaki color). I was going for the quiet luxury or “old money” aesthetic that is timeless and put together. So, how do these pants fair against the luxurious box that is me? Pros: Tummy: I have one. Do these fit mom bellies? Yes. What about the pooch effect? A little. When I sit down there is a little pooching from the fabric but if I add a blazer or sweater then it’s hardly noticeable. There is zero, and I do mean zero, stretch in the waistband so if you’re between sizes I would go up a size. I am a new size 16, from an 18, and it took me a full minute to be okay with a fitted waist since most of my pants have elastic waists. Buttons and belts: The buttons (there are 2) were sewn on well. The zipper didn’t catch and after two flights of stairs to test out whether these pants could handle the demands of being part of my closet the zipper stayed put. Belt loops are wider so while a thin belt would still look cute I would go with a standard thickness in a belt to get that old money look. Length: I could walk barefoot in them as they’re wide legs and while maybe an inch too long they’ll do nicely with heels added to them. I walked outside with a pair of booties on and they did not drag the ground. The hem on the bottom of the pant legs is nicely constructed and gives a tailored look. Thighs: Did not make me look like a can of biscuits about to make an escape. They draped nicely over my luxury edition thighs and didn’t seem to hurt my feelings. Cons: There really aren’t any that would stop me from ordering more of these. They probably require hand washing, gentle detergent, or Woolite. (Photo attached for instructions). Can you see through them? I couldn’t. I could see the line but with shape wear that would solve the issue. Bumps and lumpy bits showing? A little but this is where shape wear comes in. All in all, these are high end quality with an everybody price tag. They’re pants with substance meaning they are true corduroy trousers with a little weight to them. They’ll likely do fine through autumn but I will add a second pair a size up so that I can wear footless tights under for an extra layer of warmth during winter. 10/10 do recommend!

Wonderful casual pants I am 5'7" and these pants reach the ground from my natural waist, if you are shorter you should be prepared to have these hemmed. The waist is fully elastic with an ornamental tie that is sewn to the sides of the pants to tie in the front or back. This fabric is textured and flowy but not necessarily stretchy. This means you can walk normally without the pants bouncing up and down as with a stretchy-loose fabric. The legs are loose all the way from the hips down, so they feel really great to walk around in! Since these pants are so loose, they would pair great with a form-fitting tube or tank top. They could even be dressed up with a crisp white button down.

Classic fit, great for work This is a review of KUNMI Women’s wide leg pants. For reference, I ordered a size small. I am 5’3” and weigh 110-115 pounds. These pants are a perfect fit - I don’t even have to hem them! I ordered them in dark brown. The fabric is light and moves well. There are four small pleats in the front, which I like because it makes the waist fit without having the hip and thigh area look like it’s pulling and too tight. The legs are wide but not too much to be trendy. The back is elastic which makes them really comfortable. There are belt loops, but because I of the tailoring, I won’t need to rely on a belt to keep the waist in place. These pants are a great price for a classy, tailored look at work!

Awesome, fit my large body well Fantastic, I'm very happy with these. Got the large and fits well, For reference I am 40/36/39, 6’ tall and 195 pounds. I measured the unstretched inseam at 28", this is 4 inches longer than LinvMe. I easily put these on and pull up to my crotch. The are skin tight and see-thru. Not near as tight as other brands. The large is like XL in other brands. Thighs fit good on my 22 thigh. leg holes at hem were large enough to get over my size 11 men feet. Hem is 1/2-inch. I usually end up letting the hem out to gain that extra length and then add on another leg finish with either elastic band or stretch lace. easy to find 2 and 3 inch wide elastic bands. Stretch lace easy to find 6 inch wide. Waist is mid waist height, comes to just under my belly button. Rolls down to natural waistline. I roll over the waistband twice for the perfect front rise. Glad I took a chance on these, they are superb value.11-14-24 Bought again, still like, added photos. Even though the length seems short via measurement (I normally want at least a 30 inch inseam for my height) the length stretches during wear and is longer than the flat measurements. This pair I measured the inseam at 27" - fabric stretches so easily I think I stretched it a little to get the 28" measured before.

Love these! Definitely worth the buy! LOVE these pants! Not shiny or super fake looking at all. I would agree with other reviewers that say they run slightly small - I have a big butt & hips and small waist, so it’s always a toss up between a M and a L for me with pants, however, a lot of times the waist will be too big with a large and that was not the case here at all - they fit great and made my waist look snatched. They are a little on the shorter as some other reviewers noted, but the perfect length with heels even though I’m tall (5’8”). As for the smell - there was a bit of the typical faux leather smell, and based on other reviews that said they smelled horrible by the end of the night, I preemptively flipped them inside out, sprayed with Febreeze and ran them through the dryer with a dryer sheet on low heat for about 30 mins. No more smell, and still smelled good at the end of the night - and I live in Miami where it is HUMID to say the least. If you are on the fence, buy them! You won’t be disappointed, especially for the price!

Just meh. These pants were okay. They seemed fine quality for the price. The leather looks nice and isn’t plasticy. The button and zipper seem like they’ll break after a few wears. Definitely not high quality but I didn’t expect that. I bought size small and medium and both fit me. They do have some stretch and the small (4-6) fit nicely so I would say true to size (I’m a size 27/4 regular in most jeans). The medium (6-8) also fit well just a bit wider on the thighs and the waist was fine because it’s stretchy. However 2 things were very disappointing 1. they run very short in my opinion. I am 5’7 (the model is 5’5) and typically wear regular length and these hit a few inches above my ankles so no where close to the length on the model in the photo. 2. They are not as straight leg as the model either (both small and medium hugged from thigh just above knees, and they flare a at the bottom which I did not like at all especially with how short they were. I would not recommend If you’re looking for that 90s straight leg style as advertised and are taller than 5’4 or have any curves.

Good quality, cheap button Good quality for the price. Run a little small. I’m 5’4 135 lbs and I ordered a Medium because small didn’t fit. The button is cheap and feels like it’s about to fall off but these pants do the job.

love! but too short I never write reviews but I figured it might help someone with sizing. I’m about 130 pounds and 5’5. I got a medium in these pants, they fit great and feel good! However they are just a little too short for me. They kinda stop around my ankles which I didn’t like. Other than that theyre a good buy! (I also got the bodysuit from Amazon and I’m obsessed with it!!)

Very flattering and comfy I am extremely impressed with these pants. Many faux leather pants make this annoying swishy noise when rubbed together (aka walking) but these just glide smoothly with no detectable noise. The waist band is stretchy which makes it so comfortable throughout the day - you can eat a large meal in these and not need to unbutton yourself. There is no gap at the waist either, which is difficult to find when you’re more well-endowed. They’re very trendy and I really couldn’t be any happier. For reference, I’m 5’7” about 180lbs and bought a size XL.

Soft, stretchy and confortable I’m 5’4” and normally wear size 18. I ordered a petite size 20W and the stomach had room (a belt will help) but the rest fit perfectly.

I bought a pair of black skinny jeans in the size 12 LONG month ago. They arrived in less than a week. I've been using them for 3 weeks and I'm happy to say I'm glad I bought these pants. I gotta be honest, I was not expecting much, 1/10 jeans I buy actually fit, given how tall and oddly shaped I am. I am almost 6 ft tall, have a relatively thin waist but thick thighs and big calves. Buying pants is a nightmare for me. Or should I say was, because I finally found pants just my size. These fit like a glove. I can comfortably walk in them and although I wish they were a few cm longer, I'm okay with the length and the fit. They seem to be made out of a good thick material too. I would gladly buy another pair.

So Comfortable!!! If you’re thinking about getting them, get them!!! I travel to different job sites and it is the middle of summer and way too hot for jeans! But these are perfect, they’re light weight, stretchy, comfortable, and most importantly have extra pockets!!! I’ve already bought three pairs in three colors, so I highly recommend! Comfy but built like a rectangle These are super comfy and cute, good quality BUT they will only fit if your waist is the same size as your butt. There is a lot of gap in the back. The product pic shows it fitting the model's waist/hips in the front view but it even gaps there on the sides, not just the back. I got a size 6 short; im 5'4" and 135lbs, and this is a typical problem for me with pants. They will fit great around my thighs and butt (which these do + perfect length) but not my waist (or reverse so I can't size down) :/

Great I am 5’6 and 170 lbs. these pants are really comfy, the flex band in the top is a game changer. The pockets are nice and real , and the cargo pockets on the side are good for putting your phone or keys in. These are thick and would be good for outside jobs or the winter. I normally wear 14 or 16 . I got these in 16 plus and they are loose on me and I do need a belt but I feel 14 might be too tight. As you can see in the pictures they are the correct length when standing however these ride up SO much when you sit down and they become extreme high waters when sitting. I put them in the dryer and there was no shrinkage. I would like another pair in long.

They are flared Lee has a perfect fit for me - Spandex is great and gives a snug fit without cutting off my blood circulation like some jeans do. That being said, these jeans don't fit from the knees down. They flare all right - like jeans I had in the 60s. Which wouldn't be so bad - if they were not so long. See the tag - It says 16 SHORT. I bought these in a "Short" because I wanted to replace a pair of jeans that were too long and frayed and tore at the bottom. These are even worse. In the picture of the jeans on me, I DO have shoes on. Ankles? Yes, those, too. I love LEE, but I wish they really could make a pair of jeans that are short. Not all of us are 6' and over. Not going to return them, but I am disappointed. BTW, they have been washed and did not draw up. But I may try again.

Almost perfect fit, love them I’m 5’9” and ~180lbs. Love the weight of the material, waist height in front, fit throughout the leg, and the diameter of the flare. The waist height in the back is a little bit low and dips a tiny bit too much when sitting. I’m still happy with them and would buy them again

Great for curvy pear/hourglass shaped people! Let me start this by saying I wasn't expecting to fit in these as well as I do. I bought these knowing I could pass them down to my sister if I didn't fit in them as I wanted to. Jeans are hard for me to find that fit well, and I usually opt for trousers or leggings instead. Day 1 of their arrival I wore them to work (see picture) and I had no issues with them. They proved to withstand all the bending over, on my knees and fast movement through an 8 hour shift. They didn't make me uncomfortable, they didn't cause any chafing, or didn't unzip and expose my underwear like I've also experienced with jeans. I got a few compliments from coworkers and customers on them, as they usually dont see me in jeans. The flare on them is adorable, however I will say as im 5'3, I did have to roll them so they didn't drag while at work. So shorties, be aware of the flare drag! Color is beautiful I love the wash. I didn't notice any weird smell from them. And they're very thick, as I stated before I'm constantly on my knees at work and don't feel much stretch from them. I attribute that to their mostly cotton makeup, it's not cheap polyester that easily tears under pressure.

Awesome 1970’s Retro look Returned the bright blue day color, that cost 3 times as much but kept the elevated retro color that was a steal at $16.00 and took a chance on boiling them for 30 minutes on stove top and drying on sanitize cycle for maximum shrinkage. See photo laying on top of retuned pair, the length shrunk almost two inches. Still need to wear with heals, so not for everyday at almost 70 years old, but they make me feel young again. I am 5’3” tall with longer legs and shorter torso. I bought the size 12 short which did shrink all over so if you are big from the knees up these may not be for you. They were scary long looking out of the package but 99% cotton does shrink nicely. If I manage to shrink them a little more they could rate 6 stars!

Check the length before ordering I absolutely love these jeans. I have missed bell bottom jeans and these brought back a lot of memories. I’m size 10/12 jeans, 5 month postpartum and these hold me in all the right places. I’m 5’8 and 177 lbs. The color was exactly like shown in the photos. The only thing is… make sure to check out the length in sizing. I usually wear a regular length but if you look at the length for regular in these jeans it says 33. Longs are 35 and shorts are 31. I should have looked at that more closely and ordered short. That’s my error. The regular were a bit long on me. Next time I will order the short. Lastly, I apologize for the smudgy mirror pics. My littles love to play with that mirror and it always gets smudgy.

Good Flares I was drawn to these jeans because it said they were mid-rise (and on sale). I can’t do high waisted jeans and I have struggled to find mid-rise flares at a reasonable price. After reading the reviews I wasn’t sure if I would like them. Many sizes were sold out because they were on sale. I ordered an 8 regular in a blue wash and an 8 short in a grey wash. As you can see from the pictures, the regular comes up to my belly button. I’m only 5’ 3.5” and weigh about 135 but still. I was disappointed. They were definitely long, but I thought maybe with a heel I could get away with it. Then I tried on the short. They didn’t come up as high (although still a little higher than I wished). And the length was perfect. Some people said their size was a little loose. These are definitely not loose on me. I’m actually hoping they might loosen up a bit after I wear them. I’m returning the 8 regular and plan to keep the 8 short. I haven’t worn them other than to try on so I can’t yet speak for how they will hold their shape after all day. They do have some stretch to them; enough to make them conformable and not stiff.

Good material, odd fit for me. These jeans are quality denim and a great length for taller people. I'm 5'8", 175lbs, and I ordered the "Blue Veil Dx" color option in size 10L. The hem hits the floor and since I usually don't wear flat shoes, this isn't a problem. The price is unbeatable. I haven't owned a pair of Lee jeans in many years. Unfortunately, now I remember why: The fly area always seems to be crooked or slightly off center on Lee jeans. Every pair I've ever tried over the years has had this problem. After wearing for a while, walking around, etc, the fly pulls more to one side and looks crooked. This isn't the case on any other brand of jeans I've tried, so it must be some flaw with this brand. It's weird. But hey, they're nice jeans otherwise, and many people seem to like them. This brand just isn't for me.

After wearing for a while, walking around, etc, the fly pulls more to one side and looks crooked. This isn't the case on any other brand of jeans I've tried, so it must be some flaw with this brand. It's weird. But hey, they're nice jeans otherwise, and many people seem to like them. This brand just isn't for me.

Should have read the size chart better If you usually wear Levi's, your usual size will be too skinny and too long here. I wear a size 12 short in Levi's, and they're perfect. These Lee's are too tight in the waist, and ridiculously long. The inseam for a SHORT is 30 inches! I'm 5'4" ish, and I have to stand all the way on my tiptoes to get these to not brush the floor. The waist is about 15.5" across, which is super unflattering and uncomfortable on my post-baby belly. If you are more apple shaped and less hourglass shaped like I am right now, I suggest you size based on the waist measurement, not anything else. That all said, I gave these a 4 because they are excellent quality. They are soft and stretchy, and if it weren't for my own user error and some odd sizing, they would be great!

Highly recommend!

Best Affordable Flare Jeans! I love these jeans! I am 5 foot 9 and weigh 150 pounds. I do not have much of a waist (hello rectangle body shape!) so I have to be quite careful wearing jeans that are too low or too high of a rise. These are "mid rise" but are almost high rise on me due to my short waist. They hit at the perfect spot! The length is great too. The regular length was perfect which is RARE. I almost always have to buy long/tall but not these! I could even pair a small heeled boot to work. The dark wash is a great color. It is dark enough to dress up if your work allows. I obviously have only worn them once so I'm not sure how long they will last, but they seem just as durable as brands like Old Navy. I'm really pleased with this pair of denim!

Shockingly well-fitting and comfortable I was not expecting such a great fit -- perfect, really -- of these on me. I'm 5'5", about 142lbs, typically size 4-6 in most major brands, and the size 6 fits perfectly. No waist gap, no uncomfortable snugness anywhere, hits right at my waist; even the length works for me. The wide legs are pretty wide, bordering on baggy; I think I'm going to get them tailored slightly so they're less palazzo-like and drape more similarly to Levi's Ribcage Wide Leg (which I love the width of the legs, but Levi's just generally do not fit me well in the waist, butt, and thigh areas). These have a bit of stretch as well. I love them. I got them in another color, and have another on the way -- they're that great!

Great jean but pants fit differently depending on color which is frustrating Buying jeans is always a trial. When I got these jeans I didn't know what to expect because previous Lee's didn't fit well. As soon as I tried these jeans on I was in love. I'm 5'9 and weight 285. I have an hourglass figure (wide hips and shoulders with a narrower waist). Most pants don't fit my thighs if they fit my hips well but these hugged everything in a flattering and comfortable way. (For this pair of pants I'm referring to the majestic style pants here). I need a pair of work pants so I decided I would order the exact same pair in black because I loved them so much. What a disappointment! They're tight on the waist and when I bend over I can feel them digging into my stomach. There's no way I would be able to sit in these for any extended period. They're literally just the tiniest bit smaller than the first pair I bought and that makes them unwearable for me. The black pants are also really long even though I got the regular length. I got the majestic in the long length (which were too long but I didn't mind) and the black pants in the normal length, but the black pants were nearly the same length! Super disappointed and returning. I would buy the majestic pair again though.

Otherwise, these jeans are really nice. Solid pair of pants These are not your "sexy mama date night" jeans, they are however your "mama's got to go to work, move around and be comfortable" jeans. They are made of durable jean material while still feeling stretchy enough to bend, squat, and lift with no problems. The waist band has an added elastic feature so after lunchtime you don't feel like you're going to explode. I'm 5' 1" 130 pounds and got the 8 long. I'm not tall but I find "short" pants to be a little too short for me. These are a little baggy in the leg and extra roomy at the end of the day but honestly I love them. They don't feel like they're going to rip or tear any time soon, not paper thin, and no "decorative" holes. Work appropriate and practical while still being cute. I got the "Seattle" style, not sure what the other styles are like, but definitely interested in trying them out. -a picky pants wearer

Inseam measurement is wrong for “short” cut I was excited to try the Motion Flex because I like the softer, stretchier material. I am a perfect fit for the Lee’s 8 Short mid rise regular fit straight jeans, which have a 29” inseam. I thought these ones would have the same length, but even after confirming that I did in fact get the 8 Short midrise regular fit straight (meaning, exactly the same size and style) in the Flex-Motion version, they were way too long. I laid them out with my regular pair and measured only to find that the Flex-Motion pair has a 31” inseam instead of a 29” - a whole 2” difference. I expect that kind of difference on bootcut but not on straight. Really disappointing.

I’m not drowning in excess fabric which I appreciate!! Get these you won’t be disappointed!!

Excellent Pants, but be aware of possible defects! Let me start first by giving measurements for reference: 5'3" , 137lbs , 29" waist, 38" hips, 27" inseam That being said, I have tried all 6/8 short/petite relaxed/curvy pants on Amazon (Rafaella Gaberdine curvy, Signature Levi curvy Straight, Gloria Vanderbilt Amanda & GV Curvy Skinny, Dickies Curvy Straight, Lee Relaxed Side Elastic Straight, etc) and ALL of them either had a gap in the waist, were too tight across the rear, or were simply just too long.. however THESE pants fit great and had neither issue! I finally found a pants that not only fit, but are modest for work-- flattering but not figure hugging. I bought these in size 6 petite in colors (pictured from left to right) Indigo Wash (like a blue jean color/type fabric) Black Imperial Blue Mini Glenplaid (Black on light grey- NO BLUE in this, which i actually prefer) Cypress (Dark Olive Green) Jet Black/White Rockhill Plaid Falcon (pinkish brown) Upon close inspection, I noticed 1 of the 6 pairs I had purchased (Falcon) were defective. On the back leg, it appears as if holes were accidentally cut in the pants and then attempted to be "patched" by resewing it back together. (see pictures) Very disappointed that Amazon would allow this and have reordered/requested a return on this one pair. Because of the issue and trouble, I am deducting 1 star.

too long, (way) too wide at the hips What I love about these jeans is that they truly are high-rise, straight-legged, and fit me well around my waist. I need to order these in more colours! - Update - I did Update in September 2024: Well, I did buy them in two more colours. And I'm not particularly glad I did. The worst were the Ecru ones. A total disaster. Otherwise, I wouldn't have knocked off a star, but if it weren't for the blue ones, I might have knocked off two! I think my ecru pair is defective. All three pairs I ordered were 12 Long. In the first two new pictures, I aligned the crotch of my original blue pair with the ecru. The latter are lower in the waist (not a plus, for me) and so much shorter in the legs I would think they weren't "Long", although it is written on the labels. They barely touch the top of my foot, which definitely doesn't work with this cut! There is a lot more stretch in the ecru ones, which makes them sort of clingy. On the positive side, they are not see-through, in the sense that they are opaque. But you can see the outline of every bump and dimple on my knees. and the fabric clings so much that the ankles flare out, although when I laid them on the other jeans, I could see they are also cut that way. The blue ones have a slight taper, which makes them look straight on the body, since most people have larger calves than ankles. The ecru ones actually flare out a tiny bit at the very bottom, and it looks terrible on (you'll have to trust me, I have not taken a photo and have already gotten rid of the jeans!). Sadly for me, I live overseas and sending the jeans back would probably cost as mush as the price of the jeans, so I have given them to a much shorter woman in hopes she can cut them off above the weird little flare and get some wear out of them. I also think one leg is longer than the other. It looked like it on, and it looked ike it when I laid them on the bed. They were definitely new and had all the tags on, but there was a small brown spot on the back on one leg. I think it will wash out, but I didn't bother with it because the jeans were so hopeless. I think it might be fly p**. I also bought the Washed Black. I have kept them and I think I will wear them. Thankfully, since they cost $48 US, which is quite a bit for an ordinary pair of jeans. They are also smaller than my blue pair, but I can wear them. They are longer than the ecru pair, a smidge shorter than the blue. The colour is nice and they fit better than Ecru (I forgot to mention that the ecru pair comes up higher in the front than the back!!!). As they should be, they are higher in the back than the front, where they are about two inches below my belly button (of course, I am tall, but then again, these are supposed to be long). They are also stretchier, more synthetic than the blue ones, but less than the ecru ones (synthetic jeans are not a good thing in my book). But they are okay. I think Anchor two years ago rates a 4.5, Washed Black in 2024 rates a 3.5 or maybe 4, and the Ecru ones I received on the same day rate a solid 2!!!!!!!!!!! On the plus side: These are my jeans. They fit very well and are very comfortable. Most of the time, Lee jeans in size 12 Long are as good as it gets on me. I need a 34" inseam and these, hmm, they might be 33", but they are okay. (I'm 5'11") Although I prefer a high rise, and these are mid rise, they are okay and do not give me a muffin top. They are straight leg, but pretty narrow. Just that little bit of extra fabric at the ankles so you can tell they're not jeggings. Actually, they're nothing like jeggings, because the fabric is fairly thick, although nice and soft and doesn't stifle you like plastic (it's 30 degrees Celsius/86 fahrenheit where I am right now, so that's important). The colour is Anchor and pulling it out of the package, I thought it was darker than expected. Then I realized they have those fake wear marks down the front. The faded part goes to below the knee, so that it looks like I have really long thighs and short calves, whereas I am the exact opposite. But it's not a deal breaker. And there is a little whiskering on top, although not too much. I would prefer an even colour all over (hey, I'm on the far side of 55) but it isn't too bad. I really like these quite a bit, so I am going to go back and look at other colours. Hope this helps!

Perfectly simple, not see-through, white jeans Was looking for a plain straight leg white jean, no holes/no shredding, no flare, no wide leg, or skinny, not cropped or ankle length (I am 5'9.5" with 33" inseam), just straight leg jeans that was not see-through. These are defective and should have been sold as rejects I am 5’8 145 lbs and ordered the Anchor color in size 8, long. Received order promptly. Pants looked like size ten, regular. They are baggy and inseam is only 31.5…. 31.5 is not a tall. Thinking I could tolerate these, I removed the tags. Now on wearing them, I noticed the stitching on the left leg is uneven, causing the pant leg to turn uncomfortably. This is uncomfortable and irritating. These do not meet my expectation of quality. Today I found a review showing how much shorter the Anchor color are than a darker color, another about misaligned leg seam, and many saying they suspect these as seconds. These should have been disclosed as defective rejects. I will not order jeans from Amazon again. ugh now I see rear pockets don’t match. These are new pants received today that I have not washed

Comfortable and durable I'm 5'5" and 150lbs and the size 10(regular) fit but they are a little loose on me. This isn't a sizing issue I've simply ordered my old size even though I lost weight. I was thinking I hadn't gone down a full size yet but I should have trusted the size chart which indicated I should be in a size 8. That said these are the same comfortable styling and quality I've come to expect from Lee. Five stars because these are great jeans for less than $35.

I received higher waisted pant that has a zipper with a double button closure... since higher waisted, Waist sizing is fitted... if you have a defined waist no problem but if have no waist definition (or a short torso) go a size up or if you are in between sizes go with the larger of the two sizes... I wear size 8, am 5’2 with a short torso but have a slightly defined waist so I am ok wearing my normal size (but is snug) and the pants are perfect length to wear with heals (think business casual)... I have thick thighs so the pants a slightly fitted in the thigh but still flattering. the pant is very flattering on your bootie, in the advertisement you can see the model is slender and flatters, still is true for us full bottom beauties! material is kinda rough... Again I have thick thighs, so I am concerned the material will make noise as I walk... (oh dear, the sound of 70’s corduroy pants is popping in my head... best way to describe the sound these pants make) For the price, worth keeping to wash and wear... worse case I can give pants to one of my taller friends that has thinner thighs lol

Extremely flattering and comfortable Levi’s gets it right with curvy clothes. I have the worst time finding pants that fit my thighs and butt but also my waist. For context, I’m about 5’7”, 250 lbs, with ridiculously thunderous thighs and booty. These pants fit and flattered with no significant waist gap. I will be getting more styles.

favorite BUT THEY SHRANK So comfortable! Thick material. Stretchy waistband without buttons. They feel like thick luxurious lounge pants but they look perfect for the office. I worried about them making me look wide since I am short and size 12, but they are the perfect amount of wide. I’m 5’2” and ordered the short. They are perfect to wear with a heel. Length shrinks A LOT I am not a tall person by any stretch of the imagination -- I'm 5'3" on a good day. I ALWAYS have to order short/petite pants or hem up regular pants by several inches. Having seen the reviews on these saying that they shrink, I went ahead and bought the regular length ones. Goodness, am I happy I erred on the side of caution. They started with a 29.5" inseam (see photo). After two or three wash and dries, they've shrunk 2" up to a 27.5" inseam. They're nearly too short for even me, but they'll work. That aside, they fabric is thick, soft, and high quality. It's perfect for work, lays flat, doesn't wrinkle, and it gives a nice silhouette for curvy hips/bum. They're also pull-ons for extra comfort, and they have front pockets. I really do like them and I'll be getting several more pairs for work, but I feel bad for anyone taller than me.

Nice, but not for my body type These pants are the “pull-on formal” type with no fly/ button, a thick elastic waist and detailing like overstitched pockets and belt hooks to make them work-appropriate. The fabric is of pretty decent thickness but if I kept them I’d probably need shapewear to cover the vpl and lumps and bumps. I ordered them in long, as the current fashion for flood pants doesn’t suit me, and they would be just about right with a modest heel (I’m 5’6” and somewhat short waisted). I have a small waist and big booty, but I was hoping they would work for me due to the stretch and elastic waist but they gaped really badly at the back. If I went down a size they would probably fit better at the waist but I wouldn’t want them to be any tighter on the hips for work pants. I returned them because of this but think the quality and style are good.

Very comfortable! Excellent quality Not sure why these pants got low reviews. But these Lee pants are legit comfortable. My measurements: waist 36 inch, hip 44 inch. Material is a lil on the heavier/thicker side but soft and quality. Good stretch throughout. Try it out! I ended ordering more colors after this one.

Pleasantly Surprised I had zero expectations for these, but I had them recommended by an Amazon influencer and decided to give them a try! First of all, I sized UP. I wanted a super baggy look, so after reading reviews I went with a Large instead of my usual Small. I’m glad I did! They’re super high rise, baggy in the legs but tighter in the waist. However, I can still pull them down lower and wear them slouchy. There is no stretch in the fabric, FYI. Length is long on me at 5’2” but I kinda like it. Print and fabric quality is actually really nice. But please note: they smell AWFUL right out of the bag. Like, truly so bad. Still keeping them! :)

Love These I was really nervous about buying such a baggy style jean with my body type. I’m 5’0” and about 155lbs, with a curvy, athletic build. Usually, baggy clothes are not my friend, but I have always wanted to pull off the look so I bought these before a vacation! Wasn’t sure during my first try-on, but they became my fav jeans on my trip. I’m getting used to styling them and pairing them with different tops, but really happy with my purchase. They’re a gorgeous color, feel really high quality, and fit just how I wanted to — although I did size up. My one complaint is that they’re a little loose in the waist, but that’s on me because I could’ve purchased a smaller size and opted to go with the oversized fit. I also DO have to wear platform shoes in an effort to keep these off the ground and they still barely touch when I do. All in all, great jeans!!! Definitely a closet staple for me now.

Sizing is weird Update - I hot the 27 and they are literally perfect. I bought both colors but the one in the photo is the black. If you have no hips and are slender like a banana order your usual size. Weight 132 on a good day.

Great Jeans, Wrong Size. Beware “Amazon Size Recommendation!” Update: Got the right size (25), attached a new image of the fit above. Original review: I couldn’t decide between getting a size 27 or 28, and Amazon’s recommended size was 28. I got the 27 because it would arrive in time for my outing, and it still ended up being way too big (I prefer skinny/bootcut jeans to fit tightly), so the Amazon recc would’ve been way off. The Amazon size recc is based on just your height and weight, not all your measurements, and it messed up another order of Levi’s jeans I got, so I would trust the size guide instead! Other than that, the jeans are amazing. They’re comfortable levels of stretch while still being thick and giving quality, the deep blue color in combination with the contrasting thread in the “Casting Shadows” color is just 🧑‍🍳💋 chefs kiss perfect. Also, in the last image you can see it has the Levis stamp and tag so it’s definitely official, at least my variant! It also comes from the official Levis store and Amazon is strict against impersonators so I don’t think this is fake like that one reviewer claimed. Some Levi’s jeans have a tag that’s just red, without the logo, I own some of those. So yes! Buy it, but make sure to order the right size! My measurements: Height: 5’7 Weight: 120lbs Natural waist: 27 in Belly button: 29 in Hip: 33 in Butt: 37 in

WTH? I purchased a pair of these 725 Levi's in a size too small (recently lost a lot of weight)so I'm confused on what size I wear. I first ordered a size 6 which I was not able to pull above my thighs. Then I ordered my stand by, a size 10 reg. I'm 5'3" and weigh 143lbs. Too big and way too long. My next choice was an 8 short, my photo show how they arrived (nice, huh?) These pants aren't close to "high waisted", and they almost look like I'm wearing early 2000's jeans but with a cropped flare. They are MID RISE w/ a flare and close to being long capris. Bottom line.... Shop for clothes in person!!!

I was hoping that these would be great lounge pants, maybe even comfy enough to sleep in. The fabric is not at all what I expected, and it definitely would not be described as soft. The first thing that struck me when I opened this package was a label stating that these were waterproof. What the heck? I somehow missed that in the description, and it was certainly not something I needed. They are also SPF, according to the tags, and that is ok by me, but also not something I was seeking. These pants are a bit too short on me (I'm 5'6") even without shoes. There is a slit on the side, but it is unnecessary for ease of dressing because the fit is very loose. In fact, the legs are too baggy for my taste. I think the relaxed fit would be ok if the fabric was more like soft, lounge pants, but it doesn't work for me in this plasticky feeling fabric. The pants have a high waist, and a drawstring as well as an elasticized waist. The waist fits me fine, without cinching the drawstring, but I suspect that some might find it to be snug. There are two deep pockets, a great idea. This goes in the machine for wash and dry, and that is one thing this odd fabric handles just fine. These pants were not at all what I was expecting in fit, style or feel. If, however, you are looking for SPF pants that are waterproof and loose fitting for outdoors activities, these might be just right for you.

Petite/short buyers beware: Inseam measurement I am 5’2 inches tall with a 27.5 inch inseam (crotch to floor). I ordered size M because sizing chart referenced inseam at 27in. When I tried on the pants, they dragged the floor (even with heels). Confused, I check listing (again) and measure myself (see pics) to discover the inseam measurement is 29.4 inches. Color is a very nice (classic) deep navy blue, the high rise waist measurement is accurate and very comfortable. Well positioned pleating allows for classic and traditional trouser fit (not too full in buttock/hip). 100% poly fabric is cheap, very lightweight and retains static electricity (clinging to legs and attracting lint and hair). It is disappointing that the mfg and vendor failed to communicate and ensure sizing chart reflected accurate information to the end user consumer. The trousers are low priced reflective of their low value and unworthy of the time and money it would take to hem. I will be returning.

Poorly made. Faux zipper off centered to the right. These pants are a cheap quality version of slacksweats. They fit pretty well, and are comfortable on, but as soon as I put them on, I immediately noticed that the waist is not centered. You can see in my pictures that it’s off to the right, which is even more noticeable when they’re on. It’s a definite no for me because that’s the only thing I can focus on now; just how much off center they look. So for them to look right with the faux zipper centered on the tummy, I’d have to twist them to one side, which throws off the whole fit of the pants due to the other seams and pockets. Looking back at the other reviews, I can see that many of the other colors are cut/sewn the same way, but may not be as noticeable in a darker color. Bottom line, just not worth it when there are so many other better options with more flowy material that’s not this weird rough waffle weave anyway. I prefer a more sophisticated look, but wanted a pull-on comfortable dress slack. This is definitely not it, even if the seams did line up. FYI, I am 5’6, 190lbs, wear a size medium pant, and these were true to size in a medium.
