Lightweight, Comfortable, Breathable & Versatile Palazzo Pants I ordered these because I’ve got about 10 pairs of Libin’s cargo joggers, which I love, and these palazzos looked fun. (For reference, I’m 5’2”, 36 inches at the waist, 41” bum). After trying them on here’s my impression: PROS: 1. 100% polyester, so they won’t shrink. The outside is a textured waffle weave that is 100% breathable, but the inside is smooth and soft. 2. SIzing is Spot on. I went by both my usual Libin size (XL) and their sizing chart (36 in waist), and super happy I did. These don’t pinch at the waist, but the top part of the pants aren’t excessively baggy. 3. Versatile and cute. I was just going to try them on this morning, thinking that they’d be great for church, but then I ended up wearing them on today’s errands. Considerations: 1. These are Palazzo pants, meaning there’s a lot of material in the leg. If you’re like me (a bit chunky, significant bum) you’ll want to wear these with a fitted top, to maintain a feminine silhouette. 2. The 27 inch inseam just works on my 5”2” frame (but I have a short waist and long legs for my height). Shoes keep them just off the ground. Anyway, I’m a fan.

Short curvy girl approved I am 5’2”, 142 pounds and very pear shaped. I ordered a medium. Length is perfect! I didn’t want cropped pants, so these are the perfect petite length. I was worried about these fitting weird around my waist- I deal with IBS bloat and am horribly self conscious about waist lines. The fit is comfy given my large hips, thick thighs, and will be comfy and flattering around my IBS flare ups. Fabric is actually flattering! I tested a bunch of linen pants at various shops recently and didn’t like the thin, see through linen (again, sensitive about my big hips.). These are a little thicker, and while the fabric is a little rougher (I suspect they’ll soften in the wash with some fabric softener), they’ll wear much better. And, the pricing here really can’t be beat for the quality. I ordered the striped navy blue. Navy is super dark (my lighting in pics is bad, sorry!), so the pinstripes pop nicely. I’m considering ordering another pair before my upcoming trip! These will travel so well, I can already tell. In a nutshell- yes, buy them.

Finally found my linen pants! The pants I received are 50% linen / 50% cotton, so there may be some shrinkage. Keeping this in mind, I had ordered a large and a medium. I went with the medium because I felt like I swam in the large and if I wash in cold and line dry, shrinkage should be at a minimum. Great value for your money, not super soft, but not uncomfortably scratchy either. I’m almost 5’2” and 175lbs, plus I’ve got hips! For those fun size gals whose hips don’t lie, I think depending on the look you want, either size would look great on you!

Size up! Love these!! I got them in a large but am usually a medium, and the large fits perfectly. I’m 5’3, 140 lbs and usually size 6 or 28/28 in bottoms. One thing to note — I wear these high waisted unlike the model pictures where it’s at their hips. Wearing them high waisted, they fit perfectly around my waist and hips and are also the perfect length, usually pants are too long on me but these are the perfect full length. I also love that they are 100% cotton! The material is soft and light but not thin or staticky. It’s just 1 cotton layer so it’s not super thick but I feel like it would be warm enough for any inside wear.

Waist is tight, they run small and short, they shrink! I'm very disappointed in these pants, as they are a cute design and looked like they'd be comfortable. But I can't really wear them after washing them. For reference, I am 5'7" and 133#. I ordered size Small. My photos were taken BEFORE I washed them... 1) There are many loose threads hanging off these pants, due to low quality control. 2) The elastic waist is very tight. Too tight for a supposedly comfy pajama/lounge pant, especially one worn mid-rise. I think buyers should size-UP. 3) These pants are 100% Cotton, and definitely shrank after the first wash in cold water and tumble dry on Low heat. Now they feel even smaller and are too short for me. In general, I recommend that buyers SIZE-UP by 1-2 sizes, and either hang dry or dry on low heat for very short time. Too bad the quality is low, because the navy/white gingham design and "boxer" look is cute.

I accidentally ordered these in the medium (I meant to select L but it changed when I opted for a different colour) so I didn't expect these to fit. These would be great for summer nightwear, they're very lightwight, however, they're also quite opaque (and not from stretch). The legs are quite long and wide, which I like for sleepwear but I'm 5'7" - the length was perfect on me, shorter wearers might be tripping on them. I got the black distressed wash, and I'm obsessed with the edgy, cool look it gives the jeans. The seam detailing down the front of the legs is another nice added detail. The Medium fit me great, though maybe just a tad loose. If you're in between sizes, you may want to consider sizing down for a more snug fit. For reference, I'm a size 8, 5'6", and 145 lbs. I will say the length is a tad long on me, but I think that's intentional so you can pair them with heels or boots. Overall, these wide leg jeans are super flattering, stylish, and comfy. I can already tell I'll be wearing them a ton thanks to that perfectly wide leg silhouette.

Nice, comfortable, sized down one and fit was perfect I was looking for a mid to high wasted pair of jeans that are on the thinner/looser side and can be worn throughout the year, even in the warmer season. And, a pair that aren't too distressed because there are times when that look just isn't appropriate. These meet all my criteria. There is nothing super special about the way they look, but they can be dressed up or down and are extremely comfortable. I am usually a small or size 26 in jeans (5'4" 118 lbs) but had read these run large so I got the extra small and they fit perfectly. They are very stretchy so I believe will accommodate any body shape, but they don't bag or bunch anywhere on me. I often have that problem with jeans because my hips are so narrow but not with these. These are good everyday jeans and I'd recommend them.

Good quality, fits well... I ordered the Lookbook Denim Jeans for Women in Pecan Brown Size XXL. To start, these are not high waisted pants. They are hip huggers, meaning they sit low on your waist and will not restrict your tummy if you have a few extra pounds. Furthermore, they state they jeans are stretch. They have a slight stretch to them, but they are not what is normally considered a stretch pant. Finally, these are not baggy on the average girl. They are boot cut/flare leg, but not baggy.With all of that said, I am 5 foot, 5 inches tall, 275 pounds, wear an 18/20 USA size and a 2x/3x top. My waist measurement at belly button level is 46 inches. My upper thighs and calves are thicker than most. I was concerned these pants would not fit, however, the Lookbook Denim Jeans for Women in Pecan Brown Size XXL fit comfortably. I will say there is a bit of tightness in the knee area, but I tend to have this issue due to my thick legs. I am not use to a hip hugger pant, so was genuinely surprised that these pants fit tight, but comfortably, as they sit under my "jelly roll", so there is none of that constrictive feeling on your stomach. I also like my pants longer and I found these to fit perfectly, touching the ground, giving them room to shrink a bit.Overall, the Lookbook Denim Jeans for Women in Pecan Brown in Size XXL are well made, the denim is thicker than most and I absolutely love the color. The fit is surprisingly good and the hip hugger style makes them extremely comfortable. So if you are a curvy girl like me, check these out. Five stars.

Stylish & Comfy Wide-Leg Jeans. I got these jeans for my daughter & they look adorable on her. She especially loves how stretchy & super comfy they are. Sizing might run a bit small. She’s a size 6, which the description equates to a "small," but I decided to order up 1 size as a precaution & opted for the "medium." Thankfully, that was the right decision. They fit her perfectly. The high waist is flattering. They’re fitted on the hips & backside without being too snug. Although they’re wide-leg, they don’t look baggy, maintaining a stylish shape. These jeans have a sturdy zipper, metal button, & back pockets, giving them that classic denim jeans look. However, the material is thinner than your average denim & even thinner than other stretchy denim I’m used to. Despite the thinner fabric, you can’t see through them in the white color, so it’s not a major concern. My daughter is 5'7", & the bottom hem lands just beneath her heel, which makes them look long with flat sandals. However, they pair well with shoes that have a raised sole or heel, like boots & wedges. She did mention they’re a bit long below the zipper, causing a slight bunching when she sits. Fortunately, this improved after washing, as I expected since stretchy denim tends to draw up a bit. Although the care instructions advise against tumble drying, I did toss them in the dryer on a low, wrinkle-free setting & they held up just fine. Overall, these jeans are stylish & fit great. I’d recommend getting precise measurements & comparing them to the size chart, as you may need to size up. The denim is decent quality, though I’m a bit concerned about how long they’ll last given the thinner material. But mostly, she’s thrilled with how comfortable they are.

Super high waist but very little shape I’m 5’6”, 129 lbs., and wearing the medium (8-10) in my photo. I’m normally a consistent size 4 in US pants/jeans but I’ve learned to order up from Amazon. I’m happy with the fit of the medium. The fabric is lightweight and feels like a thick chiffon. The pants have a button/zipper fly and deep hip pockets but the back pockets are fake. There are belt loops on the waistband but the back is elastic so the belt is optional. The color is a deep navy. The legs are long enough that I have to wear at least a short heel to keep them from dragging the ground. What I like: 1. The super high waist and wide leg style. 2. Lightweight and flowy, good for summer work pants. 3. Real (and deep) pockets. What I don’t like: 1. The material has a nylon pantyhose feel, just scratchy enough to be noticeable. 2. Profile of the pants is kind of shapeless. The fabric just hangs and does very little to flatter the figure.

Soft and Comfy Fabric – Very Casual – Enough Stretch for Freedom to Move About My wife loves the twill fabric of her LookbookStore Womens Jeans. It is the first feature of the jeans about which she commented. It is a medium-weight denim that does not heat her up. The hand is very soft with a very casual pre-washed comfort. Sadly, these amazing jeans are 2" shorter than stated These jeans would be perfect if they were actually a 26" inseam, but they're just over 24." Seriously? It makes these otherwise amazing jeans (their fit, feel, look) the dorkiest high-water length that can't be pulled off with any kind of footwear - capri wide-leg jeans are not cute. The fabric is super soft, has stretch but not enough for them to stretch out and start slipping because of the flat waistband, and the style would have been exactly what I was looking for - if they had the extra 2" they were supposed to. The pockets are super cute, and the rise is 12" which, for me and my long torso, is a great/the perfect height. Going by the size chart, I technically should have taken the S, but I sized up and am glad I did. They're very stretchy but I think the S would have been too tight and not have the look I wanted. Not that I can wear them. I purchased these through Vine for a discount so I'm unable to return, or they'd go straight back. 3 stars from me is an average/the-item-is-as-described rating - these are not as described and makes them unwearable, literally, so they can't get a better rating. The fabric and fit are why they got 2. All photos are of the size M jeans.

Run slightly small, well made, lighter weight demin. I am 5'7", 164lbs, with a 31" waist. I normally wear between a size 10 and 12 in jeans so I got these in a size L. They were a bit tight around the waist but fit. I would recommend sizing up. They are a unique look and are a true barrel jean. The construction of them was high quality. The material is more of a lightweight demin and doesn't feel as high quality as other jeans I own. The material does have some stretch to it.After trying them on I am not a huge fan of this style, so I won't be keeping them. However, if you are looking for a pair of barrel jeans this is a nice budget option. They did not shrink after washing, the pockets are a good size, and they were well made. I just wasn't a fan of the lightweight denim or the style.

Cute jeans, so-so quality The seams on these jeans give them a cute, modern touch and stylistically go great with the bell bottoms. They're really stretchy, which is also a bonus. I ordered them in "darkness blue." I'm 5'4", 157 pounds and the large fits comfortably. Most of my height is in my torso (read: I have short legs) so I had to wear really high shoes for these not to drag the ground. I don't consider that a drawback, though. Bell bottoms should never be worn as floods, in my opinion. Finally, it's incredibly hard to button and unbutton these jeans -- they just aren't terribly well made. For the price point, maybe that's to be expected, but I took off a star for it anyway.

Nice fitting, middle-weight jeans. Perfect length for someone 5'4"! These are a well made pair of jeans that surprised me with a little more flare than I had anticipated. I thought it was a small flare, but when I got them I found they are full fledged bell-bottoms. That is fine with me. I loved bell-bottoms when I was growing up (yes, I am that old, lol) but my mom mostly did not allow me to have them, so I was thrilled the past 15 years or so when they have been in style a few times. They are of medium weight and softness. The stitching is solid and looks nice on them. I like the seam that runs down the front of them. I can remember a long while back when that was a style too. They have 2 front and 2 back pockets, all of them functional. I got the X-Large and they fit me very nicely with a bit of room to spare due to the 2% elastane in the material. The color is really nice and I think it is just right for what I was looking for in a pair of jeans. Another nice thing is that the length for my 5'4" height is perfect without needing to be shortened. I am very pleased with these jeans and happy that I got them! Definitely can recommend these for nice looking and comfortable jeans.

Cute pants & I like them a LOT, but they have a slight fit issue you should be aware of! Ok, I ended up buying two different sizes, & since they didn't have the large in the same color, I got it in a lighter blue instead. For reference, I'm apx. 5'7" & 138lbs & have what they say is more of an 'hourglass' figure with an apx. 29-30" waist & apx. 40" hips and the darker blue pants are the Med. & the lighter are the large! The med. fits more like a small in the waist, that's why I decided to try the large. I was thinking it'd be perfect, but it fits more like an XL! Odd to say the least! I can't decide yet which I will be keeping, so thought I'd post this with BOTH so you could see for yourself & hopefully it will help you decide. The colors are oddly close, & pretty close to true in these pics.. This is how they look right out of the package, so yes, they are very wrinkled! I have no doubt though, being as I've dealt with this type of material before, that once washed & hung or dried, they will be fine! Worst case, nothing an iron won't fix! :-) I love the flowyness (if that's even a word LOL) of the pants, but prefer the large for this! Just a little more like a skirt with that size, which is what I like better! Love the flirty aspect of the slit in the front too! It's on both sides, but I just posted the pic of the one. I like the length better on the large too, as the Med. just seems a little TOO short for my taste! But, I actually prefer the darker blue?!!? So I'm torn!! I think I will end up keeping the lighter Large & just take in the elastic some so it'll fit me better & return the Med.! The waist is mostly elastic, except for the very front where it has the tie, so it's on the forgiving side for fit which is nice if you fluctuate in weight like I do! I tend to go up or down 5-10 lbs now & then! LOL Nicely made though, and is priced ok, so I would recommend. They have some nice detail that other pants like this don't which is a huge plus for me! The material is nice & light weight & perfect for spring/summer, but then they could just as easily be paired with nylons for those colder times! Love that they can be either causal or dressy too! Very versatile! I'm always on the lookout for those kinds of buys! Especially now when our dollar isn't going as far! The material has a sort of texture to it to which is kind of nice! Not something that is distracting though! All & all, they are cute, & I like them! LOVE that they come in so many colors too! I just might have to get another couple of these in other colors soon!! Only wished they had a royal blue and/or a hot pink! Maybe they will hear me and add some?! Well....hope this helps! Thanks much! ENJOY!

Cute pants & I like them a LOT, but they have a slight fit issue you should be aware of! Ok, I ended up buying two different sizes, & since they didn't have the large in the same color, I got it in a lighter blue instead. For reference, I'm apx. 5'7" & 138lbs & have what they say is more of an 'hourglass' figure with an apx. 29-30" waist & apx. 40" hips and the darker blue pants are the Med. & the lighter are the large! The med. fits more like a small in the waist, that's why I decided to try the large. I was thinking it'd be perfect, but it fits more like an XL! Odd to say the least! I can't decide yet which I will be keeping, so thought I'd post this with BOTH so you could see for yourself & hopefully it will help you decide. The colors are oddly close, & pretty close to true in these pics.. This is how they look right out of the package, so yes, they are very wrinkled! I have no doubt though, being as I've dealt with this type of material before, that once washed & hung or dried, they will be fine! Worst case, nothing an iron won't fix! :-) I love the flowyness (if that's even a word LOL) of the pants, but prefer the large for this! Just a little more like a skirt with that size, which is what I like better! Love the flirty aspect of the slit in the front too! It's on both sides, but I just posted the pic of the one. I like the length better on the large too, as the Med. just seems a little TOO short for my taste! But, I actually prefer the darker blue?!!? So I'm torn!! I think I will end up keeping the lighter Large & just take in the elastic some so it'll fit me better & return the Med.! Comfortable, good length, but inside is not lined I like these pants overall, but WHY is the inside not lined with the same material?? The white inside sometimes shows and it really cheapens it in my opinion. If you’re going to have a giant slit the inside should be lined. Anyway, they are very comfortable, lightweight, and the inseam is just the right length for me! I’m between 5’7 and 5’8. But I should mention, I tried these pants in other colors (solid colors) and returned them. For some reason the solid colors look like long church-girl skirts to me. I only kept this pair. The pattern somehow makes it look more fashionable and less fundamentalist-churchy.

LOVE IT To begin, I love the length. I'm 5 foot 4 1/4 inches I weigh about 123 and it's long enough to cover my ankles, but it doesn't drag on the floor like some skirts or pants if I wear shoes with just 1/2" or 1" heels. I got the bright red and looks like good quality. Also, this is very comfortable and the slits are not too high - maybe about knee length? UPDATE: I now have this is four colors - white, red, navy and black; moreover I have ordered the gray striped pants as well. ANOTHER UPDATE: I now have another three colors, but here is something I noticed. Some waistbands are more relaxed than others; if you look it it, some have three seams - with one going through the middle of the waistband, which makes it a little tighter. Others have just two seams - the top and bottom, making them a bit more relaxed in the waist.

These pants have two big pockets. Pockets are hardly noticeable when empty, they have ample space which can fit my iPhone 15 Pro. The pants drape beautifully and don't feel tight. The loose, wrinkle-resistant legs create a flattering look. I look slimmer when wear it. These are definitely in my top three favorite pants that I've purchased this summer.

Great Yoga Pants These wide leg yoga pants are incredibly comfortable with nice stretchy material and a good fit. I love the fit especially for yoga as I find they have a nice, loose, wide leg style yet are still fitted in the right areas, and I love that they move with my body when I am doing different poses. The addition of pockets is a nice touch, even though I might not put anything too bulky in them since they are form-fitting to the body, but they are nice and deep and still great for slipping your hands into. I ordered my usual size Medium, and they fit great. For reference, I'm 5'6", 145 lbs, with a 28" waist and 38" hips. The only small issue I noticed is that panty lines can be visible when wearing these pants, so you might want to opt for seamless underwear depending on how they fit you. Overall, these have become my go-to lounge and yoga pants. They're so easy to slip on and off, and they look flattering while still being incredibly comfortable.

Comfy, Flattering & Stylish The Maxbee Wide Leg Pants rate high on function and style. They're super comfortable, flattering, and breathable. I got my wife a large for her size 12/5'5 form and they fit perfectly. They're slimming without feeling tight and they fall nicely over the legs. They feel really smooth and soothing on the skin and go with almost anything. They're not just for yoga, but can be used for any occasion. They pair well with tunics and tees and really hit the mark for style and timelessness. They're also easy maintenance and quick hang dry. The side pockets are a welcome inclusion as well. ~ Kort

Size Up and Bend Over I’m 5’3, 120 lbs, normal build, ordered size small. Okay, so... my first two pictures should explain why these get only 3 stars. My husband and I both busted out laughing when I pulled these out of the package. They could easily be mistaken for a child’s pants by their size, until you turn them around and they look like a deflated pin cushion. But then I put them on, and was quite impressed actually at the material - I personally love this material for leggings, it’s the only kind I’ll buy. So kudos are due there. I immediately realized I should have ordered a size up to be safe (although the description/reviews say true to size). Granted they’re a size to small, I like how they fit (assuming they’ll be more impressive when I order a medium). The butt shaper detail is “okay” at best, it definitely gives some definition but the way the butt seam is sewn in makes it shiny so it looks like you’ve got a string up your crack. I’m not sure if that part will go away when I size up, but it’s not so bad that I’m not willing to risk it. I just recommend doing a “bend over” test before leaving the house in sunlight. Overall they’re alright, crossing my fingers a medium makes them 5 star. Key takeaway: SIZE UP!

Very comfortable, size down if you are between sizes. These navy lightweight ankle pants are very comfortable. Darling jeans, surprised at the price These are really flattering and comfortable - if it weren't for the lower quality button closure used in the front waistband and the need to wear these a bit to get the painted on lines to look natural, I would think these would be a lot more expensive. I wear a 28 in Mother Denim / Anthropologie and a size small fits great. I think if you are under a size 6 you would find the small too baggy. Keep in mind there is a LOT of stretch in these so you'll be comfy and should feel better about buying in S/M/L (I've always bought jeans in exact sizes so was surprised it would come in just those categories). The wide leg is very flattering and the large pockets in the back look great with a top. Will try to remember to snap a picture and upload. I told a friend they were from amazon and under $50 and she said, "no way." For reference I'm 5'7" 145lbs, wearing size small in "Lapis." The hem fits perfectly with flats for my height, might be a little long for the shorter girls but cuffed jeans are back in style.

Cute design, thin material and snug fitting I am 5'4", 120lbs, and usually a size 4 in pants (though I can sometimes be a 6 since I am curvy). I bought these in a 28 and they are comfortable but snug. The brown color is nice, and I like the button, the hemming, and the high waist, which works well with crop tops. The length is fine, and about as short as I would go in pants. The fabric registers as a very 70's, thin polyester. VPL is a real issue, so exercise caution with your choice of undergarments. You may also want to size up for a looser fit, as these are very fitted and stretchy. I took two stars off because for this price (about $35 at the time of my writing) these are not cheaper than what I might find at a store specializing in casual basics, and those stores would have higher quality materials and craftsmanship. The tag on these says "do not put in the dryer" so they seem like they won't hold up.

Not flattering on wide hips For reference I am 5’8”, 155 lbs and typically a US Women’s size 6-8. I have wide hips, backside and thighs. I got a size Medium. This was not at all a flattering fit. Pros - color is true to photos and green was great for fall. Material was nice, thicker than expected and comfortable. It would be too thin for fall wear in colder climates though. Cons - Silhouette was bad. The high waist with elastic and zipper did not work. The added zipper was flimsy, jammed on first use and made the fit at the waist very unflattering. It wrinkled easy but that was expected. They ended up being a little short on me as well. The quality was also subpar. I was very concerned with the tightness in hips that I would tear the flimsy seam in any sitting movement. Overall I do not recommend unless you are very thin or have a more straight build.

Do better.

Fits super well, so comfy and cute! These pants are so comfy! Totally my go-to lounge at home pants when I still want to look *kinda* cute but also be comfy and casual. The frog button is super cute, and the minty color is just as shown. I wear these high waisted just above my belly button, and they end above my ankles. I’m 5’3”, 125lbs, 26 waist, and the smallest size fits me perfect, the elastic is nice and really stretchy but stays put. AND they have pockets! If you wear this with the right cropped or tight top it can be a super cute spring summer casual outfit out. Idk why some other people gave such bad reviews, this product is exactly as shown and honestly look better on than I even expected! Sizing NOT Right. 1st time I bought a grey in small, inseam (it's only 28.5inch) isn't as long as described on size chart (whixh I still holding on to, still not sure if I would keep it or not), so I bought the Khaki in medium, hoping it would longer - Wrong! Inseam was only 27 inch thus made it shorter total length (returned it). Thinking it might be a mistake in the inseam sizing, I ordered the Army green in medium. Omg!!! Same thing, inseam length isn't 29inch but only 28 inch, still shorter than the Grey small. Other than that, the Army green cutting on the bottom/hem is slanting as well (as shown in the photo) - returned it too. For the price seller is selling is not worth it at all with all the wrong measurements. I bought few pants with similar material from Singapore for only US$16 - $17 a piece (only its a little thicker, which is popularly called as scuba material pants). I thought I could find something similar on Amazon US, and only if only this Amazon seller gives the correct measurements it could have been less hassles and frustration! For ref I am 5ft 4 - 5 inch, 128 - 130 lbs, waist 27"-28", hip 37".

Stretchy, but inconsistent I like these jeans. They are inexpensive, and light stretchy fabric. I normally wear a medium, but it was huge! Small in the light blue fits great. Small in black is a little big, but I think I may have received a defective pair of small black jeans. For reference, I have 29” waist, 38” hips. 5’6”, 135 lbs. Photos are 2 smalls and 2 XS pairs. As you can see in the photos, the bottom 2 pairs were both a small, but the black pair is significantly bigger. The XS were more consistent, but due to stretchiness of the fabric, I can squeeze into the light blue pair, but not the black. Second photo is length - the small black pair is significantly shorter than the XS, even though they are both supposed to be regular length - which is why I think I got a defective pair. Im debating ordering a new pair of black Small because I think this pair may have just been sewn wrong, but I hesitate because of the cost of shipping and the length of time to receive the order.

Fits awkward body type! I have diabetes, and am insulin resistant. Because of this, my stomach bulges like a pregnant lady. It’s hard to find pants that fit right because they are always too tight in the waist and loose on the legs. If I get them to fit my waist right, the legs will be very loose and look so bad and frumpy. I am 5’7 and 175lbs, much of it carried in my stomach area. I chose 12Long but may have been able to size down 1 size, but they would be very snug. If you are my size and body type, I’d say stick with 12L, but if you prefer tight pants go down 1. If you have a big booty, go up 1. These pants fit my stomach AND legs properly. I have longer legs and the length covered my ankles very well and did not rise throughout the day like high waters, even with me bending and stooping down a lot at work. They were very soft and comfortable while still holding me in and giving me a nice shape overall. Made me look like a woman instead of an Oompa Loompa. I have only worn them once, but I have a cat and chinchilla so my black clothes always hold hair, but the hair did not stick to these. 5 stars! Will buy again! FYI: The back pockets are not real pockets.

Highest Waist Ever - And Slim Fit too! I've wanted some dress slacks that were longer in the leg, since I'm taller, and a 40" outseam sounded perfect. Usually that's about a 10" rise, so this should be perfect. Right? Well, I would be wrong there. The outseam indeed is 40", but the rise is - 14". Yikes! So when laid down and measured, the inseam is 27". So while they aren't capri pants, they are still a bit short for me. There is enough material at the seam that I can let out the hem, so that can work. However - while the size chart describes a 33" plus wait, in reality, it measures only 15" across. Which makes the waist 30". Yikes again. And with the heavy elastic in front it's a light fit. I notice that the model has a 25" waist, and wears a medium size, which is a 27" waist on the size chart. So that'll work for her, but for the rest of us, but this means ordering up a size... OVERALL - Which I should have done. Oh well...my search for the perfect dress pants continues!

Linen-Like, Casual Summer Pants For reference, I am 5’2” and a size 4 (27”) waist. The length of these pants were great on me, but seeing as how they were a perfect fit length wise, I could see that being problematic for anyone more than a few inches taller than me. The material is lightweight (linen-like) and the waist is elastic. The fabric itself is not stretchy, so I would say the waist is only moderately stretchy (limited by the actual fabric itself). I love the look of paper bag waist pants, but just have not found them to be that flattering on my body type (straight, no hips). Not an issue with these pants, just an overall life gripe! I will enjoy these in the summer with a cute pair of casual sandals and maybe a white or black casual tee on top.

SIZE UP! I am 60 y.o., almost 5'1", about 118 soft lbs. Carry most of my weight in lower stomach (30") and butt/hips(39"). The fit here totally depends on your preference. The medium fit well but was stretched a bit at the hips and pockets so I tried a large. They were too baggy but I washed on cold and dried on low and they shrunk just enough - maybe an inch everywhere (inseam is now 25"). The rise is still a bit low which makes them hang longer. They came out of the dryer just the way you see in the 2 "after" pics - no need to iron any serious wrinkles. Love the fabric for summer - light enough but not too thin or see-through. Very comfortable and DO have 3% stretch all around. Getting more colors... pics are before and after wash/dry.

Size up if you are a between size I am 5’6.5”, about 143-145 lbs, with a slightly athletic/muscular build. I tend to wear a 26/27 in pants and the medium fit me nicely. I also tried a size small (also pictured) but they were too tight in the mid thigh. Quick review version: pant material is nicer and thicker than expected, love that the waist band is smooth and not elastic. Nice length and overall shape. I can wear these with a pair of sneakers and the ends won’t drag. Longer/in depth: as mentioned, I have a bit more muscle, especially in the thighs. I first bought the small based on reviews and size guide. The waist fit me well, but the actual leg part was a bit too tight around my mid thigh. My pictures show me in the medium and small. You can see that the small is more tight in the thigh which causes a bit of buckling/a pouch to form at the top. The medium stays flowy through the leg. The waist on the medium is not too much larger and still fits me fine. These pants are good for cold weather or cold offices, but you could also probably wear them with a t shirt and not be too hot.

These fall beautifully I always hesitated to get pants online, as you never know how they end up looking and fitting. Dresses and tops are much easier. I really wanted a pair of wide leg pants though, and saw these through the Vine program. Read the reviews and pretty much everyone who got them seemed happy with them, so I pulled the trigger. Happy to these really do look and fit very well and the white is not see through. Which was surprising, as the fabric is a fairly light (but not very thin) polyester. I am not a fan of synthetic fibers and would certainly prefer these in a linen blend, but I must say I do appreciate how they fall because of the material and the feel is not bad at all either. They're quite soft and silky. At this price, I think they're a great value and totally recommend them. Might get them in another color too. For reference, I am 5'5, 130 lbs and got an S.

I would order again! Size reference: I’m 5’6” and 128 lbs Witt a 27.5” waist and 37” hips. I wear size 4/27 in pants/jeans and have both small and medium in stretchy pants. The size chart showed the small from Noahella to have a 28.34” waist and 41.34 inch hips. I think the small is just a little snug on my waist so the button looks slightly pulled. The length is perfect for flats. Description: The material feels and looks like “real” slacks, opposed to the cheap polyester look that snags easily. The fabric is 100% polyester but looks like chiffon and is flowy. The pleated front gives a dressier style and the wide legs are comfortable but look sophisticated. The back waistband is elastic but the front is flat with a button/zipper fly. There are belt loops, deep functional hip pockets, and faux back pockets. Recommendation: I love these pants for work! I feel stylish and comfortable when I wear them. The high waist is flattering with a fitted top or tucked-in loose top. I would be okay with wearing these for business travel, meetings, church, and wedding/baby showers.

Great pants & VERY comfy but run SMALL! I really like these pants! Ordered a Large to begin with, but DANG! BEWARE! They run SMALL! For reference, I'm apx. 5'7" & apx. 140lbs with 30" waist & 40" hips & the large was SNUG in the waist! For better comfort I went with the XL & I'm happier! LOVE the length! That was one of the BIGGEST reasons I went with these! The reviews said they WERE longer, and they ARE! LOVE that I can wear heels! YEAH! Only issue I have is the material! It's more a thinner cheap polyester so I took off a star for that! Would have expected, for the price, for them to have been a higher grade material! The pics made me think they were too! But, all and all, I like them & will keep them! LOVE the pockets! Just be aware that if you want that look they show in the pictures, I'd go up TWO sizes! You can tell from the pics, even with XL they are a wee on the tighter side showing ALL! So, I think I'll be adding some shape wear!! But, for the price and the LENGTH I recommend them!! Hope all this help!! Thanks much! ENJOY!

To get the look in the photo go 2 sizes above your Amazon suggested size The fabric and color are great. I am a suggested Amazon M and I’m exchanging for an XL to wear with drapes camisoles and jardigans for work, travel, or anything where I need to look out together without anything binding, clingy, or that I would have to peel off of sweaty skin. Camisole is quince M, bra size 36ddd freya, Levi straus high rise shorts size 8 to give perspective on exactly how this would work for you. Tank size M/L.

Perfection ,and read for stats I'm a high rise convert, these are perfection! I'm small but have the muscle tone of Wonder bread plus I'm a 62 Y O grandmother. But in these jeans? I don't look it. I'm 5 7 @ 127 lbs - 29" waist and 39" hips and I bought the size 4 with 32" inseam which is More than long enough. I sew so non issue. My problem area is heinous muffin top if jeans not "just right" and many times I wear a Spanx cami to compensate for this. Not comfy in the south east summers lol. No Spanx needed with these. I'd always thought if I avoid my waist all together I'd avoid muffin top hence low waist line pants, I was wrong! These fit excellent in crotch which can also be another issue with me. The dreaded CT lol. heavy enough fabric that these are not see through, wearing black under things in this picture. Not blessed in the back of the house and these make the most of what I've got by being nice and snug without flattening what I have, great back pocket placement. Nice and higher but not too high up. I'd just tried on $200 white jeans yesterday at the mall but they were too sheer not to mention too expensive for what they were offering me. I'd pay if I had to but... Came home and went on Amazon, ordered Joe jeans at big $ and ordered low at this price. Ecstatic to Find these on my door step this morning and put them on to find my happy place in my fitting world. I can't believe the price of these and am ordering another because coffee guzzling me and white anything means I always order two if I love it lol.

Not 32" inseam This denim is like butter. So comfy. Fits great. But i really need a 32" inseam. These are only 30". Ill give them a try in the wash but if they shrink at all I wont be able to wear them.

Overly stretchy mom jeans - buy one size smaller I'm a size L, 30 in waist, 32 in inseam. Sometimes I wear a 12, sometimes a 14. I purchased the Marilyn in size 12. It is too big. I should have purchased the 10. It stretches over 2 in at the waist. That is brand new. It has stretch so that wearing it over the course of the day, this pant will definitely fall off of me. Inseam is fine, a bit long (probably 33 in) but not a big deal because when I wear shoes it won't look long. The instructions on Amazon say to purchase a size smaller. Definitely do that. Weight of the fabric is heavy, winter weight. I don't think it will be comfortable outside in the summer. Honestly I would not purchase these jeans again - I like Madewell High Rise Alley jeans and Jcrew matchstick jeans better, they are more flattering. These are totally mom jeans. Rise is just below the belly button. I like the high waist, but with this much stretch, it kind of defeats the purpose. These have so much stretch that no matter what size you get, they will be baggy and ill fitting by the end of the day. You could wear a belt, but at that point you might as well wear a real pant. At least these are not the leggings that are made to look like jeans. If you buy these jeans, you will be tugging them up all the live long day. A pair of twenty dollar levis from target fit better than this. I give this product two stars. One star for good quality sturdy fabric and stitching, and one star for comfort. Attached are photos of how the jeans fit me. I couldn't photograph the tug at the waist with my phone - the jeans stretch out four inches with minimal effort. Too much!

As happy as I can be Ordered size 14, 5'7" / 202lbs and I don't love the way anything fits me at this weight. That being said, these jeans are not bad. They fit well, not super tight and not baggy. There is lots of stretch in the denim, so they are very comfortable even when sitting down. The denim is not thin so that it does not show every lump or bump. The waist goes to just at my navel, which means there is no muffin top. I posted a picture of the inside of the jeans front panel. I don't know that I look "thinner" but they don't make me look worse and thats a good thing. The jeans are long enough for 3 inch heels (I only had 2.5 inch heels on in the picture.) The denim is thick enough and has enough stretch to cuff them at the bottom to make them ankle length to wear with flats and still look cute. If someone wants the jeans to be tight and form fitting (for the lifting and tucking) I would suggest sizing down one size from usually size.

NYDJ Women's Marilyn Straight Jeans These jeans are crazy-comfortable. I wouldn't say that I suddenly appear to be a size smaller than I actually am, but I do like how they fit pear-me. The back waistline doesn't gap, which is very appreciated. Here's what makes me unhappy -- in several areas the stitching is inferior to several pairs of $25 jeans that are in my closet (hopefully the photo I'm adding actually appears). For the price (not the most costly jeans ever produced, but not cheap), the craftsmanship should be better. They're made in the US, so I like that and again, very comfortable, so that's important. By the way, I'm 5'4 and the jeans are too long for me by a few inches. Easily remedied at the local tailor's shop.

Soft, comfortable and classy I've worn NYDJ jeans for several years now and always worn a size 10. I ordered the NYDJ's Ponte Trouser in a size 10 and found the fit to be as expected for a knit trouser. Snug in the seat and upper thigh, with a very slight flare at the cuff. The length of these trousers on my 5'6" frame was slightly longer than expected but perfect with a 2-3" heel. These trousers are super comfortable. They are a mid-weight knit and look great paired with a sweater or blouse. They provide a snug, classy look that is perfect for work or play. Good looking, well made and easy to fall in love with because they feel so good on, the Ponte Trouser by NYDJ's is the wardrobe basic you've been looking for. Definitely recommend them! NOTE: I ordered both black and charcoal colors. One of the included photos are the two colors side by side.

Cute! I ordered a size up and it was perfect. I often struggle with pants being too short but these were pretty good, I'd say a 30" inseam. They would also be awesome if you needed them hemmed because it would add to the nice drape. The paper bag top is flattering and adds coverage. They would make a great travel pant.

Decent Jeans For reference: I am 5'8", 190ish lbs, pear shape with a meno-belly, 31" inseam, 37" waist, 45" hips. I typically wear 14/16 depending on the style and brand. I did look at their size chart and although my measurements did not match up I decided to try size 14 Tall. After washing on cold and drying on low heat I can say they fit. The inseam on the pair I received is 30", which works fine for me. These are snug around the waist but loosen up as you wear them. A 16 would have been too big/baggy, so with this jean I believe I am between the two sizes. There is a good weight to these but not so heavy they cannot be worn. Will be good Fall and Winter jeans, but too heavy for Summer. These are high waisted and indeed wide legged as described. These may be tummy controlled for smaller tummies, but without additional help from shapewear undergarments these do not help the meno-belly. Not a complaint, just an fyi. Overall I find these to be decent quality jeans for a reasonable price.

Soft and comfy These pants are incredibly comfortable. The waist covers my belly button, gives a nice slimming effect, and is also stretchy enough to not dig into my belly when I sit down. I ordered a pair in brown, and the color is a nice chocolate shade. The length is nice also; at 5’10”, I was worried that the 31” inseam would be too short, but they fall well past my ankles. These are high quality and look nice. Very happy!

Not a style for me but very nice quality The denim is really nice weight in these high waist jeans. No visible panty lines, they’re not so heavy that they are hot to wear and have just enough stretch for them to keep their shape. The seams and hems are uniformly stitched and sturdy also. The legs are very wide, as shown, but I don’t feel that they have a baggy fit elsewhere. Unfortunately, on my body shape and height I don’t find them to be at all flattering I don’t know if it is the back pocket placement, the leg length or the snug fit of the jeans from the waist to the hips. (I’m 5’4” tall and wearing a size 10.) Even with a 2”heel I felt I looked short and squat. Not a look I can successfully carry off. On a more slender, taller frame they would be very cute.

Definitely size up! Pay CLOSE attention to the size chart! I think the size chart might be pretty accurate. I am 5'4" and about 200 pounds. I usually wear around a size 14 but I got the 16 just in case because of the measurements. These are still too small and much tighter than I like. The only reason they even still fit is because of how stretchy they are. So don't expect the sizes to match what you are used to. Check the size chart. If these were the right size for me I think I would like them ok. The stretchy material should be very comfortable. The length is good for me. The zippers is not smooth and takes some effort. It gets caught up a lot. The pockets are not very deep and the fabric feels like it won't last very long. The size on the pants says 32 everywhere. Nowhere on the pants does it have the size 16. Only on the plastic wrapping does it say size 16. So it isn't surprising that this doesn't match closer to actual US sizes. Overall it is an ok pair of pants. Just ok though.

Great jeans- total 90s vibe I’m 5’6, 125lb, 34DD. I usually wear a 26 and sometimes 27 in designer jeans and ordered size 26. I love these jeans! It’s like I’m reliving 1993! They are super stretchy and fit great. I took photos from three angles so you can see. They’re a nice fit all the way around but almost too tight in the waist for me. The material stretches so even though it’s tight, it’s not uncomfortable. I will, however, be careful to wash them in cold and hang them to dry. These are the highest waisted jeans I’ve ever owned but the buttons on the front really make them work. This is actually perfect for the crop tops that I sometimes hesitate to wear because now my midsection will be adequately covered. The length is perfect- I can wear tennies or boots or even a low heel and these jeans will look good with any of them. I highly recommend these jeans. If you want a looser fit, I would size up one size.

Pleasantly surprised! I purchased these in the color B-BLACK in a size XL. For sizing reference, I'm usually a medium-large or a 8-12/29-31 in bottoms and jeans. If it helps, I wear a size 6 or 8 in Lulu leggings. I'm 5'3" tall with a short torso, and my measurements are about 44"-33"-44" (bust-waist-hip) right now, give or take. I'd say they run a tad small if you have bigger hips/butt. I sized up because I was concerned about them being too tight, and I'm glad I did! I could do a large but I really like the looseness of the XL, as it's comfy but still flattering, and with a bodysuit, the fit is perfect in the waist (this one is from Pumiey, btw; I have a review of it as well). They are loose in the tummy area without being too baggy. I'm glad the pleats are super subtle. This style does not have the elastic back, as I don't love that, but I do wish they had some little fake slit pockets in the back, like other slacks do. I'm a little on the shorter side and had some extra length at the bottom, even though my legs are longer in proportion to my body, so I could wear a pair of heels and still be fine, but I don't often wear anything but flats, so I'll probably end up getting them hemmed by an inch or two. The only small complaint I can make is that I wish the button was darker, as I feel the tan one cheapens the look a bit. The material is a bit hard to describe, but I'd say it's pretty standard Amazon loose slack material, if you've tried others. It's thin and a little slick, but not very stretchy, if that makes sense. They feel kind of cool on the inside and they move nicely. I couldn't tell you how well these will hold up in the long run, but I'll try to update after I've worn and washed a handful of times. Overall, I'd definitely recommend these, but maybe size up if you have bigger hips/butt. I've been looking for some cheap slacks to try out because I haven't tried to dress nicely for the office in so long, not since I gained weight years ago, and these are a nice intro back into it. I actually purchased this exact style in B-BEIGE in the same XL size, so I'll post pics of those. I may grab the C style next, since they have tonal buttons and slit pockets in the back, even if they have elastic in the waistband too.

Great pant, nice fit. Reviewing PACIBE Womens High Waist Wide Leg Linen Pants, First off, I assume a lot of people are reading the reviews hoping for some insight into the sizing, since the size chart is obviously bonkers, showing an XXL as having an 18.5 to 22.8-inch waist. A clue that the size chart is wrong is in the model details. The listing pic says the model is wearing a size S, with a 25.20” waist, but the chart shows an S has a 13.5 to 17.8-inch waist. I normally wear a 14 to 16, depending on the brand, for brand name clothes and an 18 for made-for-amz, East Asia brands. I ordered the XXL and it fits fine. An XL would’ve worked fine also. The waist is not only elastic, but has a drawstring, so you can cinch it in. The title says it’s linen, but these are a linen blend, as detailed under the product details. Since they are partly linen, they will need steamed or ironed, but since they are 50% viscose, it’s not as bad as 100% linen. The material feels like linen, although it’s thicker. I was worried about whether the material would be transparent, so I ordered khaki. It’s a nice shade of khaki and is not transparent. With East Asia clothes, I normally need to hem them, and this is no exception. I need to hem these by at least 3 inches. If I order in a size that doesn’t need hemmed, they would be tight in the waist and butt. I am 5’5”. The quality is good enough that I don’t mind needing to hem them, since it’s simple to do. The pictures were taken straighten out of the package, so they are wrinkled from being compressed for shipping. To get a nice, crisp look, you will want to iron them after each wash, though. One complaint though, other than being too long: They have a strong chemical smell and will need thoroughly washed before wearing.

Great pant, nice fit. Reviewing PACIBE Womens High Waist Wide Leg Linen Pants, First off, I assume a lot of people are reading the reviews hoping for some insight into the sizing, since the size chart is obviously bonkers, showing an XXL as having an 18.5 to 22.8-inch waist. A clue that the size chart is wrong is in the model details. The listing pic says the model is wearing a size S, with a 25.20” waist, but the chart shows an S has a 13.5 to 17.8-inch waist. I normally wear a 14 to 16, depending on the brand, for brand name clothes and an 18 for made-for-amz, East Asia brands. I ordered the XXL and it fits fine. An XL would’ve worked fine also. The waist is not only elastic, but has a drawstring, so you can cinch it in. The title says it’s linen, but these are a linen blend, as detailed under the product details. Since they are partly linen, they will need steamed or ironed, but since they are 50% viscose, it’s not as bad as 100% linen. The material feels like linen, although it’s thicker. I was worried about whether the material would be transparent, so I ordered khaki. It’s a nice shade of khaki and is not transparent. With East Asia clothes, I normally need to hem them, and this is no exception. I need to hem these by at least 3 inches. If I order in a size that doesn’t need hemmed, they would be tight in the waist and butt. I am 5’5”. The quality is good enough that I don’t mind needing to hem them, since it’s simple to do. The pictures were taken straighten out of the package, so they are wrinkled from being compressed for shipping. To get a nice, crisp look, you will want to iron them after each wash, though. One complaint though, other than being too long: They have a strong chemical smell and will need thoroughly washed before wearing.

Eh… First off, these took about a month to be delivered. Ok, so here’s what I think. If you’re someone with hips and a tush, like me, it’s hard for harem pants to work. I went with my regular size medium (I’m 5’2” approx. 140lbs) going by the size chart of a 45” hip I thought these would be plenty large to give a looser more oversized fit. Not the case. The fabric is too thick, and I honestly should have sized up a size or even two, to get the look in the product photos. Honestly, even then, I just don’t know if these would be as stylish as I was hoping they’d be. They are pretty comfortable. The fabric is thick and doesn’t breathe though, so I’d say these are for cooler temps. I am sure I’ll find a way to style these and make them cute, but I was hoping to not have to put too much work into what should be an easy pant to style. All in all, these are a pass for me.

good quality and they stay up I'm impressed with the quality of these socks. They are smooth and just slightly opaque. They provide great coverage and are great to wear with loafers and boots. I love that they stay up and stay put all day without feeling tight. They show no sign of wear after being worn all day, even on the heels. I'm 5'4" and 170 pounds, and they come up to just below my knee. Highly recommend for trouser socks.

Inconsistent Size Chart I’m 5’7”, 129 lbs, 29” waist, 38” at the widest part of my booty. Size charts always seem to be so misleading. I can never rely on them. I read reviews trying to figure out what size to order. Several reviews said the size chart was pretty accurate, which meant I would have needed to order a large. I’m usually a small, but in considering the reviews I ordered a large and a medium and figured I’d return whichever didn’t fit. I ended up ordering a small after they arrive because the medium was too big. In the photos, I’m wearing a small, and you can see the pants could easily fit someone 2-3 inches wider than I am. They stretch big time. I’ll probably keep the small but not sure because the length is right at where I would want it to be and after washing/drying, it seems like this fabric would shrink quite a bit and then be too short. Either way, I would order a size down or at the very least your regular size when ordering these.

Close but not quite I was really hoping to love these jeans. The reviews were mixed so I knew it would be a crap shoot whether they worked or not. Unfortunately, these are a swing and a miss for me. I’m 5ft 7in, 165lbs, carrying most of my weight in my belly. I wear 8/10 bottoms and M/L tops. I ordered size Large. I liked how much stretch there was but it’s almost too much. I suspect these won’t hold their shape over time and jeans this stretchy tend to accentuate my belly vs slimming it. Cute from the front, not as much from the side. The fabric is a bit rough and scratchy on my skin. The belt is super cute. These just touched the floor with bare feet, but became too short with a sneaker on. Because of that I would only wear these with flip flops and that’s just not versatile enough to add this piece to my wardrobe. Will be returning. Bummer :(

Huge Sizing Issue These pants are so beautiful (in photo and in person) and I was so excited to get them. I realize I am very thin and I am not as curvy as the model in the picture, but I am also not emaciated. I ordered the smallest size available, a small, and was thinking they may be a little loose…possibly. I am swimming in them and I’d have to gain 15-20 pounds to fill them out. They are also super long as you can see I’m barefoot in some of the pictures, and I actually wore 5 1/2 inch platform heels in another to show how they look. Which was fine in the heels. But I cannot believe how large these are for a small. I would have to guess these are about a size 28 in US sizing or a size 7/8. To me, these are a generous medium (maybe even large) and I wonder if size tags got mixed up. I’m 5f4 and weigh 106. I am thin, so I typically wear a size 0 jeans. However, I was not expecting these to be this big. Again, maybe a little loose, but wow! I am very disappointed because these jeans are beautiful. I can tell that they would look great if they made an extra (double extra) small.

Pleasant Surprise for Midsize person I am 5’8”, approx 170lbs with a 30” waist and 40” hips. I struggle to find pants - jeans specifically - that fit me how I like, and usually don’t have a ton of luck online. I took a chance on these and I’m so glad. Are they perfect? No - I wish the zipper was about 2” longer because when I first went to put them on I thought there was no way they were fitting, but once they were up they fit my waist and have the perfect amount of room through my hips and thighs that I have been looking for. They check a lot of boxes for me, and are even ever-so-slightly long which I never find anymore, and is one of my favorite things to have in loose jeans. I ordered my usual size, 12, again being skeptical, but it’s just right. They fit to my waist without digging in - I had abdominal surgery a year ago and now have a belly pooch that makes most jeans uncomfortable - but I can wear these all day. Also I haven’t had to touch it, but I love that they have the adjustable strap at the waist in back to get just the right fit.

Vivid, comfortable & stretchy These pants have such a vivid color. The green is very solid green. I feel it’s almost not true to the photos in the description but pretty close. They are stretchier than I thought they’d be and fit well. I am 5’ 5” and about 186 lbs. I hover between a 12-14 depending on the brand and the day. I went with a 14, and they’re spot on. I definitely would not have gotten away with a 12. Sometimes 14s tend to be long on me, but these are perfect bootcut length for my proportions. The rise is like a high mid. They are also on the thinner side which avoids feeling bulky like corduroy tends to, but it’s not the choice if you’re looking for something super warm. I would buy these in more colors and likely will, soon.

VERY flattering and soft/comfortable It's true about fashion. Wait long enough and it comes back in style. Flared bottoms and corduroy fabric, I'm back in Jr. high. But these pants, omg, they are NOT the pants from back then. The fabric is solid corduroy, not striped, super soft, and made from cotton and spandex like jeans these days so they have give and stretch. As soon as I opened the package I could tell I was in love. They hug your hips, high waisted, cute jean type buttons for your fly (yes no zipper but adds to the charm) 4 pockets, slimmer thigh to the knee, then flare out to the bottom. I walked into the living room for my husband to see and his eyes got big and said NICE! and now you're in style! (head slap for that).A couple of things. Normally I wear 8, sometimes 10 if no stretch or give. According to their size chart, because I have a 40" hip I went with their size 12. I should have paid more attention to the fabric because upon first try, no problem but slightly bigger than my body, a little sag in the crotch and from the pic you can see touches the ground. If you're wearing high heels or wedge, that's perfect. I could have easily gone to a 10. I decided to wash them last night, alone because they were new and black, on cool water wash, then dried on high. They came out slightly shrunk, which I hoped they would, and then tried them on again. I didn't take pics, but they still were easy to put on, hugged my hips, no crotch sag, waist a little more fitting and the length does not touch the floor anymore, just about less than 1/2" off the floor. I feel like I made them more like a size 10. So give some consideration that these are stretchy and either order your normal size and watch how you wash, or maybe try a size up and see how they fit, possibly knowing you can make them a little smaller in the wash. VERY flattering and soft/comfortable It's true about fashion. Wait long enough and it comes back in style. Flared bottoms and corduroy fabric, I'm back in Jr. high. But these pants, omg, they are NOT the pants from back then. The fabric is solid corduroy, not striped, super soft, and made from cotton and spandex like jeans these days so they have give and stretch. As soon as I opened the package I could tell I was in love. They hug your hips, high waisted, cute jean type buttons for your fly (yes no zipper but adds to the charm) 4 pockets, slimmer thigh to the knee, then flare out to the bottom. I walked into the living room for my husband to see and his eyes got big and said NICE! and now you're in style! (head slap for that).A couple of things. Normally I wear 8, sometimes 10 if no stretch or give. According to their size chart, because I have a 40" hip I went with their size 12. I should have paid more attention to the fabric because upon first try, no problem but slightly bigger than my body, a little sag in the crotch and from the pic you can see touches the ground. If you're wearing high heels or wedge, that's perfect. I could have easily gone to a 10. I decided to wash them last night, alone because they were new and black, on cool water wash, then dried on high. They came out slightly shrunk, which I hoped they would, and then tried them on again. I didn't take pics, but they still were easy to put on, hugged my hips, no crotch sag, waist a little more fitting and the length does not touch the floor anymore, just about less than 1/2" off the floor. I feel like I made them more like a size 10. So give some consideration that these are stretchy and either order your normal size and watch how you wash, or maybe try a size up and see how they fit, possibly knowing you can make them a little smaller in the wash. Fit well and washed up well It's a real gamble for me to shop for clothes online- it's difficult enough to find things that fit in stores. Surprisingly enough- these pants fit well (just a bit long, but I'm 5'4" so that's not unusual at all). With elastic on 3/4 of the waist band and pleats in front, they are a pretty easy fit. They have front pockets that sit flat behind the pleats but no back pockets, giving them a better look. The material is some sort of cotton twill, like a very light denim weight of fabric. They seem sturdy for casual pants and well made. I typically wear a size 12 (petite if available), sometimes a 10 if pants have a generous cut or vanity sizing- the size Large I ordered in these pants seemed a bit large at first, besides long enough to need a hem. After washing them in warm water and drying on medium heat though they fit just about right and I could perhaps forgo the hemming, but with the wide leg look I think a 'flood' length would look better for spring/summer, so will still hem. Besides the welcome bit of shrinkage, they did really well in the wash- they didn't wrinkle terribly like some cotton pants will- ( photos are post wash). You could skip ironing for casual wear but a hot iron would give them a better look, like for work.

However, it's too long for me (5'4",110lbs), because I don't wear high heels. So I fold it up once at the waist. I may need to hem it about 2 inches because I don't want the hem of my pants to rub against the floor . It doesn't wrinkle easily even after washing and is very easy to handle.

Cute vibrant red-orange pants! Only downside for me, is have a steamer handy because this fabric wrinkles easily. It feels kind of silky. Lot of perks though- it’s very breezy and comfortable and makes for a cute outfit! For reference, I’m 5’6” and bought a size large which fits comfortably (I normally buy a large in pants). Perrrfect! I’m 5’4” size 10, and carry most weight in my hips and thighs, with a smaller waist. Peekaboo... Everyone Can See You The fit and comfort were definitely on point; loose and flowy. However, they did not look frumpy. I usually size up for length, and I am not sure that was necessary. As a 5'9" girl, this was huge. Too bad I had to return them to the seller. Unfortunately the low rating is all about transparency. In the image attached to this review, the silhouette of my hand is on the right and the rectangle retail tag to the right of the drawstring. They were visible in indirect, natural light standing in my office. I kept the army green pair purchased in the same order, because the transparency was less evident.

Silky, comfortable, flattering. Shorter than expected length These silky high waisted palazzo pants are a timeless classic. The fabric is a silky feeling synthetic material that drapes well, and they are very flattering. When taken straight out of the box, they were a little wrinkled but that is to be expected. With a few minutes in the dryer they flattened right out. The waistband falls at the smallest part of my waist -these are very high waisted, and plenty long in the crotch. The pockets are not too shallow, and can fit my phone without slipping out. The waist band is both elastic and drawstring, so they are comfortable, functional and adjustable. I wish they were longer though- I am 5 foot 6 and with completely flat sandals they were about two inches from the floor (and I prefer a floor length pant). I think if you are shorter than me, the pants would hit the floor or perhaps allow a higher heel if you're close to five feet tall. If you're taller than me (for example 5 foot 8 or more) then they would be more of an awesome Culottes length. I ordered a size Medium, which based on the chart fits a size 8 to 10. I am a size 10 and these fit nicely in the waist (meaning the elastic was a perfect comfortable fit and did not require additional cinching with the drawstring). Overall these are a breezy daytime pant, the type that you see on European women with strappy sandals, a form fitting body suit and a great pair of sunglasses.

LOVE these pants! Comfy, flowy, cute, & POCKETS! The perfect summer pants: ~~ just pull on & go. ~~ the elastic waistband keeps them up but isn’t tight. ~~ the drawstring is real & helps with a custom fit. ~~ the polyester fabric is light & swingy. ~~ the pants style has a skirt look with pants comfort. ~~ the pattern is really pretty (I have the Print Blue). ~~ pockets. Did I say POCKETS??!! Real ones that fit my hands or a phone or my glasses!! POCKETS. ~~ I can see these dressed up with a blouse & heels or more casual with a simple tee & flats. [SIZE INFO]: I followed the size chart, ordered a Medium, & they fit perfectly. I’m 5’8”, 128 lbs & usually wears size Med or Lg & 8/10. The length is just right on me, so someone shorter would need a heel & someone taller would have more of an ankle pant. Definitely recommend & I am going to order another color or print! **Thank you for reading this review & I hope that it’s !** 😊

Great dress pants, style and quality I was hesitant to purchase these pants based on mixed opinions in posted reviews and overall score. Being a Latina with curves and big thighs, it’s sometimes challenging to find the right fit. For context I wear size 10 or 12 depending on the style, material, cut, etc… I bought these in a 12 to ensure they weren’t too fitted! The material is great quality, very soft and flowy, they are roomy in the thighs, fabric not overly thin so we’re not transparent as you can see in the pics (I zoomed in on all so you can really see the details), and the cut fit very pretty. The waist has some elasticity in the back and the zipper is in the side. I’m 5’5 and length was perfect. I can wear with flats, kitten or regular 3”heels without a problem. Finally, classy pants that fit right! I plan on getting them in more colors.

Not for curvy women If you have hips, these pants are not for you. The pleats on the front are absolutely horrible. You can't tell from looking at them, but they do actually fit me ok - they just bunch horribly from the pleats. Also, the zipper on the side is terrible and catches on the fabric, making them very difficult to zip or unzip. FYI, I'm only 5'2 and these didn't feel ridiculously long on me (which most pants that aren't petite/short do), so I'm guessing they'd be pretty short on someone of average height.

Comfortable, stretches as you wear I bought two different pairs- an xs and a s. The XS was more "fitting" but then the waist was more snug than I would have liked. The S was a looser trouser look. Also, the coloration from the images isn't exact especially for the brown, it's much more "desaturated." They are stretchy and soft. Almost like fancy sweatpants. I have yet to wash, but due to their composition, I assume the will have the tendency to shrink. The pockets are deep, the quality seems alright. It's an easy to pull fabric, so gentle cycles. No issues with the zipper and button. Opaque, decent length, lightweight. I am 5'6, 128lbs and they sit 2-3 inches above my ankle - I'm pretty used to that. I'll update my review after a few wears.

I’m 5’6, ~150lbs and pear shaped. I originally got the medium but returned it for a large because I wanted the legs to be a little longer. When I washed the large pants the legs did shrink a little bit, but so long as I wear boots or high tops they don’t look like high waters. The pants are extremely comfortable. My state is currently in the high 90’s for summer and I haven’t had any problems with sweating, so I’d said the fabric is pretty breathable. I’ve seen pictures of the pants dressed down, but I wear them to work so they’re easy to dress up with a nice shirt or sweater as well. The pockets are deep for women’s pants. You will need to wear seamless underwear or the creases will show through the back, but if you wear seamless they look great from behind. I actually liked them so much I got a second pair in brown 👍🏻

Pretty good First off, the waist is tight on these — I’m usually a size 6 and I ordered the small and right now they’re too tight to comfortably wear. Also, I am 5’1” and the jogger bottom ends up making these look a bit clowny on me with an inflated bottom. All that is a shame though, because otherwise I really like the pants. They’re cute and look dressy while still comfortable.

WOW, these are pretty good!! I am really surprised at how good these are! They fit me (almost) perfectly and they look super cute! I have them on straight out of the package in the pics so they are holding some wrinkles still, but overall, excellent pants and I will be ordering in more colors! Some thoughts below. - Fit is true to size with some flexibility. I am 5'7" and 145lbs and holding on to some extra holiday weight! I ordered my true size small and they are slightly tight but its only noticeable at the pleats since they are not laying flat!! Otherwise, these fit perfectly! The back of the waist has elastic which makes these flexible in terms of sizing. I would say order your true size. - The material is not the softest but its not uncomfortable either. I am in Michigan and they don't have a ton of warmth so not sure these will see much wear until it gets back into the 40's or 50s around here. - I LOVE the jogger look for a business outfit. It adds some fun to the outfit and they are flattering! They could easily be worn with sneakers for a more casual but chic look too! - Love that they have pockets and belt loops! Overall, these are shockingly so good. I will be buying in more colors. True to size.

Love these! Super comfy pants that are extremely versatile. They are loose/more relaxed fit, but aren't baggy looking on me which is exactly what I was looking for. For context, I bought the size small (I'm 5'7" and about 125lbs). I think these would probably be more flattering and comfortable on people who have a waist smaller than their hips. These pants could totally be worn with sneakers or dressy shoes...I wore them to work today with sneakers and a blazer and I felt super comfortable and put together. I would have given these five stars except that the type of material on the outside seems to be picking up every fluffy thing they touch...maybe a lighter colour would be better for that but on the black it shows, so if you buy the black, have your lint roller handy! Overall, I just love these pants and am seriously considering purchasing a second pair in a different colour!

Very stretchy, nice black jeans These jeans are made of such stretchy material that they make it very comfortable to sit down in, and they are soft material while still looking like regular jeans. I usually wear a 6 in pants, I'm 5'3 128 lbs and these fit me in a 6. I haven't worn them for a full day yet, just tried them out of the package. I just have a concern on whether or not they will stretch too much during the day and end up baggy, because there is actually more room in the waist already than I need, but so far they fit nicely. The petite length is great for me, I'm glad I went with them and relied on the chart. I've had petite length in the past that then seemed too short but these will be good with sneakers or moderate heels. I would recommend giving this brand and jeans a try.

Super Stretchy - looks nice - deep pockets - very high waisted I am 5’6” and about 185 lbs. I ordered the xxl in the dark wash. The jeans look great. They are a nice dark wash. The material is super soft and very stretchy. They fit really well. These are very high waisted jeans. They went well above my belly button. I kind of wish they weren’t quite so high waisted, but they are still quite comfortable.I love the pockets in the front. They are deep enough to put my iPhone 15 pro max. That is super to me because I don’t like carrying purses, etc everywhere.Overall, these are very comfortable jeans with a bit higher waist than I expected.

"Short" inseam is 25" I ordered the short 26" inseam in small and they measure 25" in reality making these too short unless I wear them lower, or below the high waist. This makes them fit oddly on my body. The right leg inseam also veers toward the front too much, more than the left leg. I am only 5' and a size 4. The material is a soft 4 way stretch jean, making these good to move and work in. 61% Cotton, 34% Polyester, 3% Rayon, 2% Elastane. The pockets are deep. I am able to fit my entire hand in to the wrist. Because the length is off an inch it makes them look like I'm waiting for a flood and for the crooked inseam on right leg I deducted stars.

If you are wondering about the waist, YES: there is a little elastic band at the top. Welp, here I am again. Wrote a similar review on a similar pair of wide leg yoga pants only a couple weeks ago. Sigh! Here’s the verdict- these are overall cute yoga pants with good options on color and inseam length. They could really use a size XS though, the size small was just a touch big for me (jeans size 24/25 waist). My real gripe here is the lack of description of the waistband and the actual waistband. I hate HATE having the little elastic band at the top of the waist in leggings/yoga pants. It is not comfy, not flattering. I want a nice wide seamless band that lays flat. I read every word of this item’s description and could not find a clear answer. I then read all the reviews, fruitless venture. I then posted my question to the Amazon community, fruitless venture. Answers were literally like 4 people said no, doesn’t have the elastic and 2 said yes. Baffled by this, I decided to just give them a shot on a lightning deal sale. They arrived today and are going back. I decided to post this review to help anyone who shares the strong hatred of skinny elastic and I decided to include a photo to help show the Amazon community what it is. (I should note that I bought the khaki color and because this is Amazon I cannot rule out the possibility that some colors may have it and others may not. I will not be purchasing 8 pairs in order to find out).

Shows every lump, bump and panty line! 3 stars for the lovely fabric, pockets and how comfortable these pants are. Unfortunately, they are not very thick, show every lump and bump on my butt and thighs and also show the lines of my "invisible" panties! They are not completely unflattering (no camel toe and not up my butt!) but this color on my size and super curvy shape is not quite flattering either. Perhaps a solid black would have been a better choice. I am 5'5" 180lbs 42-35-44 for reference and I ordered the XL petite. I will leave a new review or update if I end up purchasing the black but these are a definite return.

I could have gotten away with a small but I do like the little bit extra room for the belly, especially on vacation. These are my new favorite travel pants.

Not office wear, but great anyway… I bought this size Medium in 2 colors - I am 5’6” and 150 pounds and the fit is perfect. I bought them in hopes to “dress them up” as I’ve seen other reviewers mention, but I definitely won’t be able to wear them in my office setting workplace. The side seams (see pic) look like leggings/athletic wear which unfortunately won’t work for me at the office, but I will definitely get use out of these! They are a high rise (almost too high for me) with a loose fit thru the legs. The flare is gradual and flattering, and they hit right at the top of my foot (almost too short with shoes). They don’t do anything for my fluffy middle section, but I usually wear a longer top to cover that. It washes well - I did not dry it but the tag says dry on low. I got the taupe and cameo colors, and they are both nice. They are a comfortable addition to my athleisure wardrobe which seems to be growing lately!
