Comfortable but the waisted and… These pants are very light and comfortable, perfect for the hot, miserable weather we have been having lately. Be aware that they come with a waistband that could stand the Leaning Tower of Piza back up. This waistband is NO JOKE. It is about 2 inches wide and not sewn into the pants so when you wash the pants, it twists all around with little hope of ever being orderly again. I cut the damn thing out and thus now have even more comfortable pants minus the muffin top that any two inch waistband is bound to cook up. For reference, I am 5’6”, 145lbs (more athletic build than squishy). The pants also have a lot of extra room in the crotch, which is hard to explain because they are women’s but all the same, they are cute, comfortable, affordable and I’d recommend them (with a band-ectomy). Hope that helps :)

QIANXIZHAN Women's Boho Pants I am 5'1" 105lbs for reference. First impressions, these pants are AWESOME. I have a ton of harem and boho type pants but non that are this kind of material. It is almost like a cross between jersey and bathing suit material, is pretty thick compared to similar type pants and has a ton of stretch! The pattern/colors are exactly as shown in the listing. I absolutely LOVE these pants but alas! I cannot give them 5 stars, They are very long and are also too big. The elastic on the waist band is not very tight. The bottom seem where the band meets the pants is the tightest part of the entire waist band and even that to big to stay up. The first photo is where I think and would like the pants to sit, however, the second photo is where they fall. I cannot keep them any higher. They fall down when I walk, not completely, but enough where I caught myself constantly pulling them up. The third picture is me on my tip toes. You can see how the length is pretty perfect at that height but you can see by the other photos how it sits just standing regular. Because of the length, I was also constantly stepping on the bottoms which also pulled them down as well. I am washing them now so I will see if they shrink at all. Hoping they do but I have a feeling they won't due to the material. As is, I really need an XS or XXS in these pants to wear comfortably. If the waist band was actually tight and held them up securely, I could def wear a size small out of the house. But, as is, I can't see myself being able to wear these pants outside due to the constant falling down. So if you are more petite like me, I would pass on these unless they add smaller sizes.

So comfortable and very cool print! These harem pants are 95% polyester 5% spandex and so comfortable!! I am 5ft2in, 191 lbs, 40in waist, 47in hip. I usually order an XL in pants and so that’s what I ordered. I am happy with the XL fit. The waist elastic is very comfortable. They are a little long even with 3in heels on, so I will take them up a couple of inches and they won’t drag the floor. They will be fine for taller gals. They have pockets on the hips that lay flat and don’t stick out making your hips look larger. I washed them in cool water with like colors and tossed them in the dryer on medium heat. No color run and they came out without wrinkles. I ordered the Orangeflower print and there are plenty of colors in that print to choose from to coordinate whether I wear them in the fall or in the spring. I love them! So groovy!

Fit, fabric and pattern all don’t match the advertisement Purchased based on the photos, the green tie dye print (it isn’t actual tie dye) was not matched up right. So there was a litter colored blob above the belly. Real issues are with the fabric. Stretch fabric on the 2XL was clingy not flowy like the photos. It was almost like it had static but it didn’t. The sizing was off. 2XL was almost smaller than the XL. Both fit way too close to the body. I am a size 14 and these fit like a size Large. The inseam is very small if you wear these pants as they should be- with the gathered elastic up high. If I pulled them lower to get the length right, there was sagging in the crotch and in the back. Not flattering. Seller should get better fabric, and make sure of consistency with sizes and fabrics. I am almost 5’9” but these don’t fit well for me when worn as intended. NOT recommended for tall women.

Tall people problems Fit. I am 5’9” and I like to have a 33-34 inch inseam on my pants so that, you know, they are pants! I weigh 150 pounds. I normally like a 29” waist. I bought the medium based on people writing that they are 5’9” and 5’10” and such, and that they fit great. Okay, yes, the waist is comfortable and will fit almost any woman’s body because it is elastic and the fabric is stretchy (I bought the black pair). The legs though, do not fit tall women. The inseam is all the way stretched out here in the photo and it is not even 27 inches. (Inseam is the measurement from the bottom of the crotch to the end of the pant leg). My tall friends, do not order these pants! A lot of ankle and leg will be showing! The measurement from the top of the waist to the crotch is 15 inches, and I would consider them somewhat high waisted. Plenty of room for some “junk in the trunk.” The elastic around the bottom of the leg is not too tight, but may be if you have larger calves. Fabric. Overall, the fabric is soft, not see through, and I can see them fitting great for someone who does not have my freakish long legs. Very comfortable and I debated keeping them for pajamas. Care and washing. The tag says they are hand wash only, lay flat to dry - and that they are 95% rayon and 5% spandex. Made in China. I would recommend these pants to people with normal leg lengths.

It's raining here today, so if I hadn't been here when UPS dropped the pants at my door, rain definitely would have gotten into the packaging and possibly ruined the pants. Great pants, but you're taking a risk with the shipping.

I was skeptical but I read the reviews and glad I did! Needed some comfy - but professional- pants for an interview. I am between a 14 curvy fit to16 regular fit with waist gap so I ordered the 16. They are very comfortable and flattering so I had more confidence going into my interview than I would have had with some ill-fitting, cheaply made pants. They are very good quality for the price and I’m sure I will get quite a few wears out of them. I did not have any kind of waist gap and they stretched enough to go over my curvy hips without looking like I was going to bust out of them like a can of biscuits. The length was perfect - socks weren’t showing when I sat down and I wasn’t walking on them. I could have even worn a little heel with them if I felt like breaking an ankle to make an lasting impression on my interviewers. For reference, I am 5’5 and about 190lbs.

Decent fit but colors ran horrifically in the wash and even stained later loads These fit nicely, but first I want to make sure anyone reading this is aware of what an ordeal it was to wash and clean up after these pants, as the dye ran worse than anything I have ever seen in my life. Full disclosure: I am not 100% sure who is the culprit, as I washed both this pair of pants and a new pair of the Rafaella lightweight satin twill ankle dress pants (also in black) at the same time. I wouldn't have noticed how horrifically they color ran due to the them both being black, except that I had also tossed a brand new light pink bra in a white mesh delicates bag in at the same time - that one's on me, I was just thinking "wash the new stuff before wearing" and not about color bleeding. I used Woolite detergent, and when I pulled the previously white delicates bag out, it was the blue/purple color of ballpoint pen ink (fortunately the bra inside was just a slightly more mauve-y dusty pink, not completely dyed). I soaked the bag and bra, and have gotten some of the dye out, and I washed the pants through two more cycles. Out of an abundance of caution, I ran the washer again in case dye was left in the machine, and put in an old white cleaning rag as a test. It came out purple. I ran it multiple more times with a new white cleaning rag each time, and they eventually got lighter and lighter purple. I ran a load of Tide Laundry Machine cleaner through, and then bought a box of Shout Color Catcher sheets to run through, and after maybe a dozen wash cycles across two days, I'm finally confident I've gotten all the residual dye out and have run a load of regular laundry through with no problems. I'm nervous to wash either pair of pants again, as I'm not sure if I'll have to go through this whole process again or if I've gotten out all the dye that's going to bleed. I've included pictures of my purple rag progression (the mesh bag is also in the photo, but that's after soaking and running it through the wash again), as well as the tags from both pairs of pants where you can see that they are stained purple as well (although it has lightened significantly from the first wash). I'm actually rather glad I had the bra/mesh bag in that same load; even though they got stained, I would have otherwise just tossed a load of regular clothes in next and ruined nearly my entire wardrobe. I left this same warning and photos on a review for the other pants' page as well. But let's talk about the fit of the actual pants! These are very nice, but not perfect pants. I really struggle to find pants that fit my hips and thighs but don't have a horrible gap at the waist - I usually end up cinching it in paper bag style with a belt. For reference, I am 5 foot 3 and ordered a size 14 curvy (sized up from the suggested sizing chart, which would have put me in a 12: 32 waist, 43 hips). These pants fit and flattered my legs nicely, and the waist is a bit loose but manageable. I'll probably move the buttons/hooks over a bit to help. They are quite long on me and will need hemmed, even to wear with heels, but I expected that. I wish the material was a bit thicker - I would describe it as almost midway between dress pant material and tee shirt material. In black, this isn't as much of an issue, but in a lighter color I think it would be somewhat clingy and probably showing off bumps and dimples in ways that I wouldn't like. Overall, I'm satisfied with the pants, but I don't think they were worth the laundry debacle.

Great dress pants! I’m a curvy girl & recently gained some weight. I remembered I had some Rafaella dress pants before that fit well. I ordered 6 of these to try. Note: these pictures are the bootcut, wider leg and come with buttons. They also have narrower legged that I liked, but I chose the wide leg. I prefer the buttons over the hooks on the waist. I tried 12’s & 14’s in straight, bootcut & short. The short length was too short. The 12’s fit perfectly. Weirdly, the black pair is tighter than the navy or vintage wine. I’m 5’6, 156# with the menopause belly & big butt. These pants fit well. I haven’t washed them yet, but I think they will be fine. With the buttons, you can move them over tighter or looser! The buttons are already getting loose on the navy pair. I see seeing in my future. Other than that, they are great. I bought them in 3 colors. Comfy & flattering. I showed you the unflattering pics so you could see the buttons, how the waist fits & the belt I added to hold the navy ones closed. These I actually bought from the manufacturer & they have belt loops. Not sure the Amazon pairs had belt loops.

Elegant, Professional, Great Fit LOVE these pants! I'm 5'2 and 130lbs, and ordered a size 6. The pants fit wonderfully around the waste and legs-- and are wonderfully professional and elegant. The leg's length were a bit too long but I'm a short person so I just wore heels and it was fine. Overall, amazing fit-- worth the money! I'll definitely be buying more pairs.

Flattering relaxed cut These pants are very nice for curvy girls. I have a 31" waist and 41" hips. The cut combined with the stretch is very flattering in the size 10P for me. I think that because these are cut for this specific body shape and with the stretch in the fabric, I could have sized down (normally I size up for my hips). There is a bit of excess fabric at the hips but with a bit of ironing they relax and look great with plenty of room to move. Even though they're a bit big, I love the way that they drape, looking wide and relaxed with a gently graduating boot cut. The finish is nicely tailored and professional. The length is great and measures 29" at the inseam: flats or heels appropriate for me at 5'2". I would still recommend to size down if you're in between. With them being sold as a gabardine trouser, I was expecting them to be a bit more of a classic suiting material. It still has a nice weave to the fabric so I'm pleased with the finish. In all, these Rafaella pants are good quality and excellent value and my bum feels great in them!

Comfortable and stretchy I purchased these for work. I’m 150 pounds and bottom heavy but these pants fit me well. They’re very soft and stretchy. I love the deep pockets. I wish the legs were longer but I don’t think that’s worth losing a star.

Waist is way off. Sizing is not consistent. So I love that these slacks are actually long enough. I’m 5’9 so the long length is nice. The material is good too. BUY THESE These are so nice! I wasnt sure how i was expecting them to fit but they fit amazingly and they go all the way down to my ankles and im 5’10. Perfect little pocket, unisex, good fit, durable, phone friendly pockets, looks sharp I ordered the bright green for the color. These are true unisex. I'm a 75 year old man. I could find essentially the same cargos marketed for men. Button and zipper on same side. The men's version don't come in good colors. I like these cause elastic in waist so don't need a belt. They come in my length 29". I don't need to alter. After I wore a few time: Very comfortable, my huge cell phone in wallet case will fit in the 4 front pockets, also good for glasses case. I like the buttons on the cargo pockets. I keep the inner one buttoned and my phone does not fall out. Much better than velcro. Don't use rear or calf pockets. No more fanny pack. After a few weeks I bought the camouflaged for working around the house. Don't want to stain the green. I do a lot of DIY. Sitting on the ground I appreciate the room in the seat and crotch. Sometimes my pants will get wet like washing cars with spray hose. That's when I discovered my key fob with house keys fit easily in the little zipper pocket. NOT JUST A ORNAMENTAL DECORATION Very functional. The pants do well in the wash. The green has not faded. Just shake them out when taken out of the dryer hot. Looks good for going to doctor or market. My wife irons the green if I going to wear them to church. Now I'm looking at the women's dress/business trousers. Their almost identical to the pleated suit pants I wore 30 years ago. Just looking for the right fabric. I would like tropical wool.

Not too shabby Overall I'm pleasantly surprised. They are a sturdy/thick material and seem to be well made. The length is a bit too high-watery for me but that's par for the course being 5'7". Some day I'll find pants thar crumple up a little on top of my kicks like all my short(er) friends... but until that day comes: the option to tie the bottom of the pant leg is my saving grace. The pants sit pretty much waste high, which has been my overwhelming preference from about age 33 onward. Overall a decent purchase. Oh and they're very comfortable too.

Love these pants! I have big time love for these pants, I have three different pairs: green camo, black, and the cow print ones. I have a size 6 in the green camo and the cow ones, and a size 4 in the black ones. The fit isn't super consistent, which would be my only complaint about these pants, because the two pairs I have that are 6's fit me differently. The waistband has some elastic in it, so they're stretchy enough to be super comfy without that elastic being worn out and too loose by the end of the day. I can wear these for 14+ hours and still be just as comfortable as anything, I've worn them to travel in, and I work in them constantly. The pockets!! There are 9 of them, all completely functional and very spaciously sized. Like the front pockets are deep enough to get my whole hand down to my forearm in them. I've never used the little pockets towards the bottom of the pants, but I like the way they look anyway. The side pockets are big enough for a ten inch tablet to easily slide into them, and the bottoms of the pants have ties on them so you can wear them loose or tie them and wear them more like a pair of joggers. I think they're versatile enough that they can be cute, or they can just be your new favorite work pants. I. Love. These. Pants.

I find they run true to size First, I cant believe I am sharing such candid pics but I feel us thicker ladies never can tell what the right size is to buy so I hope this helps y'all. (This is my dads acct btw) Ok, to I am 5'6" and currently weight 182 lbs, all of my weight is in my tummy and thighs, I am only a 38B. I go between a 12 and 14 in jeans. I'm pretty sure I remembered some of the reviews saying to size up. I originally ordered size 16 bc I didnt want them skin tight, but they were WAY to big. Sent those back and tried a 12. PERFECT. I can still easily get in and out of the front pockets and I don't feel squished by the waistband when I sit down. I am absolutely getting more of these in different colors so long as they don't shrink in the washer and dryer. If that happens I will for sure update. Good luck ladies!

Perfect fit & Functional Bought these pants for hiking, sightseeing, and horseback riding because I needed pants with deep pockets and lots of them. Shipping was quick. The gray is a medium gray with warm undertones. Now for the real deets: I'm 5' 4" 120lbs. Got size 6. Fits PERFECT. The elastic waistband is very forgiving and comfortable, the rise is just right. Not too low on the hips, not too high that it digs into the ribs. Pockets are deep and plentiful. Appreciated the drawstring tie at the ankles. The material itself feels like a soft brushed, medium-weight canvas. They were very comfortable to wear all day long. I liked these so much that I bought a black pair a month later. Same fit and quality. The black is pure black, and thankfully not a washed out black. Definitely worth the purchase. Here's a noteworthy endorsement: My grown son, who's not afraid to give honest feedback, thought I looked bad@ss!

Thick/Good quality I am 5’6.5 and 120/125lbs I typically wear a size 0/2 in women’s jeans. I ordered the pink cargos in a size 2 and they fit perfect. Great Pants, but some Caveats I've owned two different lengths of these pants, and have learned some things you'll want to know before you buy. First of all, the material on these is great. They feel like professional grade pants, unlike most pull on pants of this style that are made with a cottony material, that while comfy, is also a lot like sweatpants in a different cut, and just feel very casual to me for going to the office. This material still has a lot of stretch to it, and the strength of the material plus the high waist keeps me "tucked in" around the middle, and not in an uncomfortable way. These pants do not have real pockets, so if you want those, move along. These are slim fit, so pockets would create lumps and bumps, so I'm okay letting that go for these. The biggest unknown when it comes to these pants is the length. The Regular size is listed as 32" and the Tall size is listed as 35". I am 5' 8 1/2" tall, and have issues with pants being too short, but long ones being too long. The Regular size look fine if I'm in flats and standing, but the stretchy nature of these pants means that when you sit, they move quite a long ways up your leg. That's why I included a picture of me with my leg up in the air like a stork. For the Tall length pants, 35" in any other pant would be WAY too long for me, and they are definitely long when standing, I have to have some heels on (though you can see in the picture, the heels don't have to be crazy tall). But when I sit in the Tall pants, they are the right length. For me, sitting at my desk at work in the Regular size pants, the short length drove me batty. My socks poke out, and they are super high water, so I'd rather have the long size and wear some heels with them. This is my personal preference. Also, they are quite slim fit, I am wearing a size 8 and normally I wear a size 6 in pants. I am 135 lbs. I hope this review helps you with these pants. I have several pairs now that I've dialed down the sizing I like.

Stylish & practical I love the cut and style of these stretchy pants! I bought a size up due to the potential for shrinkage if someone else did the laundry. I also didn't want a "muffin top" created by a tight waist band. They fit great--and if they shrank--they'd still fit. They have a wide waist band with belt loops. Sit nicely at at the hip (I'm short waisted).All the pockets are fake, but nicely detailed. I am 5'4", weigh about 145-150, and I ordered a size 12. Yes, I could've ordered a 10 and feel confident they would fit even better, but I can't risk the potential for shrinkage and muffin top. They were long, but I just folded them under the exact width of the existing hem and hemmed them myself. I wash in cold water and hang dry. The ONLY drawback (but worth it in my opinion) is they do need to be ironed on LOW (synthetic). If it's not a low heat, you risk melting your pants. The back pockets look puckered after washing, but once you iron them and wear them, you don't see the puckers. Also, the pants stretch back into form, meaning they don't stretch out and look saggy. These pants are light weight, perfect for warm/hot climates. Very slimming. Who doesn't like that?

They were long, but I just folded them under the exact width of the existing hem and hemmed them myself. I wash in cold water and hang dry. The ONLY drawback (but worth it in my opinion) is they do need to be ironed on LOW (synthetic). If it's not a low heat, you risk melting your pants. The back pockets look puckered after washing, but once you iron them and wear them, you don't see the puckers. Also, the pants stretch back into form, meaning they don't stretch out and look saggy. These pants are light weight, perfect for warm/hot climates. Very slimming. Who doesn't like that?

¯\_(ツ)_/¯ I am always on the hunt for comfortable work pants that are flattering to my figure. I love the color, the cut and style are cute and flattering. Unfortunately, these fell short for me for a few reasons. First of all, size wise these fit as expected and are flattering my to curves. I wear size 8 pants normally and the 8 fit good, except, there is no stretch in the waist band, which I would really expect in a pair of pants with no zipper/button. There is almost no give in the waistband, so it makes it awkward when pulling on over the hips. Second, the material is pretty scratchy. The picture looks like it would be a softer material but it's really more of a stiff polyester. And finally, this is more an issue I have with my shorter stature (5'5") these pants are too long. I would need to get them altered, which, with pants at this price, not really worth it. Again, cute pants, flattering cut...but the comfort just wasn't there for me.

These are my new go-to dress slacks! This is my first shot at trying the yoga pant style work slacks and OH MY STARS THESE ARE INCREDIBLE. I am 5'8, 160 lbs and typically wear size 10 American eagle jeans. The sizing chart dictated to order a size 12. I ordered four different pair of slacks from Rekucci. They look just like a pair of dress slacks, not like a pair of yoga pants or pajamas. Rekucci comfort fit pants will definitely be my new go to for dress slacks. You just can't beat this kind of quality, especially for, what, $33?

Nice dress slacks Update: So I purchsed a 14 regular and a 14 long hoping those would give me the shrinkage room i need to get a nice 12. Huh! Jokes on me. The 14L, although much longer in length, fit a smidge SMALLER than the 12 and are tight in the knee! The 14R fit about the SAME as the 12 with this kicker-they about 2 inches shorter!!! So no shrinkage room. Maybe the 16?? I'll try it but do i the regular or the tall? Hmm, Can't win. Too bad as they are nice work slacks.Edit: they shrink, in and up, a lot. I lost probably 2 inches in both directions. Don't let the polyester feel of these slacks fool you. And read the washing instructions. And they still wrinkle. A lot. Which is too bad because they are a great work pant!!Slacks fit great, very comfortable. I did size up 1 size (normally a 12, bought a 14). A little snug in thigh but not uncomfortablely so. I'm 5'7 and hem sits just above my boot sole. Difficult to describe the material....maybe a little too polyester-ish and they wrinkle a LOT after one wear. Overall, a nice pair of slacks and i would buy again.

Fell in love I didn’t like them much when I first tried them on. I felt they were way too long. I’m in the over 50 age range, I’m 5’1” I think my inseam is 27 (or 28”) and about 200 lbs. I bought a 16 Short. They seemed to have snugged up after the cold water wash. I did put them in the dryer with the other laundry. I lack the desire (read “patience”, lol) to hang up clothes to dry. I probably should have went up a size, but I’ve lost 30lbs and am working still working on it. I also dislike it when pants become baggy/saggy during the day; and these did not. I did feel like I had to pull them up here and there through out the day, so I may wear a belt next time. The tummy control could be better, but I could also stop eating cake, so there’s that. Overall, I’m ordering another pair as we speak because I really do like how comfortable they are. I may try an 18 and return them if they’re too big. Hahaha, you see that? I made a joke! I’m not gonna stinking return anything (said in my ‘Granny’ voice) lol. I’ll just have to eat more cake so they’ll fit better.

Silver looks like Green!! I’m 5’7 145lbs and I order a size 10. They fit as expected, they are soft and look good always. I have about 4 colors and they are consistent in size and length. I took a star off because in person the silver looks like a light olive kahki green. It doesn’t look silver. I posted a picture with my leg next to a silver bowl so you can get some sense but the picture doesn’t do it justice. These pants are more comfortable than jammies, I prefer wearing them over jeans. This same company puts out another non tummy control version of these that are exactly the same except they are actually boot cut as opposed to straight leg. After ordering about 4 pairs of each, I realized this was the only difference between them. But they don’t tell you in the description or give you an option to buy straight leg vs boot cut. The boot cut is a wider leg, but the straight leg makes me legs look more slender all the way down and fit over the top of my heels too. I just wish they had an actual very light grey in size 10. They need an option to wait list until those colors come in again. The pretty light blue is also not available in my size. But otherwise I lovse these pants!

Cute, comfortable, perfect fit! I just received these and they fit perfect! I usually wear DressBarn secret agent straight leg pants in 2 petite. They haven’t had khaki since last year when I was still losing weight and I was in petite 4. They only have black, charcoal, and navy in the similar straight leg DressBarn petite pants. I was on a mission to find comparable pants and I searched every where! I found these and I was so excited! I waited a while and I finally decided to purchase. I could probably fit a 2 but they are for work and I don’t want them skin tight. These have a nice stretch and nice thickish fabric. I ordered these in 4 short! For reference I’m 5’0 and weigh 120 lbs. I’m worried I may put them in the dryer by mistake but I’m taking the risk! I already can’t wait to order them in more colors!! EDIT: I bought these in April 2019, its now August and I've been wearing every week and washing and drying along with all of my other clothes and have not had any issues with the seams at all!! *knock on wood* I would definitely recommend these to anyone looking for comfortable pants that work with a business casual dress code.

These pants fir nice but requires ironing. . Disappointed in these pants although the fit is nice the size was perfect and the length just right for my 5”7 150 body. The problem is the elastic in the in the waist band is very thin and after first wash it rolled needs to be ironed to straighten the waist band ,the legs were difficult to iron they don’t lay flat or straight to iron crease. Great! I'm about 5'2", 135 lbs... I ordered an 8 short and they fit perfectly! The length is just past my ankle, not too long. And the cloth is just stretchy enough to hug curves without being too tight. And the fit across the butt is nice, no baggy butt! I like that the waist is fairly medium in height. I only ordered a few pair to make sure they fit ok, but I will be ordering more. I think I will get the slight boot cut next time though as the legs are a bit skinnier than I expected.

Hole on Day 1 I had such high hopes for these pants. Today was the first day I wore them, so here's my review. I ordered my regular size that I would order at any other store...the fit is okay - a bit snug in the thigh/rear area. They are also quite long. I can't remember if there is a 'long/tall' size, but I just ordered the regular inseam and found that they were quite long (I am 5'7" for reference). If you normally wear your pants with heels, then this may not be an issue for you. The material is thin, so they may not keep you very warm this winter, but I don't find that it looks too cheap or anything. The problem with these pants is that I found a small hole in the leg with the threading already coming undone (see photo). I know you get what you pay for but I was expecting them to hold up for at least a few wears. Would not order again.

Hang them up to dry. Very happy with my purchase, the price is fair for the quality.

Too many flaws for price tag I have navy and silver in this pant, I wear a size 6, 140 lb 5,9. I do really like these pants but there are definitely too many flaws for the price tag on these. - silver pair has a weird bunchy seam in the butt, also in the crotch area which makes it uncomfortable to wear without underwear -i had to send the first pair of these back bc they had a run in the fabric -the length shrinks if dried but that's not a huge deal - I have to be careful what I wash with the navy pair, it will get weird white sections on them that I have to take a damp rag to after drying and I'm not sure what is causing that. Nice but threads coming undone after one wash After one wear, my impression is that I really like this product in general. Comfy, yet professional-looking. I’m 5’4 and 145-150 lbs and got a size 10 short. I had to tug them up once in awhile, but not enough for it to be annoying. They were tight enough to stay up, but not tight enough to cause muffin top. I wish they were just a tad longer, because the short bootcut only looked decent with flats or sandals. I’m sure I’d have to hem the regulars, though. Also, panty lines are really visible with the light gray color. My main complaint is that the stitching at the top is coming undone after one wash in the delicate cycle. I didn’t even put them in the dryer. Which, by the way, they take forever to line dry... Even Walmart pants hold up better than that. I expected more from a $40 pair.

So I ordered another pair size regular 12 in silver and they are way too long even after washing and way too tight! So I think the color makes a difference in the sizing for some reason.

I’m adding this info for future buyers because I myself like to read a lot of reviews before buying clothes lol.I’m 5’7” and fluctuate between 145lbs - 150lbs. I bought a size 8 (regular length). I’ve recently gained weight so I don’t know my true size right now, I just ordered one size up from what I used to be. I still fit in Free People size 28 jeans, if that’s of any help to anyone.These jeans fit comfortably enough BUT they’re also pretty snug so if they shrink in the dryer they probably won’t fit me anymore. I honestly think I could’ve gone with a size 10 in case they do end up shrinking.. I’m still considering exchanging them. I haven’t washed or worn them out yet, so I don’t know how well they’ll hold up. I will say that the material seems to be thicker and better quality than I expected. They have some stretch but not enough to be considered that weak jegging material (I don’t like), they actually feel like jeans. I also like the length, they’re not short and not too long, as you can see in the picture (I’m not wearing shoes). As for the rise of the waist, I'd say it's mid-rise because it sits about an inch below my belly button. They're definitely not low-rise on me, which I'm glad about because I hate low-rise.All in all, they seem like a pretty good pair of jeans, and if my first impression is correct, they’ll be sturdy and last a decent while.I hope my review helped someone out!

Comfort, Good Fit for Petite This is my first time ordering jeans online because I can never seem to find ones that fit me even at the store. I'm 5 2", 120 pounds, and ordered a size: 8 Petite. The attached photo is how they fit. It's pretty good, except I had to fold in the hem (so I will be fixing that myself, yup! even in petite size), but that is a minor thing) the rest of the fit is good. They are comfortable. I'm not really having the same issue as others with the low-back, though they are a low-rise for sure. Mine lay about an inch below my belly button. I'm comfortable with that. If you're on the edge, definitely worth trying these since they're easy to return.

Sized incorrectly, fits me like painted on yoga pants I like the quality of these Indigo Riders by Lee. The fabric is soft and very stretchy, and the stitching is nice. Under normal circumstances I would love these pants, but the pair I was sent is seriously sized wrong. It is labeled as a size 12 on both the fabric label inside and the paper label outside, but it is not a 12. I’m currently in a size 10 (used to be in a size 12 a few months ago) and this pair fits me like a tight pair of yoga pants. It is really, really tight. I compared it to other pants in my closet (none of them Lee) and it narrower in the hips and waist then the size 8 that I keep around just in case I lose some more weight. Another sizing issue was the length. I’m 5’9” and these pants were actually a little long on me.So nice quality, but I think I got a size 6/8 and not the size 12 that I ordered.In the picture below the brown pants at the bottom of the stack are a size 12 by Investment, the white pair is a size 10 (a generous size 10) by Kasper, and the black stipples pair is a size 8 by Raffella. You can see that the size 12 Lee is smaller than even the size 8.

Stretchy, soft and comfortable. These wide leg, high waisted jeans are very comfortable. They fit me well, except because of my shortness (5' 4") they are not really cropped on me. More like flood pants, but I am okay with that. I do not have to worry about the bottoms getting wet or my lower legs getting too cold, lol. They have three buttons that are quite stiff to unbutton when you get them but are fine after the first time. The zipper works well. There are four functional pockets, two in front and two in back. The pockets are a little smallish, but would hold a tissue or car keys. As you can see in one of my pictures, if you are apple shaped like I am, the white of the inside of the pocket (on the front) will show, despite the fit being not too tight. Not a problem for me as I most always like to wear shirts / blouses that are long enough to cover at least most of my bottom. Not sure how the pocket issue could have been prevented in the designing of them, but there probably is a way. These jeans seem to be mostly well made. The only thing I noticed really was that the buttons are sort of the cheap type that wiggle and rattle. Not sure what the durability would be of those. Aside from that, I can recommend these jeans and I am happy with them. The reason I only gave four stars was for the buttons and the white of the pocket showing. If anything comes up about those buttons on mine, I will update my review.

Ridiculously wide legs, inseam shorter than given on size chart I ordered these in the “Blue” color in size Medium. I typically wear a size 8 in pants, but the size chart could have had me in a Large instead. However, the listing says the model is “wearing size Medium, Hips 40 / Height 5’3” / Weight 136. I’m about an inch and half taller, weigh about the same, and hips are maybe an inch smaller. The Medium fits me very well and is comfortable. The inseam is at least an inch shorter than the stated 26.38” (I measured it at about 25”). The fabric has a nice weight and is soft and stretchy. The waistband is beautiful and also comfortable. It seems like it would retain its size and shape, however, I have not worn them long enough to test this theory. There are no belt loops to hold them up should they stretch out, or if they would be too loose on you. The buttons seem a little loose, but don’t show any indication of coming off.Now, I know these are advertised as wide leg, but they have W-I-D-E legs! Since they are denim which has structure, If I stand with my feet about hip width apart (a natural stance), the legs of these jeans cross over each other, which looks rather ridiculous. The seams go crooked and it looks like I'm not standing straight even though I am. I have to stand with my legs spread strangely apart for them to look as they do on the model. If only I could slim down the legs, these would be attractive, but as they are, I just can’t see myself ever wearing them, not even just around the house, as the legs crash together with every step. I’ve included photos of how they look on me (5’4.5”, 30 waist, 39 hip, about 29 inseam), and also some with a measuring tape to show the actual measurements of the size Medium. I hope this helps in your decision.

Love These (Very) Long and Stretchy Jeans! These jeans are super cute! They’re also super long. In my image I’m wearing these with almost 3” heeled platform clogs. But I love the look - I think it’s super slimming. I’m 5’5,” 165lbs, waist is 35,” hips are 44” and my thighs are 23” around. I’m also very short-waisted. Per the size guide I ordered the XL. Because these are SUPER stretchy, I’m wondering if I could’ve gone down a size. I can literally pull these on with unzipping them. However, I don’t mind that at all. It’s also difficult to find high waisted jeans that are flattering on me because I’m so short waisted. I really like the rise on these. I’ll see how these do after I wear them for a day, out and about and also after washing. If they don’t stretch out too much or shrink, I’ll stick with this size. If anything changes, I’ll update my review. For now, I’m very happy and I do recommend these jeans. I’m almost 60 yrs old, but I think these would appeal to a broad range of ages.

I love the three buttons and thick band at waistline. They fit well with no gaping at the waist. Bottom opening is very wide and length was perfect. I always expect jeans to be crazy long as I am only 5'3" but these are the perfect length to pair with my boots. I was pleasantly surprised with how deep tge front pockets are as most women's jeans have pretty useless front pockets. The ONLY complaint I had was with the ay the denim portion is cut on the front pockets. I noticed that the denim is cut very close to pocket opening so that you can actually see the stitching where denim meets pocket liner. Not a deal breaker, but I felt myself constantly readjusting that pocket (see last photo).

Not flattering I feel like a frumpy old lady wearing these pants. They run large. My hip measurement is 40" and I got a size 8 (sizing down one because I read they run big) and they are still kinda loose, even after washing and drying. They are well tapered to the waist for me though, and my natural waist measurement is 25". As I walk, the pants bunch up, pulling away from my ankles. I'm 5'-5" and if the pants didn't bunch up, they would otherwise be the perfect length for wearing with flats. I think they are too short to wear with heels. The pants are extra loose at the knee. I would describe the fit as similar to relaxed fit jeans. The fabric is very stretchy, but also very thick yet lightweight at the same time, and prone to wrinkles. The fabric feels cheap. The pants are reasonably comfortable though.

The good: The pants look great. I'm excited to wear these to work. The sizing is perfect. The 10P fit me perfectly: waist=30'', hips= 40.5'', inseam= 26.5''. They feel well made. The BAD: However, with all the good, I can't give this 5/5. I'm very disappointed in how these were shipped to me. These arrived by themselves in a clear plastic bag with the shipping label holding the bag's open end "closed". The pants were totally viewable to anyone delivering or receiving the package. Not that I am ashamed of what size I bought, it's the principle of the matter. Someone else may be VERY upset that these arrived this way. Or what if these were a gift for someone in my household? Had I known that these pants would be shipped in a package that identifies what it is, I would not have purchased them. I mean a plastic flimsy bag just waiting to be ripped along the perforations? Come on! Also, the hanger is still on the pants and they were folded over the hook of the hanger (so the hanger doesn't rip through the filmy plastic bag).

Great pants true to picture, Black pair came dirty and used I love the pants! They’re a great length for me I’m 5’2 and wanted them relaxed fit and a longer to wear around the beach so I got them in the XL. The fabric is great! Really comfortable and light. Now for the issue... I ordered two a black and white and the white ones are perfect! Great condition, the black ones are in good condition as far as fabric and fit and length very true to the modeled picture however, I clearly received a used and returned pair because there’s a hard powdered substance on the leg of the black pair both front leg and back. The front stains seem to be almost like dried oatmeal while the other stains on the back seem to be like a powder makeup/foundation stain and the fact that someone even returned them like that is just gross to me and completely rude in my opinion, so with that said I highly recommend these pants for a great cover up for summer and I think the seller needs to do a better check of retuned items before selling them out to a new customer. I give this a 3 star despite the conditions of the black pants because the white pants came new and perfect condition, as seen in picture.

Thick, comfortable and a little long for me These are very nice, thicker jeans that are more comfortable than I thought they would be right out of the package. I am 5'4", 115 lbs. for reference and the size 26 fits me, although they are just the slightest bit snug around my waist. Thankfully they have a touch of spandex stretch in them so they work. The zip fly protrudes a tiny bit on me as you can hopefully see in the picture so it isn't perfectly smooth to wear under a more fitted top that might cover the waist. The inseam is definitely a little long on me, but I put on a 2 inch wedge with these and they could be wearable with a heel, but barely. I will probably need to have them hemmed up an inch or so, but overall, with the wide leg flare at the base, I would say these are pretty nice jeans that are definitely on the dressier side of things. It would be nice if they offered a petite or ankle version of these jeans.

my experience with these yoga pants i get these pants for my morning yoga classes and i have to say, they’re quite nice. here are my thoughts on it: advantages: fabric is so soft, i feel like i’m wearing a cloud. very stretchy, so it move with me while i do my exercises, no tight feeling at all. high waistband is great, it hold everything in place even when i bend or stretch. love the wide leg style, give me relaxed vibes, also make me look taller (even though i’m not). cons: length is a bit tricky, the shorter size is still little long for me (i’m 5’4”). after one wash, i notice tiny bit of shrinkage but still okay. overall, these pants are comfy for lounging and also good for yoga or even quick grocery trips. i would recommend if you looking for something that’s flexible and casual.

Almost perfect These pants are a near dupe of the old navy mid rise pixie full length pant. Great length (I'm 5'7), and four REAL (albiet small) POCKETS! I say nearly perfect because there are some minor flaws. They are not quite as stretchy and comfortable as I had hoped. They are slightly too high in the front which makes them bunch in an unflattering way when seated. They are slightly too low in the back so when you sit there is definite whale tale potential if you're not careful (might not be an issue for those with smaller butts). That said, they are better than 90% of dress pants I have tried, and cheaper than nearly 100%. If they don't fade or wear out, I'll probably buy more colors.

Better than I expected I was pleasantly impressed with these pants. I am 5'6", 29" waist, 40" (39") hips, 136lbs. These feel a bit like parachute pants. They are 17% nylon after all. But they do have a good amount of stretch to them. I wish they were a little more supportive in the rear and that might have been achievable maybe one size down if I thought they were really stretchy enough for that sort of feature but that's just wishful thinking for a forty-something y/o whose assets aren't where they used to be. The waistband is very comfortable, the length is just where I want it, and I think they look really good on my body. As for my measurements, I am 5'6" but I have short-ish legs and a long torso - specifically my "rise". Like, high-rise pants fit me more like mid-rise because there is over eleven inches between my crotch and my bely button. I mention this because these pants are a little tight in the crotch for me and have a little camel toe effect but the waist is so forgiving, I can adjust and pull them down whenever I feel them creeping up in there;) Plus they are black and it's not noticeable. I don't think that would be the case with lighter colors though which is a bummer because I like these pants so much I'm tempted to buy them in all the colors. Anyway, lots of reviews complaining about how they don't hold up to washing but it clearly states that these pants are best dry cleaned. I never dry clean anything so I'll probably wash on cold in the gentle or hand wash cycle and hang to dry. I included front (heeled and flat footed), back, and side pics because I know that even someone with the same measurements as me may have a different shape. Hope this info helps.

My daughter loves these pants and looks forward to wearing them once the weather improves.

Cute warmer weather slacks These pants fit fairly nicely. I wear a US 8 in jeans, and the waist is not snug (I received a M). I’m not a huge fan of the elastic band at the waist, but it isn’t too tight. The inseam is long enough for me. I’m 5’4”, but have longer legs. The crotch area leaves a little to be desired. I don’t have a butt, but if I did these pants would be riding up in places that I don’t want them riding up. The material is flowy, comfortable, and not see through, but I don’t feel that these would be as cool as lighter weight materials in the high heat of the middle of summer. They are a cotton/linen blend (70/30) so maybe they might be okay in the high heat? I just don’t know that answer for sure. I feel that since they are slightly thicker than what I was expecting, that they would be great from very early spring through mid fall - depending on the climate where you live. They are thick enough for me to wear in temperatures in the 60°F (15°C). They are well made. I love that they have pockets, and the color is perfect. It’s like a mix of denim and teal. Overall, I would recommend, but if you have wider hips, a butt, or thicker thighs, there might be an issue in the crotch area. Check other reviews and find people who are similar in body type to see how it works for them.

Super comfy and better than I expected! I am loving the weight of these cargo pants. The cotton is really soft and light enough to work as a wide-legged pant, but still feels durable. The waist has a wide, stretchy elastic band as well as a drawstring tie that can be adjusted for a comfy fit. The legs of these pants run pretty wide, as the pictures show. You can wear them open or cinch them at the ankle with an adjustable elastic tie. And like most cargo pants, there are more than enough pockets here. For reference, I am 5'5 with a 29" waist, and I ordered the medium. For me, the length was just right.

Runs large, I think? So first off, I'm 5'6", 165lbs. I have long legs and a short torso, making it difficult to find comfortable pants that fit well. I first bought the 33" inseam Medium and the waist was too long and wide. It was a comfortable fit if I rolled the waist band down, so I figured I'd try the small. Great fit! I like how thick the material is, and that there are pockets. It doesn't squeeze my midsection in an unflattering way, and the length is perfect for my legs. I'm so happy I found pants that are flattering, comfortable, and sooo stretchy! ***edit- these pants ended up shrinking in the dryer (I know they're not supposed to be dried in the dryer). So they are now too short. Also, I ended up finding the pockets were a bit annoying as they are not sewn in, they bunch up on the inside. I ended up buying the bubble lime yoga pants instead and am happier with these.

Serviceable but Don't Love It I feel that this runs a little large in comparison to the size chart. I just ordered my usual size instead, M, and overall the fit is ok and matches up to a typical M, I am just a bit too short & chubby for these to fit right. I am 5'3", 38-33-38 and the hem is about 3 inches too long, rise is too long for my taste (definitely a high waist on me when I would have preferred mid-rise). This is a very fine-boned corduroy fabric (see pic) and it does not stretch a lot apart from the elastic waist, so it's more like real pants than stretchy leggings. You may possibly want to order up and think of it more like baggy comfy pants? Or it may fit better on someone with a trim body. These won't fit well if you have large thighs (mine are 20" and I feel that they're about the max) or bootie, I think they will drag down on you. Overall these will probably work the best on someone with a 30-32" inseam & a slim-rectangle kind of body type. Looking at other reviewers that seems to match up with who was more happy with these pants. Really nice color selection (I was so tempted by both the greens but am surprisingly lacking in plain black pants) & they look good but they're a bit ill-fitting for me on comfort.

Fun and comfortable These are comfortable pants with a good amount of stretch to them. My wife wasn’t sure what to expect with these pants but she is happy with them. The waist is elastic, but there are no belt loops and no front pockets. For reference she is 5’4 and about 120lbs and likes how the small fits her. They are a high waist fit and the length is just right, just barely touched the floor without shoes on. Overall she likes them. The color is a little more golden brown than khaki in person, but she still likes it and thinks they are versatile. The fit is flattering and comfortable. The quality could be better but is okay for the price but she thinks the front pockets and belt loops would add a lot to them. I hope this assists you with your shopping. Tall plus Girl Okay so first off I didn’t pay for expedited and it still came the next day! When it says earliest delivery date, most times Amazon pushes through and it’s COVID-19 so I’m shocked. I am 6 foot I weigh 280 pounds and I’m thick thighs no butt. For the thick thighs women with no butt is best to get your exact size because we want to also flatter our butts just as much as are thighs. If your thighs are bigger than your butt or vice versa to an extreme amount probably get one size bigger but it may be a little loose. I like tighter fit jeans in my thigh area to complement my hips and thighs also because I have a small butt once again lol! And you also know the more you wear jeans they stretch. So I know that my thighs will start to make room for themselves if I need any more LOL.These are perfect. They stretch but you can tell There’s some denim in it because it doesn’t have an extreme stretch. These are not jeggings! They are the regular polyester plus size material for big girls I just have jeans are being right now with a little stretch but I still denim feel! I don’t mind that lip space under the abdomen because I know my thighs are the reason why! Also it goes all the way down ! No high water !!!!

Love them but elastic is off My first darker Jean pair was perfect. They are so comfy and flattering. I am 5’6”, 155lbs with wider hips/butt and the Medium fit perfectly. I returned to by the orange and then the black. The orange elastic doesn’t stay in place. It constantly folds over on itself. I’m constantly fussing with it to open it back up. I have the black on today for the first time and I can’t tell if the elastic is folded over or it’s just too thin. Something is definitely off. I added a picture for reference. These pants are not expensive but they also are not dirt cheap. I expect better quality for $35. Esp when you can get decent quality jeans at Target for $20.

Almost perfect I like how stretchy the material is. Thick enough. I love the flare as well. For your reference: I am 5'2", US Medium. 160 lbs. Somethings can be improved in the design. Adding some side pockets would be really nice because it would really make it look like real pants instead of jeggings with elastic waistband. I kind of like to tuck my shirt in my pockets. The waistband is a bit too tight. These are high-waist pants and it being so tight around the waist is restricting because I have GERD it messes up my stomach because it pushes my stomach too much. For that reason I can't wear it as much as I want to. If the waist band is at least 2 inches loose, these would be comfortable. I like that the hemline is raw so I was able to cut these to match my height and not worry about sewing. Because I am short, the hole is not hitting me at the knees, but it doesn't bother me. The only thing I really would say are not very good at not having pockets and the waistline being super tight. Other than that, I would have give these 5 stars because they are cute! If you are tall and skinny, you would love these!

Buy the jeans! Super cute and fit just right! The stretch in the waistband covers and sucks in my mama pooch, which makes my waist and butt flattering in these jeans! I’m 5’5”, 160 pounds, & I usually wear a 29/30 waist in jeans. I ordered a medium and they fit spot-on, the length was perfect for if I’m wanting to wear flats or a small wedge. This is after a wash on cold and medium heat dry, which I do with all my jeans. The knee slits aren’t super noticeable unless sitting or kneeling, but I wasn’t worried about the distressing. The downside is if you want front pockets, but I hardly use front pockets so it didn’t break the deal for me. I’m so glad I bought these jeans, worth the money for sure!

Overall nice pants I am 5’5” and 187 lbs for reference. Just took these out of the package today, and the first thing I noticed was some sort of animal hair on both legs. Which has nothing to do with the fit, but is still a little weird to me. I ordered an XL to be safe, and I probably could have gone with the Large because they aren’t as fitted as I would like in the stomach or thighs. So if you are about like me in size and like a more relaxed fit, the XL would be great. I’m hoping I can dry them and shrink them a little. Also the “distressing” (slits in the knees) don’t come up quite high enough because they don’t hit my knees, so maybe I’m not tall enough. But I feel like they’re a good length because I plan to wear heeled booties with them. But overall they’re nice and if I buy the dark denim pair I’ll probably get a Large.

Best jeans ever! LOVE LOVE LOVE!!! I am a big fan of flair/bell bottom jeans. I loved them back in the 90s and early 00s and I still love them. It's irritating that stores don't sell them. I don't care what's in fashion, I care about what I like and how it looks on me. I was a little apprehensive to buy these because of the elastic waist but I did it anyways and bought 2 pair because I need flairs back in my life again. Once I tried them on, the elastic was out of my mind. They are a little long but that's how I like my jeans. I am 5'4" and 162 lbs. and bought a large.. Favorite jeans ever. I will wear them probably every single day this fall and winter season. And spring as well. Probably summer too. Buy them. You won't regret it.

Bigger than 2xl don’t bother So the jeans showed up damaged but that’s not even the issue really my issue is anything above a 2xl doesn’t have prime so it took 6 weeks to get to me which is insane. Then it got here damaged and instead of being able to return it by the UPS ppl scanning a QR code I now have to go to a staples and print the shipping label (bc who tf owns a printer anymore) and then mail it out which will more than likely take at least a week before I get my money back so we are looking at almost 2 months of them having my money for nothing. I also ordered a size 2xl with prime and I love them but I refuse to make my rating higher because of it simply on the principal that yet again another company is inconveniencing ppl because they’re fat. Same fatphobia different company, smh. So yeah if you’re above a 2xl or bellow 170lb don’t waste your time. Side note I see a lot of stick thin women giving these pants bad reviews because they’re “baggy” on them that is because they are elastic waist with stretch they are meant for big girls if you want cute bell bottoms that will fit you get classic denim with a button closure! I’m 203lbs and 5” 2’ and the 2XL fits great

Runs slightly large I am about 165 lbs @ 5'3, measure 36DD-31-41, and wear a sz 12 in most jeans. I bought these in a size 12. I like the overall feel and style of the pants, the length, and that the waistband doesn't pinch or cause love handles. However, they are slightly too big overall. They are a bit baggy through the back of the legs and butt in a less than flattering way, and I can easily pull the waistband away from my body at least an inch. I don't want to have to worry about them loosening throughout the day, and maybe putting them in the dryer will shrink them, but I'd rather just try on the next size down.

Nice quality, cute and comfortable! For reference, I'm 5'3" 145lbs and thick in the thighs. The size 8 fits perfectly! They seem to be good quality. I love the high waist and they are comfortable - bonus they are cute. Haven't noticed any stretch after wearing several hours. Love them!

Short, not capris length At 5’7”tall these are more like clam digger length on me rather than capris length. I’m also not in love with the fit because while the waist fits me perfectly (for reference 127 lbs 27” waist ordered size 4) there’s some rather odd bunching going on in the hip, leg and butt area that’s not shown in the model photos and the fit is somewhat a little tighter in the calf area. Material wise, I got the dark blue and although the material is pretty nice, not too thin or too thick, I’m not really liking the color too much, makes them look kind of cheap and again, doesn’t look like the same color as shown on the model. The description also lists these as mid rise but they are actually high rise and hit me just slightly below my belly button, not necessarily a bad thing if you prefer high rise but if you’re looking for mid rise, keep looking. What I liked, good stretch (no need to size up - if anything size down one, especially if your measurements fall in between the sizes noted in the sizing guide) , nice deep front pockets and they are comfortable and seem pretty durable. Bottom line: 3 stars mainly because the listing description and seller photos are somewhat misleading. These jeans are just okay. I’ll still wear from time to time but for casual outings and outdoor activities only.

I bought these jeans for my kids. They like trendy clothes, they wear jeans with holes in them, and I thought these are a shoe in. The jeans arrived yesterday, and surprisingly my girls weren’t too interested. I think because it’s summer and they’re thinking shorts and mini skirts rather than jeans. Big win for me! I cannot believe how amazing I look in these jeans. Now, I’m not the type of person to compliment myself pretty much ever. I’ll be the first person to point out my wrinkles, jowls, saggy bat wing arms, and lumpy cellulite allll over. But these jeans make me look like a movie star. I don’t know what kind of magic was sewn into the fabric, but they’re comfy with just the right amount of stretch, they stay on all day… So they’re stretchy, but they snap back where they need to, they aren’t low rise, but they don’t go up to my chest. I’m telling you, these are perfect. I think the only people who might not love these jeans as much as I do are taller people. I say that only because I have a 30 inch inseam… a lot of my friends have a 32 or 33 inch inseam and these jeans would be a little bit too short for their liking. Some women prefer the ankle length look, and this would definitely work for that. These jeans are absolutely perfect on me and I’m going to be looking at some of the other colors as soon as I’m done writing this review.

5ft 4, Petite Friendly! I Moonwalked down the hallway! Oh my word it’s a size 4~~!! It’s really difficult to find a size 4 in stores and in general jeans that I’m not walking on because I’m a petite. The hems are right at the top of my feet. I’m 5’4”, Waist 26, Hip 36, and 107lbs. The elephant in the room needs to be addressed—yes the “light blue” is a little darker than in seller’s pictures. These are super soft and stretchy with a feeling that a distant relative of it could have been a Jegging! Lol I usually wear size 5. Size 5 is too big around the waist but it’s the looseness that I like in the legs. Size 4; complete opposite; perfect around the waist so they’re not falling down but tighter than my *personal* preference in the legs. Tighter around the legs is easier for me to deal with than having to hike them up constantly! They are not as heavy as my other jeans and they are not as cold when putting on. I’m very, very happy with these jeans, I skipped and moonwalked down the hallway!!

Cute Style, Flattering Cut, Comfortable Stretch, Sturdy Denim I ordered these jeans in color Blue, size 8. They fit me just the same as most name brand jeans of that size fit me. The waist is high yet well-proportioned in that it doesn't rise above my belly button like some other high-waisted pants I've purchased. I like where the hemline hits my legs. For reference, I'm 5'4". The fabric is substantial in weight and has the feel of a very comfortable, stretchy cotton denim. I've put these jeans through 5 laundry cycles using cold water and one brief drying cycle on medium heat and although they did shrink a bit, it didn't affect the fit much because the fabric is stretchy. I think the cut of these jeans is both stylish and flattering, and the wide pant legs allow lots of freedom of motion in the thigh area. There was one hiccup, however. The jeans arrived carrying a strong odor in the fabric, which to me smells like petroleum. This is the reason I took off one star. I've now put them through 5 wash cycles, one brief dryer cycle and hung them outside in the sunshine. I'd researched that baking soda, vinegar and/or lemon juice might help, so I used those in the last wash cycle and it did help to a degree.

So close to perfect.. I have been searching the this style of pants for quite a while now and these are *almost* perfect. The material is wrinkle resistant and super lightweight - perfect for summer. Not see through! The back of the waistband is elastic so you can wear it with a tight shirt or chunky sweater without issue. I wish I had sized down because they're not as snug as I'd have liked. Nice big pockets. Seam work is consistent but I did find quite a few loose threads that I had to snip, not ideal but I expected as much. The biggest drawback for me is the length. The hem is just small enough so that letting it down wouldn't quite fix it. I would 100% buy more if they had a long/tall option. I'll still be wearing these often because they are so comfy and I love the style. *PLEASE* ADD TALL SIZE OPTIONS!! I ordered a size 4. My measurements are: 5'9" 130lbs 31" inseam 26"-27" waist 36" hip

Nice for summer. As someone who practically lives in jeans, I’m always on the lookout for something lightweight to wear when it’s warm. These cropped, gauzy pants looked like a great option, so I selected the neutral khaki pair. Here’s a list of what I liked and what I didn’t.What I liked:• The pants are a cotton/linen blend which makes a soft, lightweight fabric that keeps you cool.• They are washable on a gentle cycle in cold water. I hung them to dry, and they looked ready to wear afterwards.• The color didn’t fade.• The length was great and ended right above my ankles.What I didn’t like:• According to the manufacturer’s sizing chart and the Amazon recommendation, I selected a size large (my usual size). On me (5’6”, 185 pounds, size 12), these pants seemed to run a little small. The waist was comfortable, but they fit slightly small through the hips. As a result, I didn’t get the casual, flowy look that I was wanting.• As much as I love pockets, I don’t like the ones on these pants. They are positioned on the outermost sides of your hips. These pants probably would have looked and fit better without them.I tried these pants with both a cropped t-shirt and a long t-shirt. Because of the fit through the hips, I didn’t care for the look on me when I tried them with the cropped shirt. I preferred the look of the longer shirt over the pants. It created more of the casual, flowy look that I was hoping for. I wore these pants for a day of errands and was delighted with how comfortable they were. I was secretly hoping that the fabric would stretch out through the hip area, but it didn’t (maybe a tiny bit).Overall, these are a nice, lightweight summer pant. I might consider sizing up if you want them looser through the hip. Additionally, I’d probably remove the drawstring, so you don’t see the bulky bow underneath a longer shirt. I’m giving these pants four stars because of the minor sizing issue and the unattractive pockets.

Great color & comfort ~ Size small = fairly big + high rise Just got a compliment on these from someone in my house & she is glad to take these off my hands as they're pretty massive on me ~ Hip = 45" / Front rise 13.5" / Back Rise 18.5" I am 5'4 and pulling up to my belly button I still have 2' left hanging down in the crotch (and also must use the drawstring even to get them to stay as the elastic is also made for someone a bit larger). So it's not a fix to make them look tighter/more fitted just pulling them up unless you are taller with a wider hip. I range from xs to 4/6 all depends but these to me feel like a medium or like one of those very generous vanity sizing smalls. Also just realized the knees stretch so I get the kangaroo look like I'm bending my knees when I'm standing straight. The front panels of fabric down the legs are for some reason narrower than the back, so essentially you can see both inseams & outward seams from the front & not sure what that's doing to the silouette & wear. The wide hip takes away from the wide leg because it's almost seems like it's tapering, even if it's the same width.. there's just a whole lot more upper fabric + rise for me than I'm personally used to in a pant. Nice saturated teal just as in the ad, very drapey and linen-y to some extent (like linen washed a lot and blended with something else) There is no materials tag in the pant, only a brand tag with size & care tag on reverse of it. If you got a lot of hip, are preg or looking for a not-too restrictive short pant with a wide leg & high rise, give em a try! :)

Pretty nice linen wide leg pants! These lineny pants are made of a heavier material with a linen feel to it. They could possibly be hotter than I would like for summer but are not itchy, like most linen pants are for me. I’m 5’4”, so these are full length pants for me instead of capris, but I expected this. I would estimate the inseam length to be 28-29” in the XXL size. Their measurements of the length of the whole pant isn’t all that useful. They did fit perfectly in my normal size and are actually wide leg. These pants will be nice for spring, cool summer nights, and fall. The navy color is a bit darker than their picture and more of a truer navy color which I like better than the brighter blue in their pictures. They did arrive a bit wrinkled and folded into a small box for some odd reason. I’m hoping they will hang out, because this crinkled material won’t iron well. I can’t say yet about how they wash up but will update review if they shrink too much once it gets warm enough to wear them!

Very stretchy material These are cute trouser style jeans, I love the front pockets, they are so stylish. The material is very stretchy so you might want to size down one. I'm 5'8" and currently around 158lb, I got the 31x31 and they are a little loose fitting, which is ok because the length is just long enough for me because these hang a little lower on my hips. I love that you can choose your length, I often have a problem with pants not being long enough. I just ordered the next size down and figured I'd make it a longer length (30x33) to see if I like the fit better. I wish they had this style in a darker denim and other colors, I would get more. They are very comfortable.

PERFECT! Finally...a pair of jeans that fit perfectly and feel amazing! The Silver Jeans Co. Women's Suki Mid Rise Curvy Fit Trouser Leg Jeans are a high-end high-quality pant. They are well made with "real" denim that is heavy weight and durable. The double button and zipper fly is attractive. The fit is amazing. I don't have many curves at 5'6" and 120 lbs. Most women's size pants fit terribly so I am doomed to wearing leggings 90% of the time or juniors clothing. These jeans are the answer to my pant needs! There is a very slight stretch to the denim...ever so slight...that is a really nice plus. I will likely buy an additional pair in another color. These are fabulous! Bravo!! I am so happy to have found a pair of jeans that fit great, look fantastic, are high quality, durable, and well made.

Disappointing I've been looking for a pair of bright white pull-on, straight leg pants for a while now. I thought I'd found them with these Slim-Sation pants. Pros: nice bright white color, perfect length, perfect fit in the legs. However... the back of the waistband gaps terribly!!! Please see photos taken from back and side. (Ignore the butt, please.) I have to continuously keep pulling them up in the back. I am 5'6", 120 lbs. and purchased size 4. Everything fits well, except for the way the back portion of the waistband is designed. There is a separate section below the waistband that just gaps out very weirdly. Unfortunately, I've worn these pants, so cannot return them. But, I am so disappointed that after paying $67 (with tax) for a pair of pants, this is the sad look that I'm stuck with. Oh well... keep looking. I plan to send this same message to the seller and hopefully I'll get an explanation as to the waistband design. Hope this review helps others.

Great dress pants These basic dress pants look great with either tank top blazer or a blouse. I didn't see a sizing chart in the description, so I ordered my normal size and the fit turned out great. They are snug enough to show my figure but not skin tight that they appear unprofessional. The pockets are very functional but not deep. The fabric is good quality and also comfortable with a little stretch. These pants are long. I am pretty tall (5'9") and they fit me perfectly. If you are shorter, these are still great pants with heels or can be easily tailored to be shorter. I think these are great and recommend them for both business casual or professional wear.

comfy stretchy pants for daily wear & workouts *pic* My teen daughter likes how this feels and fits her. The XL fits her usually-XL pear-shape figure and 5'5 ft height well. Its polyester-spandex fabric has a soft stretchiness to it and hugs her just right on her behind but not in a tight manner. She can stretch with these pants on without worries of coverage or tearing. The leg hems ends right above her ankles just like it does for the model of its Amazon product photos (see pic). The white stripes on the sides sets these pants apart from her multiple solid black pants. She wears these pants for working out as well as for casual daily wear with t-shirts or workout shirts. Didn't shrink when I laundered it in warm washer water and high dryer heat.Really recommended.

Soft, Comfortable These pants are very soft and comfortable but, as others have said, just a bit thinner than expected. However, for the price, I have no complaints. They come just to the bottom of my feet and I’m 5’4”, so if you’re tall they might be a bit too short for you, and if you’re short you may need to hem or fold them. Overall, they are worth the price These are really cute. The straps at the end are unnecessary so we ended up cutting those off. No biggie. The zipper is no big deal but at the top it kinda seems as tho you didn’t zip it up all the way. but other than that these pants are good. They do require a lot of ironing tho. Not like photo I ordered a medium. I am 5’7, 153lbs. They are definitely not as baggy as the picture and what I preferred. Amazing FP Dupe I was worried ordering these pants but they exceeded my expectations! The fabric is not super soft but I love whatever material this is made out of!
